骤然停滞的木船，在海面溅起波涛，借着星光，你得以看清前方的礁石。法吉娜的眷属们倾倒在其间，幽邃的歌声令你意乱神迷。

The abruptly-stopped dinghy splashes waves upon the sea's surface. Under starlight, you catch a glimpse of jagged rocks ahead. Phagousa's kin lie scattered among them, a haunting dirge clouding your mind.
被歌声吸引的海兽们将礁石围住，你的小船也被簇拥的鱼群困住，进退两难。
Sea monsters charmed by the song surround the rocks, while your dinghy is trapped by a school of fish, caught between misfortunes.
在简陋的船身被挤压至崩裂前，你必须做点什么，从沉没的结局中逃离。
Before the fragile boat is crushed under the pressure, you have to do something and escape a watery grave.
你将珍藏的蜜酿倾倒进大海，漂浮在浪花上的琼浆令海兽也陷入沉醉，就连歌吟中的海神眷属，也不由地分心舔舐起嘴唇。
You pour the precious honey brew into the sea, its drifting nectar intoxicating the sea monsters. Even the singing Titankin are momentarily distracted as they lick their lips.
虽然对策已显露成效，但贪婪的海洋可不会就此满足。
The tactic seems to be successful, but the greedy sea is far from sated.
忍耐不住的眷属们竞相跃入海中，争抢蜜酿，因歌声而来的鱼群也彼此归去。你小心翼翼地驶过礁石，在烂醉如泥的眷属中央，大海的宝藏正熠熠生辉。

The Titankins, unable to resist, leap into the sea and scramble for the honey brew, while the fish drawn by song return whence they come. You carefully steer past the rocks — amidst the drunken throng of Titankins, the treasures of the sea sparkle with radiance.
你将法吉娜的传说咏唱，从诞生的瞬间，到欢宴的如今。歌声中，如痴如醉的眷属们为你敞开了道路，并将沉没在海域中的宝藏作为你的报偿。

You sing of Phagousa's legend, from the moment of birth to the revelry of the present. Amid the melody, the intoxicated Titankins open a path for you, presenting treasures sunk to the bottom as your reward.
你试图打造曼妙的歌声，但蹩脚的唱腔却连鱼群都尝试逃离。愤怒的眷属们向你一拥而上，打算终结这撕心裂肺的曲调。
You attempt to craft a graceful melody, but your clumsy singing drives even the fish away. The enraged Titankins surge towards you, intent on silencing this heart-wrenching tune.
你深吸一口气，卯足全力划动船桨，无视掉溅入船里的鱼腥味，让船笔直地撞进暗礁。
You take a deep breath, mustering all your strength in your rowing, ignoring the fishy stench that has splattered onto the boat, steering it straight for the submerged reef.
木船不出意料地被撞得七零八碎，法吉娜的眷属们将你从水里捞起，嫌弃地扔到岸边。
As expected, the dinghy splinters into shreds. Phagousa's kins fish you from the water, tossing you onto the shore with disdain.
你深吸一口气，卯足全力划动船桨，无视掉溅入船里的鱼腥味，让船笔直地撞进暗礁。
You take a deep breath, mustering all your strength in your rowing, ignoring the fishy stench that has splattered onto the boat, steering it straight for the submerged reef.
等你清醒过来时，阳光已落在海面，破碎的船身里，不知何时多出了些许宝藏。作为大海对勇者的赞赏。
When you awaken, the sun is already pouring its light over the ocean. The shattered boat is inexplicably laden with treasures — a tribute from the sea to braves.
你回忆着吟游诗人的曲调，用桨敲打船沿作为伴奏，咏唱古往今来的英雄史诗。被你的歌声吸引，原本躁动的大海也变得平静。

You recall the melodies of wandering bards, slapping the oar against the side of your dinghy to create an accompanying cue as you regale epic tales of heroes from ages past. Drawn by your song, the once-turbulent sea turns tranquil.
虽然对策已显露成效，但贪婪的海洋可不会就此满足。
The tactic seems to be successful, but the greedy sea is far from sated.
忍耐不住的眷属们竞相跃入海中，争抢蜜酿，因歌声而来的鱼群也彼此归去。你小心翼翼地驶过礁石，在烂醉如泥的眷属中央，大海的宝藏正熠熠生辉。

你深吸一口气，卯足全力划动船桨，无视掉溅入船里的鱼腥味，让船笔直地撞进暗礁。
You take a deep breath, mustering all your strength in your rowing, ignoring the fishy stench that has splattered onto the boat, steering it straight for the submerged reef.
等你清醒过来时，阳光已落在海面，破碎的船身里，不知何时多出了些许宝藏。作为大海对勇者的赞赏。
When you awaken, the sun is already pouring its light over the ocean. The shattered boat is inexplicably laden with treasures — a tribute from the sea to braves.
你笨拙的举动惊醒了海的生灵，愤怒的眷属们向你一拥而上，打算终结这场劣质的献祭仪式。
Your clumsiness has stirred up the sea spirits. The furious Titankins are swarming at you, determined to halt this botched sacrificial ritual.
在吕奎亚的监狱里，你的狱友正计划一出「精密」的越狱行动。
In the depths of Loukas' prison, your cellmate is hatching a "precise" jailbreak plan.
作为刺杀僭主的惩罚，等到艾格勒重新睁开眼，这里的所有人都会被扔进斗兽场去喂狮子。
As punishment for assassinating the tyrant, everyone here will be cast into the arena to be fed to the lions the next time Aquila opens their eyes.
「欧洛尼斯在上，我们愿以身外的所有，换取自己的生命。在僭主因愤怒将我们推上行刑台前，请您以奇迹拯救卑微的信徒。」你们将财物都放上托盘，甚至连镶金的假牙也不放过。

"Oronyx above, we offer all we have in exchange for our lives. Before the tyrant pushes us onto the gallows in their wrath, may you grant us a miracle to deliver us, your humbled disciples." You all place your worldly possessions on the tray, even gold teeth.
也许欧洛尼斯还在梦里？又或者向它祈祷的人太多了？要不向其他两位命运泰坦祈祷试试？
Perhaps Oronyx is still dreaming? Or are there too many people praying to them? How about trying the other two Fate Titans?
在永夜之帷下，岁月为你们展现古老的过去，没有监牢也没有暴君的时代。监狱的石壁在眼前瓦解，你们信步向外走去。
Beneath the Veil of Evernight, time shows you the ancient past, an era without prisons or tyrants. The prison's stone walls crumble before your eyes, and you all stride freely into the world beyond.
一道微光淹没了你们的祭品，在监狱的地上被巨大的破洞吞噬，下方的河流汹涌澎湃，但总归比留在这儿等死更好。
A faint sliver of light engulfs your offerings, swallowed by the gaping chasm on the prison floor. A raging river surges below, but leaping in seems the much better option than waiting to die here.
在跨越阻隔的低吟中，万径之门的赐福向你们显现，原本紧闭的监狱洞开，被暴君锁在这里的所有人，都看到了动人的夜色。

Amidst low whispers that bridge the divide, the blessing of the Gate of Infinity reveals itself to you. The once-sealed prison opens itself, and all who were imprisoned here by the tyrant see the mesmerizing night sky.
公正之秤的赐福永远伴随着代价，在狱门洞开的瞬间，巡逻的守卫也发现了你们。
The Scale of Justice's blessing always comes at a cost. The instant the prison gates swing open, the patrolling guards catch sight of you as well.
锐利的阴影将牢狱的石壁撕裂，凄厉的寒风刺骨，公正之秤的赐福也到此为止。
Sharp shadows shred the prison's stone walls, piercing winds chill to the bone — the Scale of Justice's blessing ends here.
这是你第一次离开故乡奥赫玛，轻盈的铠甲让你身心振奋。你扛着工匠淘汰的次品长矛，脑海里反复闪过吟游诗人们浅唱的那些英雄史诗。

This is your first time leaving your homeland of Okhema. The light weight of your armor lifts your spirits. You shoulder a shoddily-crafted spear discarded by its smith, and heroic poems hummed by traveling bards play over and over in your mind.
在海上与法吉娜化身的波浪共饮，涉身进入冥界寻找已死的恋人。总有一天，其中也会有你的位置。
Share a drink with waves that are the manifestation of Phagousa on the sea and venture into the nether realm in search of your dead lover. One day, there will be a spot for you as well.
你听说悬锋城正在举办新的竞技比赛，刻法勒在上，看来你注定要在那里夺得最初的荣誉。
You hear that Castrum Kremnos is holding a new tournament. Kephale above, it appears you are destined to seize your inaugural glory there.
好一位勇士！就连悬锋城的本地观众，都不由得为你喝彩。
What a warrior! Even the local spectators at Castrum Kremnos can't help but cheer you on.
失败主义的英雄就不算英雄？再输不能输钱包不是？
A hero who believes in failing isn't a hero? No matter how much one loses, one shouldn't lose money, right?
你在悬锋城的竞赛中一路过关斩将，只可惜，王翼冠军的出手还是让你止步于此。不过英雄可不会就此气馁，刻法勒在上，你背着友人赠予的盾牌，继续漫游翁法罗斯的旅程。

You advance smoothly through every round of Castrum Kremnos' tournament. Alas, the King's Aegis' intervention stops your progress. But a hero must not give up. Kephale above, you carry the shield given to you by your comrade, pressing forth on your journey across Amphoreus.
在雅努萨波利斯的神殿里，你向祭司们祈求神谕，若想要成为英雄，应当去往哪里？
In Janusopolis' temple, you seek a prophecy from its priests: Where should one go, if one wanted to be a hero?
听闻，一场以生命为赌注的远征就要开始，你登上航船，期待着未知的冒险故事。
Word has it that a perilous expedition with one's life on the line is about to begin. You board a ship, anticipating adventurous stories of the uncharted.
那是一只以人类和牲畜为食的怪物，趁它尚在沉睡，你一跃而起，刺瞎它的眼睛。
That is a monster that feeds on both humans and livestock. As it slumbers, you lunge forward and pierce it in its eye.
你们将呼啸的巨兽引入陷阱，可濒死的它却挣脱束缚，向你们发出最后的反扑。
You all lure the howling behemoth into the trap, but it breaks free of its fetters in its dying moments, launching a final, desperate lunge at the group.
岁月流转，伤痕累累的你已重新回到自己的故乡。你看向窗外，刻法勒的光辉仍庇佑着圣城，而风中流传的诗句，已将你的名字传扬。

As the years pass, you return to your hometown once more, battered and scarred. You look out the window. Kephale's radiance still embraces the holy city, yet poems carried by the wind have already spread your renown far and wide.
你曾是一位英雄，只是英雄也终究迟暮。孩子们总爱拉着你，让你讲述过去的故事，和你一同扮演远征的英雄，打败满是涂鸦的木头怪物。

You were once a hero, but all heroes eventually face their twilight. Children would always tug at you to mesmerize them with your feats of past, pretending to be heroes on an expedition with you, defeating wooden monsters adorned in painted scribbles.
随着你一根木棒挥下，扮演怪物的孩子们便纷纷入戏地倒下，然后发出咯咯的笑声。
With a swing of your wooden stick, the children playing monsters would fall dramatically to the ground, followed by gleeful giggles.
一阵寒意从你的脚尖传来，潮汐的尽头，塞纳托斯的长船即将把你载向来生的旅程。
A chill rises from your toes. At the end of the tide, Thanatos' longship will ferry you on a journey into the next life.
在山羊学派的奇兽培育记录中，你发现了一个统计错误。恒温环境制造的奇兽，在体型扩张中的畸形率比常规值低了3％。

In the breeding records of the Caprists, you discover a statistical error. Creatures created in an environment with controlled temperatures show a deformity rate lower than its standard rates by 3% when their size expands.
但你清楚，作为你们当前的研究基础，一旦公开，此前所有的学术成果都将毁于一旦。
But you are fully aware that this is related to the current foundation of your research. Once this goes public, all existing academic progress will be destroyed.
更别提，错误的撰写人，还是树庭中赫赫有名的学者。你看向眼前正在分裂繁殖的小家伙们，苦恼是否要修正培育方案中的错误。

Not to mention that the one who made the erroneous entry is a prominent scholar of the Grove. You look at the tiny critters multiplying before you, conflicted over whether to correct the error in the breeding scheme.
这数据哪儿有错了？都用几十年了，肯定是你算错了。
What's wrong with this data? It's already been in use for dozens of years. You must be the one who's wrong.
你的结论得到了众多前辈的支持，但学派内的同僚们却与你日渐疏远。
Your conclusion garners the support of many esteemed seniors, but your peers in your school grow more estranged from you.
畸形的奇美拉从石壁上啃下一块，在房间里横冲直撞，因两只奇兽的错误结合，无机的怪物竖起尖刺，向你们发出阵阵嘶吼。
The deformed chimera gnaws off a piece of stone wall, romping about in the room. Because of the erroneous fusion of two chimerae, the inorganic monster sprouts spines, growling menacingly at you all.
已功成名就的你骤然意识到，这正是多年前被你无视的错误数据，在机缘巧合下诱发的结果。如果这件事被捅了出去，想必你很快就会被树庭扫地出门。

Having achieved fame, you suddenly realize that this is the result of a stroke of luck from the erroneous data that you ignored all those years ago. If this matter were to get out, you would surely be unceremoniously cast out of the Grove.
看着眼前的怪物，你决定——
Looking at the monster before you, you decide to...
你举起铁棍，和疯掉的奇美拉大战了三回合，终于收获了一堆废铁。你好像明白了，以前导师让你处理的那些废品是什么……

You raise an iron rod and fight the crazed chimera for three bouts, finally ending up with a pile of scrap metal. You now understand what the scrap metal pieces your mentor had you deal with really are...
你费尽九牛二虎之力，锁住疯掉的奇美拉。在山羊学派的成果展示上，作为野化培育的研究结果。
Grappling with every ounce of your strength, you manage to lock up the crazed chimera. It will be shown as the results of animal reintroduction into the wild at the Caprism school's exhibition.
你的丑闻成为了树庭内的一大谈资，但至少，未来的学者再也不会被这样的错误刁难了。
Your scandal has become a hot topic within the Grove, but at least future scholars won't be obstructed by such blunders again.
你身处某张怪异的棋盘，两个模糊的身影在高处摇曳，依照顺序投掷手中的木棍，将你的位置挪动。你的身子圆滚滚的，就像…一颗轮回棋的棋子。

You find yourself on a strange chessboard. Two hazy figures sway above, throwing out wooden sticks from their hands in order, and moving your position. Your body is round and smooth, just like... a Cyclicality chesspiece.
命运泰坦的信徒以你的行动为依据，模仿人生与轮回的变换。
The Fate Titans' disciples model their actions after yours, emulating the change between life and reincarnation.
你在棋盘上飞快地跳跃，从飞雪连天的北域到黄金战争的中心。你开始猜想，自己究竟扮演的什么角色？与此同时，另一颗棋子却和你同时站到了战场的两端。哦，你们现在是两座城邦的将军，必须马上进入一场紧张刺激的对局！

You leap quickly across the chessboard, from the snow-laden northern domain to the center of the Chrysos War. You begin to question the role you are playing. Meanwhile, another chesspiece stands at the other end of the battlefield. Oh, you're both now generals of two opposing city-states, about to engage in an intense battle!
塔兰顿在上，为了胜利，你愿意付出何等代价？
Talanton above, what is the price you're willing to pay for victory?
你知道这个赌注有点危险，握住棋子的手指都在颤抖，但你的勇敢也将得到泰坦的回应。
You know this bet to be somewhat dangerous — even your hand holding the chesspiece is trembling. But your courage will earn a Titan's response.
在战场上，财富是最无足轻重的东西，你跃跃欲试，将要把敌人挤下棋盘。
On the field, wealth is most insignificant. You are raring for action, ready to knock the enemy off the chessboard.
你献上手中的秘宝，如同命运三相殿的祭司，向泰坦祈求加护。
You offer the treasure in your hands, akin to a priest of the Temple of the Three Fates beseeching the Titans for protection.
两位执棋者共同掷下木棍，一场战役的胜负，只由两枚棋子决定。
The two chess players toss out their wooden sticks at the same time. The fate of the battle will hinge on only these two pieces.
博识尊在上，也许是阮•梅的存在感太过强烈，也许是你根本无法忽视她，本不应出现的天才也来到了商店——当然，只是模拟。
By Nous above, perhaps Ruan Mei's presence is too strong or perhaps you simply cannot ignore her, but the genius that should never have appeared is in the store too — Only as a simulation, of course.
「十二泰坦…有趣的『生命』。」阮•梅笑盈盈地将目光转向你，「不过，在那之前，还是先解开这些和『方程』纠缠在一起的数据，给你增加些助力好了。」

"The Twelve Titans... fascinating 'lifeforms'." Ruan Mei smiles as she turns to face you. "But before we dive into that, let's first untangle these data mixed with the 'Equations' and give you a bit of extra help."
由记忆模拟出的黑塔出现在商店区域，心情看起来相当愉悦。
Herta, a memory simulation, appears in the Store area in surprisingly high spirits.
「虽说你头脑中的我远不如本尊的百万分之一，但是嘛，偶尔能体会下模拟阿哈的感觉，倒也不赖。」黑塔打了个响指，「不用谢我，这座数据库的藏品权限也给你打开了。老样子，拿<color=#dbc291ff>宇宙碎片</color>来换。」

"Though the me in your mind is a far cry from the real deal — no more than a millionth of my true self — but hey, it's not bad experiencing every now and then what the simulated Aha felt," Herta snaps her fingers. "I've also unlocked access to this database's collections for you — Don't mention it. As always, go buy them with <color=#dbc291ff>Cosmic Fragments</color>."
拟造的螺丝咕姆站在商店前，饶有趣味地看着眼前的世界。看来，他没有在意差分宇宙被你和树庭联合复刻这件事。
The simulated Screwllum stands before the shop, observing the world with keen interest. It seems he's not bothered by the fact that you and the Grove have recreated the Divergent Universe altogether.
「即使身处困境，也能让你如此挂念，我感到十分荣幸」，螺丝咕姆亲切地向你问好，「至于树庭的文档解析工作，就由我来为你带来更多『祝福』。」
"Even in difficult times, to know that you think of me is an honor." Screwllum greets you warmly. "As for the Grove's document analysis work, I'll be the one to bring you even more 'Blessings'."
I wanna participate in the special test.
「难得你这么积极，正好来验证下新程序的稳定性。」黑塔信手创造出一团奇异的数据体，「来吧，通过之后，我会给你奖励的。」
"It's rare to see you so eager. This is a perfect opportunity to test the stability of the new program." Herta casually created a strange data entity. "Come. Once you pass, I'll reward you."
让我们回顾一场未曾落幕的战争，亦是世间的第一场战争。
Let us reflect on a war that never ceased, the world's very first war.
当黄金世被灾厄的降临终结，狮子咬断云羊的脖子，强者的匕首刺进弱者的胸膛，无数的尸骸坠入冥河，河水为之截断，死亡也无暇引渡。

When the Era Chrysea came to an end in calamity, the strong devoured the weak. A lion's jaws crushed the throat of a lamb, a dagger struck deep into the helpless, and countless corpses flooded the River of Souls, bringing its flow to a halt. Even death faltered, overwhelmed and unable to ferry the fallen.
哀怨的累累白骨被巨浪挤压，化作坚不可摧的巨像，它呐喊着自冥河中起身，攀爬上地面，誓要向所有不义者降下天谴。

The mournful pile of bones, crushed beneath the relentless waves, was forged into an indestructible colossus. Rising from the River of Souls, it climbed in a roar onto the land, vowing to unleash divine retribution upon all who were unjust.
一切征服者的征服者，尼卡多利至此行走于世，并誓要征服诸神。
Nikador, the conqueror of all conquerors, thus strode across the land with a vow to overthrow the gods themselves.
我听闻它对天空的屠戮。
I have heard tales of their carnage against the sky.
为何千年以来，尼卡多利唯独对晨昏之眼穷追不舍？是因为它的雷枪杀死了僭越天空的狂徒，故而要向天空的暴君复仇？还是因为，浴血的战士希冀挑战翁法罗斯最为强大的权威？

Why has Nikador relentlessly pursued the Eye of Twilight for a thousand years? Are they seeking vengeance against the tyrants of the skies for striking down the rebels who dared to reach their heights with their Lightning Spear? Or does the blood-stained warrior aim to challenge the most supreme authority in Amphoreus?
在悬锋人的歌谣里，不过是天空的百眼巨鸟心生畏惧，藏身在云端，才令尼卡多利不得不追逐太阳，昼夜不息。
The Kremnoans sing that only the fear of the hundred-eyed giant bird, cowering in the clouds, caused Nikador to pursue the Sun endlessly, day and night.
世间仍在传颂，它率领众多的眷属，涉身踏入天际。长矛与雷枪碰撞，敲击出阵阵轰雷，神明的羽毛纷纷坠落，降下一场铁雨。
Legends also tell of the day Nikador led their countless kin into the heavens. Lances clashed against lightning spears with deafening roars, and the feathers of gods rained down like a storm of iron.
前来协助盟友的法吉娜，将雨水都化作蜜酿，让数万的兵士陷入酣醉。愤怒的战争之神点燃了天空的酿池，令火烧的残云遍布黄昏。

Phagousa, arriving to aid their ally, transformed the rain into honey brew, intoxicating tens of thousands of soldiers. The enraged god of war set celestial brew ponds ablaze, covering the twilight with burning clouds.
The war is far from over.
为何天空的边缘略显黯淡？那都是被天谴之矛刺瞎的眼睛。
Why do the edges of the sky seem dim? Those are the eyes blinded by the Lance of Fury.
愚昧的诗人弹奏乐曲，臆断纷争与死亡的盟约。却不知，荣耀和胜利，才是尼卡多利的旋律。纷争的事业未毕，它追猎每一位血亲，直到将它们尽数征服。

Foolish poets play their tunes, conjecturing about the covenant of strife and death. Yet they do not know that glory and victory are the melodies of Nikador. The conflict rages on, as they hunt down each kin until none remains unconquered.
所以它劈开吉奥里亚的堡垒，击落欧洛尼斯的繁星，剑指端坐于王座的刻法勒。
Thus, they cleaved Georios' fortress, cast down the stars of Oronyx, and aimed their blade at Kephale upon the throne.
A war that was meant to end.
伤痕不断地出现在两位泰坦的神体，然后又痊愈，直至千年，胜负也未曾锚定。悬锋之下的尼卡多利终丧失理性，圣城之巅的刻法勒已长眠不醒。

Scars etch themselves again and again into the divine bodies of the two Titans, only to heal and reappear, with no victor after a thousand years. Beneath Kremnos, Nikador has descended into madness, while atop the holy city, Kephale remains in eternal slumber.
而战争的军团，仍怀揣着最后的荣耀，向着远方的奥赫玛进军。
Yet the legion of war, clinging to the last vestiges of its glory, still marches toward the distant Okhema.
让我们回顾一场生生不息的战争，亦是地上的众多战争的开始。
Let us recall a war that continues to thrive, the precursor to many across the land.
尼卡多利锻造出诸多神兵，模仿它的手笔，地上的众人也得以冶炼武器。
Nikador forged many divine soldiers and inspired those on the earth to master the art of weapon forging.
其一为矛，在艾格勒创造刻法勒时，投入大地脊髓的雷枪，经由冥河的淬洗，成为刺穿天空的闪光。

The first of these was a spear, created during the creation of Aquila by Kephale. The lightning spear thrust into the earth's spine became a gleaming streak that pierced the heavens after tempering by the River of Souls.
其二为剑，在见证歌耳戈的勇武后，用神血精炼，被城邦累土拱卫，成为指向全能者的锋刃。
Next came a sword, tempered with divine blood after witnessing the heroics of Gorgo, guarded by the braves of the citadel, it became a blade that pointed defiantly at the omnipotent.
其三为盾，在塞纳托斯的馈赠下，由冥界的泥土抟造，足以抵挡愤怒的雷霆。
The third creation was a shield, gifted by Thanatos and molded from the nether realm's clay, strong enough to withstand furious lightning bolts.
因那卑劣的扎格列斯，将盾牌盗走，熔炼成金币，只为向法吉娜换得一杯绝世的佳酿。
Zagreus, in their vile greed, stole and melted the shield into gold coins, merely to barter for a cup of Phagousa's finest brew.
在众神争斗不息的时代，战争在盾牌上溅射出火花，落下的碎屑成为悬锋城盾的秘密。无论事实如何，那些古老的盾牌已无法再造。

In an age where divine battles raged incessantly, war made sparks upon the shield. Its fragments became the guarded secret of Castrum Kremnos' defenses. Anyway, these ancient shields can no longer be replicated.
但真正的战士从不因丢失武器而变得胆怯，尼卡多利也不会是躲藏在盾后的懦夫。
But true warriors do not falter due to a lost weapon. Nikador has never been a coward who hides behind shields.
它挥舞长矛，眷属便列阵前进。它发出战吼，眷属便舍身前进。海浪的旋律冲不散军阵，滚滚的磐石也阻拦不了刺击。

Nikador brandishes their spear, commanding their kin to align in formation. At their fierce battle cry, the kins charge fearlessly into the fray. Neither the surging waves nor the rolling boulders can slow their relentless advance.
于是，如孩童习得语言，幼兽学会撕咬，人类懂得了如何组建军队，如何制造战车，如何行军布阵，如何杀死仇敌。
Just as children learn language and young beasts learn to bite, humans learn to assemble armies, craft chariots, march in formation, and slay their foes.
模仿神明的战争，人子们创造了自己的战争。他们中的佼佼者，则围绕在尼卡多利的身下，以纷争为它献上血祭。
Imitating the divine battles, the children of humanity crafted wars of their own. The fiercest among them gathered under Nikador, offering their blood in ritual conflict.
神明掷出投枪，划破夜空的光辉会指引悬锋城的子民，沿着光的轨迹，践踏沿途的每一座城邦，直到抵达已被焚毁的废墟。

As the god cast their spear, trails of light across the night sky would guide the people of Kremnos. Following the trail of light, they trample every city-state in their path, until they reach the burnt ruins.
As a revelation from the Titan.
已经没人记得，在黄金世的尾声，世上曾有多少城邦。在礼赞尼卡多利的歌声中，锋刃高悬的移动要塞成为他们最后的噩梦。

At the twilight of the Era Chrysea, no one could recall just how many city-states had once thrived. In the ballads glorifying Nikador, the grim specter of the mobile armed fortresses with a hanging blade became their final nightmare.
纷争的种子已长成参天大树，就连瑟希斯的学者，也懂得征战的知识。从尼卡多利的身姿映入世人眼帘的那一刻起，便再也无法停息。

The seeds of strife had blossomed into a giant tree, with even the scholars of Cerces learning the knowledge of warfare. From the moment Nikador's imposing form was seen by the people, the unrest never ceased.
让我们回顾一场失去荣耀的战争，被悬锋人所不齿的历史。
Let us revisit a war that lost its glory, scorned by Kremnoans.
摈弃众多诋毁和恶评，尼卡多利并非愚昧野蛮的怪物，它看重荣耀，甚至高于胜利本身。
Despite widespread criticism and defamation, Nikador was not the mindless brute many portrayed. They held glory in higher esteem than victory itself.
曾有「赤足的弗雷托」，自沿海的希罗科萨斯一路奔来。他的双脚如同领受了法吉娜的赐福，比海上的浪花还要迅速。
Consider "Barefoot Freto," who dashed from the coastal town of Silo Kossas, his feet as swift as the sea's waves, blessed by Phagousa.
他向尼卡多利叫嚣，要与它比拼脚力。男人的傲慢与狂妄，甚至让泰坦都陷入沉寂。它随即放声大笑，并与其约定，只要弗雷托能比它的军队更早抵达希罗科萨斯，就向他承诺家乡的和平。

He challenged Nikador to a foot race, and his arrogance and pride quieted even the Titan. Nikador laughed heartily and struck a deal: If Freto reached Silo Kossas before their army, they would ensure peace for his homeland.
But no mortal can outpace a god.
纷争之泰坦信步出发，弗雷托却拔出短剑，刺入自己的咽喉。他的灵魂比泰坦更早抵达了故乡，因那里正遭受邻邦的侵袭，冥河已漫至郊野。

The Strife Titan set off nonchalantly, while Freto drew his dagger and ended his life. His soul reached his homeland sooner than the Titan, as neighbors besieged his city and the River of Souls overflowed into the fields.
为人子的胆魄所震撼，泰坦掷出长矛，焚灭了入侵的敌军。至此百年以内，再未有邻邦的僭主胆敢逾越神与人的约契。
Awed by humanity's courage, the Titan launched their spear, vanquishing the invaders. For the next century, no tyrants dared defy the sacred pact between mortal and god.
因此，当国王德索斯暗中派遣信使与喀诺尔停战，却又出尔反尔，率领悬锋城的军团兵临城下，尼卡多利第一次向它的子民降下天谴。

When King Desos secretly brokered a ceasefire with Canor only to betray them, leading his forces from Castrum Kremnos to their gates, Nikador unleashed divine fury on their own people for the first time.
它站立于战场中央，任何胆敢穿过这条轴线的士兵，他的盾牌将变得比磐石还重，他的剑刃将顷刻崩断并锈蚀。
The Titan positioned themself at the heart of the conflict. Any soldier bold enough to cross the line would find his shield impossibly heavy, his blade shattering and corroding instantly.
泰坦在维护战士的尊严。
The Titan preserved the honor of warriors.
泰坦在捍卫战斗的荣耀。
The Titan safeguarded the sanctity of combat.
泰坦的眷属们撕裂德索斯的战车，将侮辱战争的国王绞死在战场。他的同伙皆被带上血祭，成为对后人的警示，以及结束战争的礼仪。
The Titan's kin dismantled Desos' chariot, strangling the king who disgraced warfare, turning his allies into a blood sacrifice — a stark warning and a ritual to end the conflict.
战争需要胜利，但胜利并非战争的目的。曾几何时，正是由尼卡多利维护着这样的秩序，以暴力的威慑，支撑短暂却宝贵的和平。
War necessitates victory, yet victory isn't its sole objective. Nikador once upheld such order through intimidating force, fostering a fragile yet cherished peace.
永夜笼罩尘世，失去理性的神明将所有生命都视作假想敌。曾力战黑潮，捍卫翁法罗斯的征服者，终究也成为了黑潮的帮凶，点燃无意义的战火。

An eternal night shrouds the mortal realm, and the god now devoid of reason sees every life as an adversary. The defender of Amphoreus against the black tide eventually turn traitor and assistant to the black tide, igniting futile wars.
尼卡多利啊，坚韧如你，是否也会料到，自己会沦落至这般荣耀全无的结局？
Nikador, resilient as ever, did you ever anticipate such a disgraceful fall from grace?
可千万要小心愚弄众神之神，自它诞生起，世间便再无安宁。
Caution against The God Who Fooled Gods. Since their emergence, the world has known no peace.
塞纳托斯撕裂了大地，令无数生灵被吞没。尼卡多利带来了纷争，让人子们自相残杀。伟岸的全世之座在大地上徘徊，终究因悲伤流下了血泪。

Thanatos ripped the earth asunder, swallowing countless souls. Nikador sowed discord, prompting the children of humanity to turn on one another. The majestic Throne of Worlds, wandering in sorrow, wept bloody tears.
眼泪在地面溅起，化作一大一小的水滴，诡计的扎格列斯。正如身子与影子，笑容与哭泣，一体双面的翻飞之币。
Tears hit the ground, turning into droplets. Zagreus, the master of deception, the Coin of Whimsy, embodies duality — just like the body and its shadow, a smile with tears.
它曾在神殿里扮作黄金之茧，接受世人的供奉，直到墨涅塔飞入其中，才化身为影子，不急不慢地逃离。愤怒的泰坦，只能降罪于盲目的祭司。
They once pretended to be a Chrysalis of Gold in the temple, worshiped by many, until Mnestia flew into it, and they escaped as a shadow. The furious Titan could only curse the blind priests.
它曾在宴会上偷走法吉娜酿造的器皿，哪怕是长年沉溺在享乐中的泰坦，也因此掀起狂风巨浪。
They stole Phagousa's crafted vessels at a banquet, provoking a storm from a Titan usually lost in pleasure.
它也曾藏起过塔兰顿的天平，致使人间丢失了公平和正义的准绳。用于平衡良善和恶的砝码掉落人间，欺骗和妒忌，偷盗和掠夺就此应运而生。

They also concealed Talanton's scales, disrupting the balance of justice on earth. As the weights balancing good and evil fell to the earth, deceit, envy, theft, and plunder emerged, flourishing in their wake.
当然，它也试过偷走那台巨大的机器，但弱不禁风的影子，显然无法负载此世的「黎明」。
Indeed, they attempted to steal the colossal machine known as "Dawn," but their fragile shadow was incapable of carrying the weight of the world.
它必须承受众神的惩罚。
They had to endure divine punishment.
于是，艾格勒闭上眼睛。全能的刻法勒，将「黎明」带至世界的边缘，一片黑暗里，唯有扎格列斯的影子无限地延伸，指引出它的踪迹。
Therefore, Aquila closed their eyes. The omnipotent Kephale shifted "Dawn" to the world's edge, where in the darkness, only Zagreus's ever-reaching shadow marked the trail.
对罪人的鞭笞持续了十二个昼夜，但扎格列斯的把戏会就此终止吗？天上的贼星已告诉我们答案。
The sinner's punishment lasted twelve days and nights, but would Zagreus' tricks truly cease? The Thief Star in the sky already told us.
它潜藏在一年的终末，机缘月的最后一日。待到所有的泰坦都已出行，才偷偷跟在雅努斯的身后，翻转指引的门径。
It lurks at the year's end, on the last day of the Month of Fortune. Once all Titans have departed, it secretly follows Janus, reversing the guiding paths.
众神在道路中迷失了整整一日，世间失去秩序。日历在末尾多出不存在的、名为幽灵日的一页。虫子吃鸟，大地兽在海面奔跑，陷入疯狂的场景。

The gods lost their way for a day, and the world fell into chaos. The calendar now has an extra, nonexistent "Spirit Day," a day of mad scenes such as insects devouring birds and dromases running madly in the seas.
至于扎格列斯？无可奈何的众神在它身上绑了一颗星星。
As for Zagreus? The gods, in their helplessness, bound a star to them.
每当它从天上遁逃，滑落的红色贼星便会揭露它的轨迹，谕示诡计的灾厄就要降临。
Whenever they escape from the sky, the falling red Thief Star unveils their path, foretelling an imminent calamity of deception.
可千万要小心戏耍人间之神，自它降临以来，世间便充斥纷乱。
Beware The God Who Teased Mortals. Their arrival brought relentless turmoil.
它为世间带来的机运就像是一枚硬币，落在正面就走运，落在反面就只能认命，正如变化莫测的扎格列斯。它乐于见到好运连连的人被妒忌，也喜于目睹陡然的厄运令人家破人亡。

The fortune they bring to the world is like a coin — heads for luck, tails for resignation, reflecting the capricious nature of Zagreus. They delight in seeing the fortunate envied and revel in watching sudden misfortune bring others to ruin.
所以，哪怕是它的信徒，也只会在走投无路时向它祈祷，又在时来运转时忽视它的馈赠。
Hence, even their disciples only turn to them in dire straits, disregarding their boons when luck favors them.
这就是扎格列斯的报应。
No one truly believes in them.
故此，它在戏耍世人时也毫无顾忌。
Thus, they meddle with mortals unreservedly.
它为玛雷拉城降下赐福，令长发化作黄金。可一旦削落，头发便恢复如初。众人蓄着满头黄金行走，直到被压断了脖子，也不愿将它们剪下。

They bestowed blessings on the city of Marela, turning hair to gold, which, once cut, returned to normal. People bore their golden burdens on their heads until their necks snapped, never willing to shear them off.
它创造出一种人人都能获胜的棋局，直到连泼利斯王都压下了家当，才带着棋盘逃之夭夭。
They crafted a board game where all could triumph until even the king of Polis gambled away his realm and fled with the board.
还有坐拥整座矿脉的哈厄耳，它祝福那里的小孩都有一双好运的贼手。于是祭司教唆女儿摸钱包，国王指导儿子学偷盗。

In Haerel's mines, they gifted the children there with unnaturally lucky hands for stealing, inspiring priests to teach their daughters pickpocketing and kings to train their sons in the craft of thievery.
它的谎言足以替代真实。
Their lies could supplant the truth.
它的机运足以腐蚀人心。
Their fortune could corrupt the purest hearts.
它的诡计足以颠覆王国。
或许，它也做过些好事。譬如把一颗星星藏在湖底，诱惑贪婪的僭主溺死。或者让英雄的战车被石子颠簸，躲过弓箭。
Perhaps they once performed benevolent acts, like concealing a star at the bottom of a lake to drown a greedy tyrant, or unsettling a hero's chariot to help him evade an arrow.
但有谁愿意相信比海洋还善变的诡计？命运有雅努萨波利斯，大地有阿卡索斯，就连受人畏惧的塞纳托斯，也有以它为荣的哀地里亚。
But who would place their trust in a schemer more fickle than the ocean? Fate has its Janusopolis, the lands have its Icatus, and even the dreaded Thanatos boasts of Aidonia.
唯独扎格列斯，没有任何城邦愿意接纳。
Yet Zagreus finds no welcome within any city-state.
当然，那奇妙的多洛斯是个例外。他们沉浸在欺诈翻飞之币的壮举中无法自拔，世代都在尝试再现伟大的骗局。
Of course, the remarkable Dolos is an exception. Enthralled by the exploits of the Coin of Whimsy, they have been engrossed for generations in attempting to recreate grand deceptions.
It's akin to an insult to their essence.
Perhaps this, too, is a form of faith.
诗人们愿意将泰坦看作深爱世人的神，即使是尼卡多利，也守护了翁法罗斯千年的岁月。
Poets opt to see the Titans as deities enamored with humanity. Even Nikador has safeguarded Amphoreus for ages.
可扎格列斯，它真的爱着我们吗？倘若有利可图，恐怕，就连翁法罗斯的覆灭，也不会耽误它的交易。
But does Zagreus truly hold any affection for us? If profitable, surely even the downfall of Amphoreus would not hinder their dealings.
可千万要小心流离纷乱之神，即使在它已陨落的如今，诡计也无处不在。
Exercise extreme caution around The God of Displacement and Chaos. Now that even they have fallen, stratagems pervade everywhere.
猜猜看，既然没人把利剑刺进它的火种，扎格列斯为何会消失无踪？还是说，这不过是假死的把戏，好逃出泰坦间的战争和黑潮侵蚀，继续离乱人间？

Why did Zagreus disappear without a trace when no blade was thrust into their Coreflame? Could this be merely a ruse, a fake-out death to avoid the battles amongst the Titans and the encroaching black tide, thus continuing to stir chaos amongst mortals?
它的眷属，满嘴鬼话的贼灵巴特鲁斯，不是还在游荡吗？有谁知道，在陨落前，诡计之泰坦曾托付给它怎样的秘密？
Their kin, the silver-tongued Spirithief Bartholos, still wanders. What secrets did the Trickery Titan confide to it before their fall?
The insane truths of the Titans.
「看看遍布翁法罗斯的贼之手，其实扎格列斯把自己的灵魂藏进了里面。只要集齐所有的手臂，把它们拼接在一起，就能复活千手的扎格列斯！比艾格勒的眼睛还多上十倍！」

"Behold the Thief's Hand strewn across Amphoreus — Zagreus has embedded their soul within them. Gather all the limbs, piece them together, and resurrect Zagreus the Thousand Hands, with ten times the power of Aquila!"
「哎，只要付给我一百个金币，好心的巴特鲁斯就能帮你把它们搬运到一起。当然，你不给，我也能偷走你身上的金币。」
"Give me a hundred gold coins, and the generous Bartholos will help you assemble them in the same place. Of course, if you refuse, I might just have to take the coins off you myself."
「其实扎格列斯老祖只是打了个盹儿，我们所有人都活在它的梦中。」
"In truth, the venerable Zagreus merely slumbers, and we all exist within their dream."
「想要带走诡计之泰坦的宝藏吗？它把它们都留在了冥界。只要放弃生命，就能在塞纳托斯的国度找到比一座山还要沉重的黄金。」
"Looking to claim the Trickery Titan's treasure? It's all stashed in the nether realm. Sacrifice your life, and in the domain of Thanatos you'll find gold heavier than mountains."
「其实，那位来去无踪的半神少女，就是扎格列斯的化身。她不也在多洛斯说过吗？因为扎格列斯从不说谎，所以这一定是真的。」
"That elusive demigod is none other than Zagreus' incarnation. She proclaimed it in Dolos — since Zagreus never lies, it must be true."
贼灵自讨没趣地离开，「扎格列斯老祖的秘密，可都藏在它老人家的传说里。」
Annoyed, the Spirithief departs. "Old Zagreus' secrets are hidden within their legendary tales."
传说，扎格列斯的宝藏早已被搬运一空，那盗贼在抢夺宝藏时误杀了神明。传说，扎格列斯根本就没有宝藏，它拿到多少就花掉多少。

Legend has it that Zagreus' treasure was spirited away long ago, the thief accidentally killing a deity in his greed. Another tale contends that Zagreus never had any treasure, as they spent everything they obtained just as quickly.
传说，陨落的扎格列斯藏在奥赫玛的浴池里，方便它偷走法吉娜信徒的蜜酿。传说，尼卡多利陷入疯狂，正是扎格列斯的恶作剧。

Legend has it that the fallen Zagreus hides in the baths of Okhema, making it easier to steal honey brews from Phagousa's disciples. Another tale claims that Nikador's madness was caused by Zagreus's mischief.
扎格列斯的事一件都不能信。
Nothing about Zagreus can be trusted.
传说，扎格列斯的坏主意多到能填满大海。传说，这个传说正出自扎格列斯自己。
Legend states Zagreus' misdeeds could fill the seas. Legend admits these stories are spun by Zagreus themself.
聆听一则孩提的睡前故事，关于万物不可违背的生死流转。
Listen to a Lullaby For Children to Sleep, a bedtime story of life and death's inescapable cycle.
那是人类已无法归去，无法想象的黄金时代。野兽无需捕食，新叶不会枯萎。上千的城邦屹立在翁法罗斯，没有忧愁的生灵无限制地增长。

It was an epoch beyond comprehension, one we can never return to — The Golden Age. Predators did not need to hunt and foliage remained evergreen. Thousands of city-states dotted Amphoreus, teeming with beings who knew no sorrow.
但大地终究无法承载如此的重量，它的脊骨崩裂，化作沟壑，涌出的血液汇聚成河流，吞没坠落的生命。
Yet the earth could not sustain such a burden. Its backbone shattered into ravines, spewing forth blood that formed rivers, devouring the fallen lives.
一只灰黯的手臂自长河中浮起，塞纳托斯，在它诞生的刹那，黄金世便宣告终结。
A shadowy arm emerged from the river — Thanatos, heralding the Era Chrysea's demise with their birth.
From that moment, life became capable of exhaustion.
From that moment, life became capable of aging.
From that moment, life became capable of death.
在公正之秤的见证下，它与众神签订了誓约。以冥河为界，死亡将平衡翁法罗斯的生命，逝去的灵魂终归于塞纳托斯的国度，登上摆渡者的长船，从今生去往来世。

Under the watchful eye of the Scale of Justice, they swore oaths with the gods. The River of Souls marked the boundary, where death would balance life in Amphoreus. Departed souls would journey to the realm of Thanatos, boarding the ferryman's long boat to transition from this life to the next.
它是欧洛尼斯的好友。当人们因一天的繁忙而疲惫时，永夜之帷便晃动裙摆，令繁星闪烁，呼唤它的到来。
They were a friend of Oronyx's. When people were weary from the day's toil, the Veil of Evernight would sway their skirts, making the stars twinkle and calling for their arrival.
悄然来到尘世的塞纳托斯，用它的小指轻掠世人的眉梢，伴他们安详入睡。
Thanatos quietly descended into the mortal world, gently brushing the brows of the weary with their pinky finger, guiding them into a peaceful slumber.
夜莺的低吟与星星的摇曳，那正是死亡与岁月齐唱的诗篇。
The soft hum of the nightingale and the gentle flicker of the stars form a hymn to death and time.
梦境是塞纳托斯的轻语。
Dreams carry the whispers of Thanatos.
晚风是欧洛尼斯的呼吸。
The evening breeze is the breath of Oronyx.
安享此刻，才能迎接明日。
Only by cherishing this moment can one embrace tomorrow.
它也曾与墨涅塔隔冥河而长谈，众神的战争误伤了瑟希斯的神体，它的伴侣甘愿冒着陨落的风险，来到塞纳托斯面前，祈求挽回理性之泰坦的残魂。

They once conversed across the River of Souls with Mnestia, as Cerces' divine form was gravely harmed in the gods' wars. Braving the risk of demise, Cerces' partner pled with Thanatos to preserve the fading soul of the Reason Titan.
作为交换，这对伴侣的造物和眷属，它们将作为礼物赠予死亡。从此，塞纳托斯的国度里，也有了花朵与蝴蝶。
In exchange, the creations and kin of the couple were offered to Death as a gift. Since then, the realm of Thanatos has flourished with flowers and butterflies.
在瑟希斯沉睡的时日里，花朵凋谢，化为泥土，而它们又在泰坦归来后滋养嫩绿的新叶，恰如灵魂的流转，时节的更迭。
As Cerces slept, flowers wilted and returned to the earth, nourishing tender new leaves upon the Titan's return — reflecting the cycle of souls and the turning of seasons.
From a brief rest to eternal sleep.
所以，请不要厌恶塞纳托斯，它虽沉寂，却也温柔。
Therefore, do not despise Thanatos. They are silent yet gentle.
它守望着世人热烈地度过一生，直到辉煌落幕，才小心翼翼划动长船，叩响你我的门扉。
They watch as people live their lives to the fullest. When the curtain falls, they carefully stir the ferry, softly knocking at the door of our souls.
聆听一则警世的睡前故事，关于傲慢与荒诞的寓言。
Listen to a Lullaby to Warn the World — a fable of arrogance and absurdity.
就从塞纳托斯的船说起吧。世人总妄想挑战死亡，譬如疯癫的弥卡斯王，还有躲藏进石棺，陷入永眠的赫耳斯特拉。但他们终不过成为戏剧家笔下的丑角，真正可笑的，还要数密底斯泰歇的皮里曼斯。

Let us begin with Thanatos' boat. Mortals have always tried to defy death. The mad King Mikas, and Herostratus who hid in a sarcophagus and entered eternal sleep to escape mortality, only became the laughingstock of playwrights. But the most ridiculous of them all was Pylamans of Mythemis.
他掰断冥河河畔的苇草，编织成筏，以渡过死亡的湍流。又把黄金磨成匕首，以抗衡腐蚀与崩解。他跟随死者们的灵魂顺流而下，试图刺杀掌船的塞纳托斯，挑战它的神权。

He broke reeds from the banks of the River of Souls, weaving them into a raft to cross Death's perilous waters. He ground gold into a dagger to defy decay and disintegration. Following the current of the dead, he sought to assassinate Thanatos while on the boat and usurp their divine authority.
瞧，他的肉体依然温暖，匕首仍旧锋利，而目之所及只有一艘长船。
Look, his body remains warm, his dagger still sharp, yet all he finds is a single long boat.
Thanatos is nowhere to be seen.
皮里曼斯跃上长船，自诩死亡之主。可渡船却缓缓移动，将他载向彼岸。
Pylamans leaps onto the boat, calling himself the lord of death. Yet the ferry drifts slowly, carrying him to the other shore.
那么，是谁记录下的这则寓言？或许，灰黯之手也钟情书写奇妙的故事。千年以来，像这般痴傻的狂徒虽寥寥无几，但亵渎死亡的愚人却不在少数。

Then, who wrote this fable? Maybe the Hand of Shadow has a knack for telling strange stories? Over the centuries, there haven't been many fools as crazy as him, but there's no shortage of idiots who've dared to mock death.
多半是诡计作祟，王妃听闻，用冥河的河水作为染料，任何被描摹出肖像的人，都将坠入死亡。
It's probably a trick. The queen, having heard the rumor, drew upon water from the River of Souls, believing that any portrait painted with it would cause the subject's death.
她派遣仆人带来一瓢浑浊的河水，将它偷偷倒进国王的陶碗，希望以此杀死令他魂不守舍的情人。
She sent her servant to retrieve a scoop of the river's muddy waters, secretly pouring it into the king's clay bowl in hopes of killing the lover who captivated him.
But the bowl contained muddy water and nothing more.
仆人并无胆量涉身冥河。
The servant dared not approach the River of Souls.
当国王在宴会上拉开幕布，将精心准备的礼物赠予爱人，她却在画布上看到了自己的笑容。
At a grand banquet, the king unveiled a carefully prepared gift for his lover. The queen was greeted by her own smile painted upon the canvas.
酒杯摔落，王妃也一命呜呼。这告诫世人，勿要谋害他人，也勿要被妒忌蒙蔽眼睛。
The wine glass shattered, and so did the queen's life. It serves as a warning to all: Do not scheme against others, nor let jealousy cloud your vision.
最后，还是讲一个美好的故事吧。
Finally, let us tell a beautiful story.
无名的英雄被构陷后身死，他的宝剑也坠入冥河。当河水退去，剑却仍未腐蚀。塞纳托斯在梦中呼唤英雄的子嗣，他依着梦里的指引，来到宝剑面前。

The nameless hero was framed and killed, his sword falling into the River of Souls. When the river receded, the sword remained uncorroded. Thanatos called the hero's offspring in a dream, who, guided by the vision, arrived at the sword.
多年以后，年轻的英雄手持利剑，率兵攻破了被僭主统治的故乡，让剑尖笔直地刺进王冠的中央。
Years later, the young hero, wielding the sword, led his troops to overthrow the tyrant ruling his homeland, driving the blade straight into the center of the crown.
这告诫世人，死亡从不会包庇罪恶。
This reminds mankind that death never covers up evil.
聆听一则归乡的睡前故事，关于去往来生的旅途。
Listen to a Lullaby to Return Home, about the journey to the afterlife.
冥河的潮汐，令航船腐朽，血肉成灰，禁绝现世的生命。它追随死亡而来，漫上战场与荒原，直到引渡去所有的死者，才退回塞纳托斯的国度。

The tides of the River of Souls corrode ships, turn flesh to ash, and forbid mortal life. They follow death, flooding battlefields and wastelands, receding only after all the deceased are ferried back to the realm of Thanatos.
若想要见到塞纳托斯，这便是我们旅途的开始。
If you wish to meet Thanatos, this is where the journey begins.
Accept the end of this life.
在浅滩的两侧，你能看到许多徘徊的灵魂。它们有的忘了带上登船的硬币，只能在岸边流浪百年。有的犯了不可宽恕的罪孽，在身形溃散后不断呻吟，等待最终的审判。

On either side of the shallows, you can see countless restless souls drift aimlessly. Some, having forgotten to bring the coin needed for the ferryman, are cursed to wander the shores for centuries. Others, burdened by unforgivable sins, groan in torment after their forms dissolve, awaiting the final judgment.
还有些被死亡所摈弃的狂人，他们或是在欺骗泰坦后遭受惩罚，或是在跃入冥河后，得到了肉体的不朽。身获不死的诅咒者，只能在冥河边缘，苦苦哀求神明，直到塞纳托斯在某日生出恻隐之心。

There are also madmen abandoned by death, punished for deceiving the Titan, or cursed with immortality after diving into the river. These undying cursed souls linger on the river's edge, begging the god for mercy, waiting for the rare compassion of Thanatos.
不必在意他们，你会遇见一只蝴蝶，塞纳托斯派出的信使，为你指引冥界的方向。
Ignore them, and you will encounter a butterfly, the messenger of Thanatos, guiding you toward the nether realm.
可要当心扎格列斯和它的眷属，除却塞纳托斯，唯有它们在死者的世界来去自如。
But beware of Zagreus and their kin. Apart from Thanatos, only they can roam freely in the realm of the dead.
它们以偷走死者的财产为乐，在黄金战争最为血腥的年代，被藏在死之国度的金币，甚至堵塞了奔流不息的冥河，令河水泛滥至现世，竞相争抢金币的士兵被冥河吞噬，在史书上留下骇人的灾厄。

They delight in stealing from the dead. During the bloodiest years of the Chrysos War, gold coins hidden in the realm of death even clogged the River of Souls, causing the waters to overflow into the mortal world. Soldiers scrambled for the gold coins, only to be consumed by the flooding river, leaving a grotesque chapter in history.
当你抵达河畔，塞纳托斯已撑着长船等候多时。没人知晓它的相貌，知晓它相貌的人早已前往来世。
When you reach the riverside, Thanatos will have been waiting with the longboat for ages. No one knows their true face, for those who do have already crossed into the afterlife.
They may appear as a bird.
They may show as a dragon.
They may come as a breeze.
它无声地探出灰黯之手，收下酬劳，将你牵引上船。然后，划动船桨。
They silently extend the Hand of Shadow, accept your payment, and pull you aboard before rowing the oars.
到这里，便是连吟游诗人们也语焉不详的世界了。他们有的说，英雄们都在死亡的国度重新聚首，举杯对饮。也有的说，那里只有累累白骨，点缀渺无生机的世界。

From this point on, even bards speak vaguely. Some say the heroes gather in the realm of death to raise their glasses in celebration. While others say it is a lifeless place, adorned only with endless piles of bones.
但无论如何，你终会嗅到花的芬芳，在西风尽头，沐浴灵魂的雨滴，直到作为婴孩睁开双眼，发出啼哭。

One way or another, you will catch the scent of flowers carried on the end of the west wind, feel the soul's rain cascade upon you, and finally, as a newborn, open your eyes and let out your first cry.
宴会已然开始，奏响那曲天与地狂欢之歌，颂唱满溢之杯的诞生。
The banquet has already begun, playing the song of Carnival of Heaven and Earth, celebrating the birth of the Chalice of Plenty.
那是最初的支柱，大地之泰坦的呼吸。一缕因炎热而升起，另一缕则因寒冷而凝聚，在天空成形前，下坠成雨滴。
It is the breath of the first pillar, the Earth Titan. One stream rises with the heat, while another condenses with the cold. Before the sky was formed, it fell as raindrops.
冰冷刺骨的雨水，惊醒了沉睡的大地。它的耳畔充斥着大雨的嘈杂，身体成为酿造的器皿。于是它立下誓言——海洋与大地将永恒对立！

The icy rain startles the sleeping earth awake. Their body becomes a vessel for brewing, and their ears ring with the downpour's roar. They then swear an oath — the land and ocean shall forever oppose each other!
法吉娜被大地所唾弃，只能在天地间漂浮起舞，一刻不停地狂饮，一场徘徊在空中的，永无止境的大雨就此诞生。
Phagousa, spurned by the earth, can only dance endlessly between heaven and land, drinking ceaselessly. Thus, an unending rain hovers over the skies.
The raindrops are their finest brew.
The sound of rain is their laughter.
The rainwater is their divine form.
直到公正之秤的介入，由大地让渡出一半的领域，承载在天地间起舞的海洋，这场大雨才终于迎来休憩。
Only when the Scale of Justice intervenes, asking the land to concede half of their realm to carry the ocean between sky and earth, does the rain finally cease.
所有落到地面的雨露，它们都将渗透、蒸发、流淌，回归法吉娜的神域。
Every drop that lands on earth will seep down, evaporate, and flow back to Phagousa's divine realm.
可无常的法吉娜怎会遵守约定？海浪一刻不停地拍击沙滩，神志不清的海洋呼唤大地参与到它的狂饮，而吉奥里亚永远以沉默回应。
But how can the fickle Phagousa obey an agreement? Waves ceaselessly lash the shores, and the drunken ocean calls to earth to join their revelry, but Georios always answers with silence.
所以大地的风排斥海洋。
Thus, the earth's winds repel the ocean.
所以海洋的风讥讽大地。
Thus, the ocean's winds mock the land.
即便如此，在神明的殿堂里，宴会仍永不停息。
Even so, in the halls of the gods, the banquet never ends.
命运泰坦们喜爱温和的酿造，它们常为法吉娜带来警示与指引。晨昏之眼被灌得烂醉如泥，落下漫天大雨，作为法吉娜对大地的挑衅。
The Fate Titans enjoy gentle brewing. They often bring warnings and guidance to Phagousa. The Eye of Twilight, drunk to stupor, showers the earth with heavy rain, as Phagousa taunts the land.
刻法勒喝得沉稳，它唯一一次在微醺后睡去，还险些被扎格列斯偷走黎明机器。尼卡多利钟情口感锐利的陈酿，它总在豪饮后挥舞长矛，要和血亲们来场生死竞技。

Kephale drinks steadily. The only time they slumbered slightly tipsy, they nearly had their Dawn Device stolen by Zagreus. Nikador loves the sharp taste of the aged brew, and they always brandish their spear after a heavy drink, challenging their kin to deadly games.
墨涅塔探出舌头舔食，醉倒在瑟希斯的怀里。漂在水面的双耳瓶里盛着温暖的蜜酿，随河水流向西风尽头。

Mnestia flicks their tongue to taste, falling drunkenly into Cerces' arms. Floating on the water's surface is a two-handled jar filled with warm honey brew, drifting with the river towards the end of the western wind.
你问扎格列斯？法吉娜厌恶它比吉奥里亚更甚，要想换走绝世的佳酿？除非带来足以让海洋都哈哈大笑的诡计。
You ask about Zagreus? Phagousa loathes them even more than Georios. To trade away the finest brew? Only if they can pull off a trick so clever it makes the ocean burst into laughter.
And Zagreus has plenty of tricks up their sleeve.
Phagousa isn't one to hold grudges for long.
众神相聚一堂的过去已成往昔。再回首，不免叫人以为，这都是诗人们的杜撰而已。
The gatherings of the gods are now distant memories. Looking back, one might dismiss them as nothing more than the fanciful tales of poets.
宴会已至高潮，奏响那曲雨与雪欢腾之歌，颂唱众神的荒诞游戏。
The banquet reaches its climax, with the song of Delight of Rain and Snow echoing, celebrating the gods' absurd games.
再举杯对饮吧，若你能给到法吉娜独一无二的至宝，它也将馈赠你此世难得的蜜酿。
Raise another toast — for if you can offer Phagousa a treasure unlike any other, they will gift you a honey brew rare beyond imagination.
艾格勒收集来一百道寒风，它们被法吉娜封装进瓶子，带着冰晶和世间的喷嚏声，就连晨昏之眼也要因它打起哆嗦。天空陷入酣眠，雪花如迷梦般落下。

Aquila gathers a hundred cold breezes, which Phagousa bottles with frost and the echoes of mortal sneezes. Even the Eye of Twilight shivers at its chill. The sky falls into a deep slumber, and snowflakes drift down like fleeting dreams.
塞纳托斯采撷冥河边缘的花朵，托蝴蝶送至海岸，法吉娜留下花瓣边缘的露珠，将花蕊浸泡，香醇里满载着死亡的气息。

Thanatos plucks flowers from the edge of the River of Souls, sending them via a butterfly to the shores. Phagousa collects the dew from the petals' edges, soaking the stamens, and the fragrance is infused with the scent of death.
传说，扎格列斯曾偷走这瓶蜜酿，试图在宴会上毒死刻法勒，但全知的神明却用计倒换了金杯，令它自食苦果。
Legend has it that Zagreus once stole this honey brew to poison Kephale at a banquet, but the all-knowing gods outwitted them, swapping the golden goblets and forcing Zagreus to reap what they sowed.
The God of Trickery suffers terribly.
A curse will torment their Coreflame.
但在多洛斯的传说中，扎格列斯只是化身为海蛇潜入法吉娜的宫殿，偷喝了将要赠予死亡的礼物。从此，扎格列斯也可在生死间自由来往。

But in the legends of Dolos, Zagreus simply transformed into a sea snake and sneaked into Phagousa's palace, then drank the gift meant for Death. Since then, Zagreus can also freely traverse between life and death.
再随海潮歌唱吧，反正大海不喜沉寂。
Sing with the tides once more, for the sea despises silence.
它曾包庇欧洛尼斯，用连绵的大雨，掩饰逃离天空的繁星。可欧洛尼斯的嬉笑声焕发出光亮，连最深的海沟也无法隐藏。
They once sheltered Oronyx, cloaking with endless rain the stars fleeting from the sky. But Oronyx's laughter shone so brightly that not even the deepest ocean trench could hide their light.
当塔兰顿兴师问罪，海洋也只能乖乖交出藏在怀里的群星。作为惩罚，海洋将永远照映星空，提防贪玩的泰坦再度逃离。

When Talanton comes to exact justice, the ocean has no choice but to reluctantly hand over the stars they harbored. As punishment, the ocean will forever mirror the night sky, guarding against the playful Titan escaping once again.
它也曾孤身去往尼卡多利的身前。是时，蛮神正因法吉娜城邦的羞辱而举兵进攻，但满溢之杯却暗中指引子民，以石榴汁酿造琼浆。

They once went alone to face Nikador when the savage god was launching an attack on Phagousa's city due to humiliation, but the Chalice of Plenty secretly guided their people to brew divine nectar from pomegranate juice.
「若是城邦倾毁，此后的岁月里，又要如何制出此等佳酿？」
"If the city were to fall, how could such finest brew be produced in the years to come?"
诸神的战争尚未平息，有几位神明敢于直面纷争的投枪？但别以为法吉娜有多勇敢，它不过是顺路邀老友喝上一杯，不得已在盛怒的尼卡多利面前想出此等伎俩。

The war of the gods raged on, and few dared to face the spears of Strife. But do not mistake Phagousa for being brave. They merely seized the chance to invite an old friend for a drink, forced to conjure such a ruse before the wrath of an enraged Nikador.
瞧，昏昏沉沉的法吉娜仍在歌唱，浪花是它醉后的笑声，漩涡是它轻盈的舞蹈。
Look, the dazed Phagousa is still singing. The waves are their drunken laughter, and the whirlpools their graceful dance.
宴会终将谢幕，奏响那曲海与风起舞之歌，颂唱人世的诸般享乐。
The banquet will eventually come to an end, its melody echoing the Waltz of Ocean and Winds, celebrating the countless pleasures of mortal life.
在墨涅塔教会信徒们何为艺术之前，是谁掀起浪涛，教会人们在喜悦时起舞，在亢奋时呼号？法吉娜，它把酿造的技艺传播到每一寸土地，难道只是为了有人在宴会上陪它一起欢笑？

Who raised the waves before Mnestia taught the disciples what art is? Who taught people to dance in joy and shout in excitement? Phagousa spread the art of brewing to every inch of the land. Was it merely so that someone would join them in laughter at the banquet?
它藏在醉客的酒杯里，与众人一同参与对自己的祭祀。它吞下远征的航船，邀水手们在肚子里纵情豪饮，又在迷醉后连人带船吐上岸去。

They hide in the drinkers' cups, participating in the ritual made to them along with everyone else. They swallow expeditionary ships, inviting sailors to revel in their belly, and after the humans get drunk, the god spews them and the ships back onto the shore.
哦，法吉娜，哪里有宴会，哪里就有你。
Oh, Phagousa, wherever there's a banquet, there you'll be.
Raise a glass, and partake in the ritual.
向大海倾倒迷人的祭品。
Pour enchanting libations into the sea.
以歌舞赞颂泰坦的威名。
Sing and dance to praise the Titan's renown.
但大海向来无常，它也曾在醉意里掀翻战船，用波浪把游子推向孤岛。
But the sea is ever capricious. In their drunken whims, they have overturned warships and cast wanderers onto desolate islands.
还有那些随意制造的眷属，在半梦半醒时，用珊瑚、礁石、水草和浪涛抟成的海兽。若是被问起，泰坦定早已将它们遗忘。
There are also those kins created in half-dreams, molded from coral, reefs, seaweed, and waves. If asked, the Titan has surely forgotten their existence by now.
它们有的比堡垒还高大，有的长着两颗心脏和九个头颅，牙齿可以咬碎宝剑，尾巴能够刺穿磐石。
Some are taller than fortresses, some have two hearts and nine heads, some have teeth that can shatter swords, and tails that pierce stone.
若不是众多英雄在海上来来往往，谁知道它们会怎样兴风作浪。
If not for the many heroes traveling the seas, who knows how these creatures would wreak havoc?
They lure the monsters into traps.
英雄们也在船上设宴，鞋子在甲板上敲打出节拍，附和着吟游诗人、琴师和随意的哼唱。
The heroes, too, hold banquets on the boat, their feet tapping rhythms on the deck, accompanied by the spontaneous hums of bards and harpists.
于是法吉娜也混入其中，鱼群在船下游曳，海洋的眷属登上木船，带来宝物和待宰割的云羊。蜜酿在宴会中流入海里，引得泰坦和鱼群争抢。

Thus, Phagousa mingles among them. Schools of fish swim beneath the ships and oceanic kins board wooden vessels, bringing treasures and sheep to be slaughtered. Honey brew spills into the sea at feasts, inciting the Titan and fish alike to scramble for it.
海风吹过，英雄们倾倒，向眩晕的海浪抱怨。
Sea breezes blow, and heroes stagger, complaining to the dazed waves.
哎，法吉娜，若不是你，哪有这么多海怪和麻烦？再者说，那些宝物，本就是启程前献祭给你的东西！
Ah, Phagousa, if not for you, where would all these sea monsters and troubles come from? Besides, those treasures were offerings sacrificed to you before our journey!
The heroes lie awake through the night.
人子啊，海洋将庇佑你们的征途，所以——即使黑潮正吞没世界，即使众神已陷入疯狂，也不要遗忘欢宴。
Children of humanity, the sea will bless your voyage. So — even as the black tide swallows the world and the gods descend into madness, do not forget the banquet.
要载歌载舞地踏上英雄的旅程，再载歌载舞地迎来胜利或终焉。
Embark on your hero's journey with song and dance, and meet victory or doom the same way.
你可见过蝴蝶驻足在枝头？倾吐爱与美的恋歌。
Have you ever seen a Butterfly Perched On A Branch, pouring out a love song of beauty and affection?
是时，动物在刻法勒的足迹上生长，植物因瑟希斯的垂枝发芽。可蓦然回首，却没有谁能理解自己的智慧，理性之泰坦的树心因悲伤而生出裂痕。

Yes. Animals grow in the footsteps of Kephale, and plants sprout from the hanging branches of Cerces. Yet, upon sudden reflection, none could comprehend their wisdom. The heart of the Reason Titan cracks with sorrow.
它遍寻翁法罗斯，采撷众多生灵最美丽的鳞羽，创造出黄金的蝴蝶。而世间传述浪漫、美与爱情的诗篇，就此翻开了第一页。
They wander Amphoreus, collecting the most beautiful scales and feathers from many creatures, creating golden butterflies. Thus began the world's poems of romance, beauty, and love.
它的智慧足以同瑟希斯论辩，雕塑的技艺让艾格勒也自惭形秽，歌唱令法吉娜也噤声，墨涅塔，还有什么能比你更动人？
Their wisdom could rival Cerces in debate, their sculpting shamed Aquila, and their singing silenced Phagousa. Mnestia, what could be more moving than you?
But the butterfly loved in vain.
But the sacred tree did not understand those feelings.
漫长的陪伴令蝴蝶心生悸动，向创造者述说爱意，慌乱的瑟希斯躲进圣树，直到蝴蝶泣血而死，也不曾归来。

Long companionship stirs the butterfly's heart, so it confesses its love to its creator, but the flustered Cerces flees within the sacred tree. Even when the butterfly weeps blood and dies, Cerces never returns.
就连看惯了死亡的塞纳托斯，也不由为其垂泪。它收集游离的残魂，投入茧中。金蝶飞散而出，去往世间追求浪漫的足迹，羸弱的人形也从残茧中诞生。

Even Thanatos, long accustomed to death, sheds tears for the butterfly. Gathering its fragmented soul, Death cast it into a cocoon. Golden butterflies emerge to pursue romance in the mortal world, while a fragile humanoid form is born from the remains of the cocoon.
「去寻找大地上的爱意吧，到那时，沉寂的树也将为你开出花朵。」雅努斯为它指引前进的道路。
"Go seek love upon the earth. When the time comes, even the silent tree will bloom for you," Janus guides their path forward.
「去纺织大地上的浪漫吧，到那时，无泪的神也将谱写你的诗歌。」欧洛尼斯交予它黄金的纺锤。
"Go weave romance on the earth. When the time comes, even the tearless god will compose your poetry," Oronyx hands them a golden spindle.
The one in the cocoon departs.
They begin a journey around the world.
它看见云羊带着子嗣在原野上漫步，簇拥的人子在篝火前放歌，正如它与创造者。
They see sheep with offspring wandering the plains, and clusters of humanity singing around the campfire, just as they once did with their creator.
它看见孩童手牵手奔跑，少年相约出海远行，恰似它与教导者。
They see children running hand in hand, and young men vowing to sail far across the seas, just as they once did with their mentor.
它看见鸟儿衔来果实装点巢穴，将军向少女献出花朵，爱恋之情比火种还要灼热。
They see birds carrying fruits to decorate their nests, generals offering flowers to young maidens, their love burning hotter than any Coreflame.
从世界的尽头到尽头，带着大地上所有的爱，漫长的苦旅终于回到原点。
From one end of the world to the other, they gather all the love of the earth, and their long, arduous journey finally brings them back to their origin.
They connect the broken chrysalis with familial love.
They hang the heart with the love of friends.
They weave the golden cocoon with pure love.
圣树为族亲敞开道路，枝桠与友亲血脉交融。墨涅塔将自己的所有献给瑟希斯，终于在黄金之茧中，等来爱的垂语。

The sacred tree opens their path to kin, their branches intertwining with family and friends. Mnestia gives all of themself to Cerces and finally, within the Chrysalis of Gold, receives the whispers of love.
于是，蝴蝶自茧中重生，圣树为恋人开花，自此相伴相依，永不分离。
Thus, the butterfly is reborn from the cocoon, the sacred tree blooms for their lover, and from that moment, they remain inseparable.
你可知晓蝴蝶曾掠过窗前？纺织此世爱的金线。
Do you know the Butterfly Flitted Past Window, weaving the golden thread of love into this world?
王子和邻邦公主的私奔，战场上所向睥睨的夫妻，人类与泰坦的不伦之恋，墨涅塔的鳞粉轻触，赐予众生恋美的眸与求爱的心。

The prince and the neighboring princess eloping, the married couple commanding the battlefield, and the forbidden love between humans and the Titans — all are brought to life by Mnestia's touch of golden wing dust, bestowing upon all beings the eye for beauty and the desire for love.
艾格勒的眸子，凝视着斯廷法洛的工匠，他锻造的机械能够令水往高处流淌，铸造的战车能在阳光下攀登上山岗。
Aquila's gaze falls upon a craftsman in Stymphalia, whose machines make water flow uphill and whose chariots climb sunlit hills.
它化身为一道光影，与工匠夜夜私会，可增长的爱慕，却令工匠多出了几分欲念。倘若能与恋人在白昼相见，向乡邻炫耀她的美丽……

Appearing nightly as a beam of light, Aquila meets the craftsman in secret. Yet, as affection deepens, so does the craftsman's desire — to meet his lover by day and boast of her beauty to his neighbors and friends.
他紧紧地抱住恋人，哪怕对方一再警示，黎明就要到来。当艾格勒睁开它的百目，炙热的火焰将工匠焚尽，连绵的大雨持续了整整一月。
Ignoring her warnings, he holds her tightly as dawn approaches. When Aquila opens their hundred eyes, blazing flames consume him, and a month of unceasing rain follows.
墨涅塔的金蝶收集来残存的爱意，熔铸进工匠的造物，它们在白日追逐晨昏之眼的光辉，在夜幕下凝视爱人的睡脸。

Mnestia's golden butterflies gather the lingering affection, forging it into the craftsman's creations — creations that chase the Eye of Twilight by day and watch over his lover's sleeping face by night.
May they embrace under the sunlight.
May they rest beneath the night sky.
年轻的战士，未死于战场的刀锋，却亡于挚友的妒忌。他背着冰冷的身躯回到故乡，假作哀悼，在少女面前痛哭流涕。
A young warrior is not felled by battlefield blades but by his friend's envy. The murderer carries the cold body home, feigning grief as he weeps before a maiden.
可墨涅塔早已乘着死亡与夜的西风，飞入少女的晚梦，戳穿了男人的诡计。她将匕首刺进仇敌的咽喉，并负罪割去长发，成为战场的先锋。

But Mnestia, riding the west wind of death and night, flies into the maiden's nocturnal dream to expose the man's deceit. She stabs a dagger into her enemy's throat, shaves her hair as a sinner, and becomes a vanguard on the battlefield.
但少女常胜不败，有只虫儿总会飞在她的身边，在出征前亲吻她的盔甲和武器。
Yet, the undefeated maiden is never truly alone. A tiny insect always flutters around her, kissing her armor and weapons before each campaign.
多么漫长的时光，当头发花白，老人在病榻逝去。两只甲虫也穿过战场，飞向爱之神的花园。
As time passes, hair turns white, and the elderly pass peacefully on their beds. Two beetles cross the battlefield, journeying to the garden of the god of love.
墨涅塔，世间爱与浪漫的捍卫者。
Mnestia, the eternal defender of love and romance.
它为逃离婚约的少女披上隐形的轻纱，引领她在海边寻到真正的爱恋。它蒙上傲慢的少年的眼睛，等到所有的爱慕者都因盲眼的他而逃之夭夭，仍有一个吻仍触上他的嘴角。

It drapes an invisible veil over a girl fleeing an arranged marriage, leading her to true love by the sea. It blinds the eyes of a prideful young man, and as all his admirers flee from his sightless gaze, yet one kiss still finds its way to the corner of his lips.
蝴蝶的翅膀掠过天空与大地，哪怕尘世崩塌，人子啊，也不要摒弃比黄金更璀璨的爱情。
The wings of the butterfly sweep across the sky and earth. Even if the mortal world collapses, children of humanity, do not forsake love brighter than gold!
你可听闻蝴蝶已悄然离去？只留下残破的羽翼。
Have you heard that the Butterfly Quietly Departed? Leaving only tattered wings behind.
唉，再谈谈「美」吧，纪念曾屹立不倒的璀璨岁月。你去过帕福斯吗？以美为荣耀的城邦，就连高贵的墨涅塔，也曾在那儿败下阵来。

Ah, let us speak of "beauty" once more, in tribute to the resplendent eras that stood unshaken. Have you ever been to Paphas? A city that glorifies beauty, where even the esteemed Mnestia once met their match.
那少女的美貌非凡，眸子里仿佛藏着星光点点，就连拉冬的王子，都因目不转睛而跌入河里。愤怒的神明化为人形，在选美中夺去众人看见美的眼睛，而少女却掩嘴偷笑。

The beauty of that girl was extraordinary. Her eyes seemed to hold starlight, and even the prince of Ladon fell into the river from staring too hard. The angered god transformed into human form, competed in a beauty contest, and robbed everyone of their sight to see beauty, while the girl just covered her mouth and giggled.
「墨涅塔在上，我本不如您的万分之一，可嫉妒偏偏夺走了您的美丽。」
"Great Mnestia, I am not even a fraction as beautiful as you, yet jealousy has robbed you of your beauty."
羞愧的浪漫之泰坦在逃跑时扇动翅膀，鳞粉落下，让苹果都染上黄金。当然，这不过是诗人们蹩脚的牵强附会，仅当作笑谈，倒还算合理。

The shamed Romance Titan, in their flight, flapped their wings, and the scale powder fell, turning apples to gold. Of course, this is just a clumsy justification by poets, merely a reasonable jest when taken as such.
还是回到埃杜利亚吧，一位学者宣称能用数字和几何诠释真正的美丽，在瑟希斯的神殿，用螺旋与垂直线勾勒出两轮月亮与贼星的变化轨迹。

Let's return to Idilia, where a scholar claims to decode true beauty through numbers and geometry, within the temple of Cerces. Using spirals and vertical lines, he charts the shifting paths of the moon and the Thief Star.
墨涅塔自无法允许如此的傲慢，它将雨滴落在树叶的歌声、花的叹息、星星摇曳的轻响捻作丝线，披上恋人的肩膀。

Mnestia cannot tolerate such arrogance. They weave a melody of raindrops falling on leafy canopies, the sighs of flowers, and the soft flicker of starlight into silken threads that drape over the shoulders of lovers.
Is it a triumph of knowledge?
这可算是个千古谜题。如果塔兰顿的天平左托盘和右托盘一起掉进水里，它会先捡哪一个？什么才是真正的美丽？依我看，哪怕聪明如瑟希斯，也回答不上这些难题。

This poses an age-old riddle. If both pans of Talanton's scale were to fall into the water, which would they retrieve first? What constitutes true beauty? Even Cerces, with all their wisdom, struggles to unravel these enigmas.
但这个问题的反面，墨涅塔却知道答案。
Yet, Mnestia knows the answer to the converse question.
什么是丑陋？纷争与鲜血涂抹的世界，美丽的华装被撕裂，宏伟的雕塑被推倒，悦耳的歌喉被刺穿。
What is ugliness? A world marred by strife and bloodshed, where beautiful garments are ripped apart, grand sculptures toppled, and melodious voices stilled.
墨涅塔在上，在美已衰颓的此世，要怎样才能换回，那双你曾赋予众生的，窥见美的眼睛，与那颗求爱的心灵？
Great Mnestia, how might we reclaim the eyes you bestowed upon all beings to perceive beauty, and the hearts that yearn for love?
The wings of the butterfly are torn.
可高贵的墨涅塔仍尝试振翅，它希冀一种优雅的死因，将灼热的火种传递到谁人的手里。
Yet, the noble Mnestia still tries to flap their wings, seeking an elegant demise, passing the fiery Coreflame to another.
然后，人子啊，披上黄金的残蜕，化茧成蝶吧，替陨落的神明带回消逝的美丽。
Then, children of humanity, don the golden remnants, transform from the cocoon to butterflies, and bring back the faded beauty of the fallen gods.
当命运的果实坠地，是谁一口咬下，从无中创造出世界？
When Fate's fruit fell, who will take the first bite, creating a world from the void?
在比古老更古老的过去，一无所有的混沌第一次诞生出「有」，一个杂乱的线团，命运的丝线包裹住混沌的琼浆，结出果实。原初的火种，开始在果实中燃烧。
In a time older than the oldest, chaos first spawned forth "existence" — a jumbled mess where fate's threads wrap chaos' nectar, bearing fruit. The primal Coreflame ignites within this fruit.
雅努斯在黑暗中睁开眼睛，命运的丝线令它感到拥挤和窒息。它用指尖勾勒出门径，言语描摹出通路，为广阔的虚空腾留出喘息的余地。

Janus opens their eyes in the darkness, cramped and suffocated by the threads of fate. They outline pathways with their fingertips, and articulate passages with their words, carving out breaths in the vast void.
这些原初的道路成为翁法罗斯的边界，时至今日，仍有无数人试图触及。
These primordial paths define the boundaries of Amphoreus, still sought by countless souls today.
Prohibitions and closures come into being.
命运的果实膨胀，高悬在枝头，塔兰顿发出轻柔的呼吸。但漆黑的丝线杂乱无序，纠缠不清，如刀刃割裂泰坦的神体。
The fruit of fate swells, suspended high, as Talanton breathes softly. Yet, the chaotic black threads are entangled and unclear, slicing through the Titan like blades.
它在果实表皮刻下最初的裁决，宣告因果，令丝线舒展成框架，支撑世界。有寒冷便有温暖，有获得便有失去，如此，万事万物方能在平衡中诞生。

They carve the initial judgment into the fruit's rind, declaring cause and effect, allowing threads to stretch into frameworks that support the world. Where there is cold, there is warmth. Where there is gain, there is loss. Thus, all things can emerge in balance.
这些框架成为翁法罗斯变化的源动力，所以轻盈的会上浮，沉重的会坠落。
These frameworks become the driving force behind Amphoreus' transformations, causing the light to rise and the heavy to fall.
可世界依然静止，事物本应如律法约定的那般变化，但「永恒的下一刻」却永远不会到来，直到第三位泰坦自朦胧中苏醒。
Yet the world stands still, as things should morph according to the laws, but the "next moment in eternity" never comes until the third Titan stirs from obscurity.
年幼的欧洛尼斯，以第一声啼哭唤醒了时间。命运的果实在成熟后绽裂，黏稠的混沌逸散，涌向空无一物的世界。
Young Oronyx, with their first cry, awakens time. The fruit of fate bursts upon ripening, its viscous chaos spilling out, flooding the empty cosmos.
恰如新生的孩童，在啼哭过后，世界才真正拥有生命。
Like a newborn, the world only truly lives after they cry.
The past and the future are inscribed.
以雅努斯为首，三位命运泰坦在混沌中划定时空，赋予法则，抟造出翁法罗斯的雏形，留下万物诞生的基石，是为创世之始。
Led by Janus, the three Fate Titans delineate time and space within chaos, bestow laws, and mold the embryo of Amphoreus, laying down the cornerstone for all creation.
世人皆知，命运乃不可违抗之理。可在命运诞生之前，世界要怎样运行？原初的动力从何而来？混沌的世界到底是何模样？
It is universally acknowledged that fate is inevitable. But how did the world function before fate was born? What was the original force? What did a chaotic world look like?
纵使诸神的智慧超越凡世，如此诸般谜题，又有谁能解明？
Even with the gods' wisdom surpassing that of the mundane, who can unravel such profound puzzles?
当命运驻足不前，是谁行于尽头和彼岸，为众神开辟通途？
When Fate's Steps Stalled, who walks to the ends and beyond, forging pathways for the deities?
万径之门，它在大地上雕刻出道路，用于行走的盘桓在山间和平原，用于流淌的凹陷成为河流和山谷。
The Gate of Infinity carves paths upon the earth, creating walkways through mountains and plains and sculpting riverbeds and valleys for streams.
它为艾格勒的视线引导方向，在天空中勾勒出车辙，容晨昏之眼的造物滚动。太阳自吉奥里亚的脊背之巅升起，又落向法吉娜那醉意尚浓的海洋。

They guide the sight of Aquila, drawing trails and roads in the sky, enabling the creations of the Eye of Twilight to traverse. The sun rises from the pinnacle of Georios and sets towards the deep, intoxicating seas of Phagousa.
当刻法勒的火种被点燃，担负起创造生命的重任，雅努斯轻点命运的丝线，它们在刻法勒的眼前化作两条道路。若是走上第一条，它将创造出力量与智慧的结晶，比艾格勒冶炼的奇兽更富逻辑。

When the Coreflame of Kephale is lit, taking on the heavy responsibility of creating life, Janus gently nudges the threads of fate, which split into two paths before Kephale. If the first path is chosen, they will make beings of strength and intellect, forming a creation more logically complex than the mythical beasts forged by Aquila.
若是走上第二条路，它将创造快乐与自由的化身，如海浪无拘无束，似云朵变化无穷。
If the second path is chosen, they manifest as the incarnations of joy and freedom, as boundless as the waves and as ever-changing as the clouds.
可全世之座并不满意这样的道路，它祈求命运将绳索解开，散乱的丝线垂落，有的随风缠绕住山岭，有的飘进了云层，有的沿着河流延伸进海洋。

Yet, the Throne of Worlds is not satisfied with these paths. They plead for fate to untangle the ropes, letting the threads fall and entwine around mountains, drift into clouds, or flow along rivers into the sea.
雅努斯为负世泰坦的选择降下赐福，延伸出无限的道路。
Janus bestows blessings upon the choices of the Worldbearing Titan, stretching forth endless roads.
每条丝线都是一种选择。
每条丝线都是一条道路。
生命自此有无限的可能。
From here on, life possesses limitless possibilities.
可如此的选择，自然也带来代价。无限生长的生命，终于招致死亡的垂怜。
However, such choices naturally come with costs. Infinite growth of life ultimately invites the compassion of death.
于是，雅努斯将丝线赠予驾驭长船的塞纳托斯，沿着冥河，缠绕在苇草和花朵的茎秆上，最终在西风尽头缠绕成绳结。
Thus, Janus entrusts the threads to Thanatos, who steers the longship along the River of Souls, winding them around reeds and blooms, finally tying them into knots at the ends of the west wind.
死亡将生命的绳结交予雅努斯，重新与现实连接。而灵魂便沿着丝线，一无所有地抵达来生。
Death hands the life knot back to Janus, reconnecting it with reality. Souls then travel these threads, arriving empty-handed in the afterlife.
它也曾剪断道路，把它们缠绕成团，替众神监禁扎格列斯。纵使是诡计之泰坦，也无法从门径中逃离。
They have cut off paths, entangling them into knots to confine Zagreus for the gods. Even the Trickery Titan cannot escape these maze-like doors.
它若是跳跃，便坠落向天空，若是奔跑，便陷落进泥沼。直到全能的刻法勒心生怜悯，又或是窥见了未来的隐秘，才在十二个昼夜后放出那该死的扎格列斯。

If they jump, they fall toward the sky. If they run, they sink into the marsh. Only when the omnipotent Kephale shows mercy, or glimpses a secret of the future, do they release the cursed Zagreus after twelve days and nights.
在人子们的足迹布满翁法罗斯前，已有那伟大的神明开辟前路，因它曾到来，所以我们才能触及。
Before the footprints of humanity marked Amphoreus, this great deity had already paved the way. Their prior arrival enables us now to reach them.
当命运揭露指引，是谁在悄然追随，寻找神明的足迹？
When Fate Revealed Guidance, who quietly follows, seeking the gods' footprints?
你还想去到世界的尽头吗？在黑潮尚未降临的黄金时代，在无尽之海中寻到雅努斯的门扉，穿过它们，你将抵达翁法罗斯的每一个角落。而只有最虔诚的信徒，方能在惊涛骇浪中将它们开启。

Do you still desire to reach the world's end? In the Golden Age before the black tide, find Janus' gates amidst the Endless Sea, pass through them, and you can arrive at every nook of Amphoreus. Only the most devout disciples can open them amidst turbulent seas.
无畏的阿科托斯，传说中最后一位完成朝圣的勇士。雅努斯为他的战船降下恩赐，只要不僭越艾格勒的领域，翁法罗斯的天空将任他自由穿行。

Fearless Akotos is the last warrior to complete his pilgrimage, according to legend. Janus graces his warship, granting him free passage through Amphoreus's skies as long as he does not venture into Aquila's realm.
唯有众神与天空祭司们的城邦，方有资格与他俯瞰同一片大地。
Only the gods and the cities of the sky priests are worthy to share his view of the world.
可惜，你已无法窥见那战船的影迹，它在黄金战争中被撕裂，成为夜空中的流星。
Regrettably, you can no longer see that warship. It was ripped apart in the Chrysos War, becoming a meteor in the night sky.
万径之门，你是最亲近人子的命运。你呼唤人们来到自己的立足之地，建立连接一切的雅努萨波利斯，所有旅程都将经过的世界中心。
Gate of Infinity, you stand closest to humanity's fate. You call people to your own foothold, to build Janusopolis that connects all, the heart of the world through which all journeys must pass.
要走向善的道路，而非恶的；要打开勇敢的门，而非懦弱。人们沿着道路向前，在谕示之下，打造万世的伟业。

Choose the path of virtue over vice, and open the doors of courage rather than cowardice. As people venture forward on these paths, guided by divine mandates, they craft legacies that will endure through the ages.
雅努斯的祭司们低吟浅唱，呼唤神明的赐福。沿海百邦的渔获带着腥味跨越门扉，北域的野兽被剥下皮毛，和冰雪一起被带上出海的航船。

The priests of Janus hum softly, invoking the blessings of the god. Fish from the seaside states, carrying the scent of the sea, pass through the gates. Northern beasts, stripped of their fur, are loaded onto ships with ice and snow.
雅努斯，世人为你献上鲜花与羊奶，以「繁荣」赞颂你的功绩。
Janus, the people offer you flowers and milk, extolling your deeds with "prosperity."
Yet, eras of abundance can wither.
当天空开始黯淡，又是谁为生命留下指引？
When the skies start to dim, who else provides guidance for life?
你看见永不停歇的信使。他们穿过镜中的道路，赞颂着泰坦的名讳，以鸟儿般轻盈的步伐，传递生与死的讯息。
You witness the tireless messengers. They navigate the mirror-like paths, extolling the Titans by name. Their steps are as light as birds, conveying messages of life and death.
更有视死如归的眷属，为即将陨落的城邦解开命运的绳索，使道路崩解，迷雾四起，就连灾厄也在其中迷失方向。

The devoted kin who decided to give up their lives severed the ties of fate from the city-states about to fall, causing paths to crumble. As the mist ascends, even calamity finds itself adrift and directionless.
Yet, it is better than nothing.
The black tide and calamity are unstoppable.
无人还记得，雅努斯的最后一次垂迹。土地破碎，海洋被吞噬分割，残存的大地上，只留下笼中困兽般的人子。
No one recalls the last time Janus descended. The land shattered, the seas engulfed and divided. Only the remnants of the earth remain, trapping humanity like caged beasts.
可无数门扉仍向你我敞开，为世间带来最后的指引。
Yet countless gates remain open to us, offering the last guidance to the world.
「瞧一瞧看一看咧，小店商品全是从黄金世遗迹里翻出的尖货，假一赔十咧。」一位蒙面商人在大街上卖力地推销着。

"Step right up! Feast your eyes on the finest relics, all excavated straight from the ruins of the Era Chrysea! If it's fake, I'll pay you back tenfold!" A masked merchant calls out, their voice cutting through the marketplace bustle.
你翻了翻货架，发现还真有不少宝贝，但是又看了眼标价，不禁捂紧了钱包，随即开始和商人讨价还价。

You sift through the shelves and, sure enough, spot some real treasures. But after sneaking a glance at the prices, you instinctively tighten your grip on your wallet — looks like it's time for some haggling.
「扎格列斯在上，我这可是冒着生命危险从遗迹里偷…哦不，找到的，得把卖命的钱也算在里面啊。」
"By Zagreus above! I risked life and limb to recover... Oh, I mean, acquire these relics! You can't expect me to leave out the danger fee!"
你犹犹豫豫，不知道该如何抉择。
You hesitate, unsure of what to do.
「这宝贝现在是你的了，交易愉快！」
"And just like that, it's yours! A pleasure doing business with you!"
「呀，真不巧了，宝贝又卖掉一件。您要还有看上的，可得抓紧出手啊。」商人拼命怂恿你。
"Ah, too bad! Another prized treasure just left the shelves. If you've got your eyes on something, don't wait too long!" The merchant prods.
「您算是赶上时候了，促销活动火热进行中，再不出手下次可就不一定有这个机会了。」商人拼命怂恿你。
"Lucky you! We're running a very special promotion right now — hesitate, and you might never see a deal like this again!" The merchant prods.
「呀，真不巧了，宝贝又卖掉一件。您要还有看上的，可得抓紧出手啊。」商人拼命怂恿你。
"Ah, too bad! Another prized treasure just left the shelves. If you've got your eyes on something, don't wait too long!" The merchant prods.
「您算是赶上时候了，促销活动火热进行中，再不出手下次可就不一定有这个机会了。」商人拼命怂恿你。
"Lucky you! We're running a very special promotion right now — hesitate, and you might never see a deal like this again!" The merchant prods.
「呀，真不巧了，宝贝又卖掉一件。您要还有看上的，可得抓紧出手啊。」商人拼命怂恿你。
"Ah, too bad! Another prized treasure just left the shelves. If you've got your eyes on something, don't wait too long!" The merchant prods.
「您算是赶上时候了，促销活动火热进行中，再不出手下次可就不一定有这个机会了。」商人拼命怂恿你。
"Lucky you! We're running a very special promotion right now — hesitate, and you might never see a deal like this again!" The merchant prods.
你的头罩被粗暴地扯下，映入眼帘的是恢宏的环形竞技场、躁动的观众、以及一地的尸体，你立即意识到自己危险的处境，但好消息是你还活着。

Your hood is yanked off, and in an instant, your senses are assaulted — a grand circular arena, a frenzied crowd, and corpses strewn across the bloodstained sand. Your instincts scream danger, but at least there's one silver lining — you're still breathing.
这里是悬锋城的竞技场，悬锋子民正在为他们那残暴的主庆祝一场胜利。而你，便是这场狂欢中注定要被牺牲的战利品。

This is the arena of Castrum Kremnos, where the citizens of Castrum Kremnos exalt in slaughter, celebrating yet another triumph for their bloodthirsty king. And you? You are nothing more than the loot, a doomed offering in this twisted carnival of death.
青铜门闸缓缓打开，面生十目的巨狮一跃而出，它嗅到了你伤口散发出的血腥味，那比人小臂粗的牙齿磨得铮铮作响。

The bronze gate rumbles open, revealing the beast, a towering lion with ten burning eyes. It catches the bloody scent of your wounds. Its enormous fangs, thick as a human forearm, grinding together with an ominous metallic screech.
「小猫咪乖乖，再闹我就把你门牙打掉！」巨狮眨了眨十只眼睛，一脸不可置信，但是很快它变得更加暴怒，张开血盆大口扑向你。

"Listen up, kitty — keep acting up, and I'll knock your front teeth out!" The giant lion freezes, its ten eyes blinking in sheer disbelief. Then, its shock twists into unrelenting fury. With a deafening roar, it lunges straight for you, jaws unhinging.
「这样的小猫咪，我还能打十个！」你不知道哪来的勇气蹦出这句话，巨狮似乎听懂了你的嘲讽，十只眼睛气地冒出血丝。

"A little kitty like this? Ha! I could take on ten more!" You have no idea what possessed you to blurt that out, but the giant lion seems to have understood the taunt — its ten eyes blaze with bloodshot fury.
你竭尽所能摆出各种挑衅姿势，骂出各种羞辱话语，你自己都不知道这些姿势和话语是从哪学来的。但效果是显而易见的，巨狮已经彻底疯狂。

You unleash a barrage of insults, each one sharper than the last, your body moving instinctively into taunting stances you don't even remember learning. Whatever possessed you, it's working — the beast has snapped.
强者死于话多，你沉默地摆出进攻姿势，一场血战在即。
The talkative ones die first. You drop the theatrics, shift into a battle stance, and steel yourself for the bloodbath ahead.
刚才的战斗你已经勉为其难，你只想快点结束战斗。
The last fight had already drained you. You just want this over with.
警用飞车穿越如织的无人机运输带，稳稳停在三千层摩天大楼的屋顶上，距离报案已经过去了整整7分钟，身为「高危生物处理专员」的你才匆匆赶到案发现场。

The police aircar weaves through the tangled lanes of drone traffic, touching down smoothly atop a three-thousand-story skyscraper. Seven minutes have passed since the emergency call, and as a High-Risk Biohazard Disposal Specialist, you've finally made it to the crime scene.
「是变形种，我们不少兄弟都被骗了，你得小心。」驻场警官把详细情报通过脑机绳传给了你，突然你感到脑子一阵剧痛。

"It's a shapeshifter. It's already tricked a few of our officers — stay alert." The on-site officer sends a data packet straight to your neural link. The next thing you know, a stabbing pain rips through your skull.
「啊哈哈哈哈，又上当一个！」警官化成一摊软泥怪，然后又对着你变成一根巨大的中指。
"Ahahaha! Got another one!" The officer's body liquefies into a writhing mass of slime — then reshapes itself into a giant, mocking middle finger.
你邪魅一笑，殊不知刚才你也通过脑机绳向它反向输送了一份病毒，你的智商在它之上！
You flash a knowing smirk — what your slimy opponent doesn't realize is that, just moments ago, you hijacked the neural link and slipped a virus into its system. Outwitted and outclassed.
出师不利，还是走为上策，下次再收它也不迟。
Not the best start to this mission... but a tactical retreat is better than a reckless demise. You can deal with it another day.
你被一名身穿长袍的修士拦下，她手持一顶颇具分量的黄铜天平，想必是塔兰顿的信徒。
A robed friar stops you. She carries a weighty brass scale — likely a follower of Talanton.
然而你并没有公平正义想要让她伸张，正欲赶她走时，她却将天平凑到你的眼前。
You're in no mood for justice or balance, ready to wave her off, but she raises the scale toward you before you can speak.
「我行于八方，向世人宣告塔兰顿平衡的力量。你大可向我许愿，放置于这天平一侧，若将来愿望成真，另一侧也会生出额外奖赏，你便能一同拿去。」

"I walk in the world, spreading the word of Talanton's equilibrium. You may entrust a wish to this scale — place it upon one side, and if your wish is fulfilled, then the other side will bear a matching reward. Both shall be yours."
看来如今世道确实不佳，连塔兰顿的信徒都玩起了江湖把戏，但话又说回来，试一试好像也不亏。
If even Talanton's followers are resorting to mystical sales pitches, times must be rough. Then again... no harm in trying, right?
「我能感受到财富已经汇聚在天平的一侧，待天平归正之时，我会再次找到你。」修士微微一笑，转身离开。
"I can already sense wealth gathering at one end of the scale. When the balance is restored, I will return to you." With a faint smile, the friar turns and walks away.
「我能感受到权力已经汇聚在天平的一侧，待天平归正之时，我会再次找到你。」修士微微一笑，转身离开。
"I can already sense power gathering at one end of the scale. When the balance is restored, I will return to you." With a faint smile, the friar turns and walks away.
「我能感受到智慧已经汇聚在天平的一侧，待天平归正之时，我会再次找到你。」修士微微一笑，转身离开。
"I can already sense wisdom gathering at one end of the scale. When the balance is restored, I will return to you." With a faint smile, the friar turns and walks away.
「你放弃了一个绝好的机会，但或许你的天平本来就是平衡的。」修士不禁赞赏。
"You've passed up a golden opportunity, but perhaps your scale was already in perfect balance." The friar gives you a knowing nod of approval.
灶筒涌出的热气将羊皮气囊迅速膨胀，涂了糖油的螺旋桨发出咯吱咯吱的愉快声响，你最后整理了一遍包裹着飞行器的棉花糖，摇摇晃晃地起飞了。

A burst of heat from the stove pipe inflates the sheepskin airbag in an instant. The sugar-glazed propellers creak and groan in delight. After one last check of the cotton candy-wrapped aircraft, you lift off, wobbling into the sky.
你当然听说过天舟的惨剧，但你笃定过于明目张胆才是失败的原因。你睿智地选择了飞行器小型化的技术路线，甚至开创性地用棉花糖把自己伪装成一团云彩。

The tragedy of the skyship is no secret, but you're convinced its downfall came from being too conspicuous. With that in mind, you took the smarter route — miniaturization. And for an extra touch of brilliance, you used cotton candy to disguise yourself as a drifting puff of clouds.
山谷和河流尽收眼底，耳边吹拂的是高空独有的凉爽微风，但被艾格勒注视的风险也在上升，你需要做出抉择。

From up here, valleys and rivers unfold beneath you, a breathtaking panorama. You feel the refreshing chill of a breeze unique to high altitudes. But with every inch you climb, so does the risk of catching Aquila's attention. A choice must be made.
你已经比翁法罗斯<unbreak>99.99%</unbreak>的人站得高了，还是下去吧，地上比较踏实。
You're already standing above <unbreak>99.99%</unbreak> of Amphoreus. Perhaps it's time to descend. The ground is far more dependable.
你已经见过了常人不曾见过的风景，至于后面更美的景色，留给不要命的人看吧。
You've witnessed sights that few ever will. As for the even grander views beyond? Those are for the reckless to pursue.
你已经想好了，回到地面上要重新开家棉花糖店，这辈子再也不碰飞行器了。
Your mind is set. Once your feet touch solid ground again, you're reopening a cotton candy shop. And you're swearing off flying for good.
你卖掉布利萨的祖传棉花糖店，还欠了一屁股债打造出终极飞行机器，不是让你在这么低的高度听麻雀唱歌的。
You sold off your family-run cotton candy shop at Bulsa and drowned yourself in debt to build the ultimate flying machine — not to hover at this pathetic altitude listening to sparrows chirp.
气流变得越来越不稳定，飞行器像落叶一样飘来飘去，连棉花糖也被撕扯掉不少，你知道这是艾格勒在搜索你。
The winds grow more erratic, tossing your machine like a drifting leaf. Strips of cotton candy are ripped away by the turbulence. You know what this means — Aquila is searching for you.
但是艾格勒小看了你，这架飞行器的引擎同时也是一台棉花糖机，你迅速生产出大量棉花糖填补了空缺的伪装，甚至还有余量填自己的肚子。

But Aquila has underestimated your ingenuity. The machine's engine is also a cotton candy machine. You immediately crank out many fresh layers of cotton candy, reinforcing your disguise. You even make enough to sneak a snack.
不冒险那还能叫冒险家么，那还不如老老实实在地上给小孩炸棉花糖。
What kind of adventurer backs down when faced with danger? If you were the type to play it safe, you'd still be on the ground, spinning cotton candy for children.
闪电撕裂天空，空气散发出焦灼的气味，这是艾格勒在震怒。他那一百只眼睛都没能发现你，于是便降下漫天神雷企图将你击碎。

A bolt of lightning splits the heavens, the air thick with the sharp tang of scorch — Aquila is furious. Their hundred eyes have failed them, and in a fit of rage, they unleash a relentless storm of divine thunder, determined to obliterate you.
飞行器全身上下都是可燃物，只需一个火星子，这片伪装的云彩就会化成真正的火烧云。
Your machine is a flying tinderbox. One stray spark, and your cloud-like disguise will ignite into a fiery inferno.
也许是冒险家的进取精神支撑着你，又或许单纯是因为不愿回到地上烤棉花糖，你义无反顾地冲向了头顶的雷网。

Maybe it was the relentless drive of an adventurer pushing you forward — or maybe you just couldn't stomach the idea of returning to the ground to spin cotton candy. Either way, you throw caution to the wind and surge straight into the heart of the thunderstorm above you.
雷暴过后，空气里都是甜丝丝的气味，地上的孩子们惊奇的发现，天上飘下了棉花糖雨……
When the storm finally subsides, the air carries a lingering sweetness. Down below, children stare in wide-eyed wonder as soft tufts of cotton candy drift from the sky like rain...
神悟树庭向整个翁法罗斯发出了实习学者考试，这对于苦心钻研学术二十年的你来说，是一个绝不能错过的机会。
The Grove of Epiphany has issued its Intern Scholar Examination — an open call across all of Amphoreus. After twenty long years of academic toil, you know this is a chance you cannot let slip away.
你知道竞争者多如牛毛，而实习学者的名额才区区三人，你赶紧按捺自己躁动的内心，冷静下来思考递交哪项科研成果。

The competition is staggering, with countless hopefuls vying for just three precious spots. You force yourself to calm the excitement buzzing in your veins — focus. The real question is: which of your research projects should you submit?
由于你博学多才兴趣广泛，科研成果也是五花八门，但是成果的学术价值只是一方面，能不能投评委的所好也是关键。
Your scholarly pursuits span a dizzying array of fields, but in academia, raw merit isn't everything — Submitting something the panel likes is just as crucial.
山羊学派的评委应该会对这个成果感兴趣，但是标题似乎不太行，奇美拉有「产后」这个说法么？
The Caprism reviewer might find this particular outcome intriguing... but the title? A bit questionable. Do chimeras even have postnatal physiology?
一周后，你收到了神悟树庭发来的悲报「您的学术成果非常具有科研价值！但遗憾的是与主流研究方向不符，您的简历已进入人才库，期待未来与您合作。」

A week later, the Grove of Epiphany delivers its verdict — a polite rejection. "Your research holds immense academic significance! However, it does not align with our current primary research direction. Your credentials have been added to our talent database, and we look forward to potential collaboration in the future."
「也罢，神悟树庭如此不识人才，不加入也罢。」说着说着你的眼角有泪划过……
"So be it. If the Grove of Epiphany can't recognize true genius, then I want no part of them." You say the words with defiance... but as you do, a lone tear betrays you, sliding down your cheek.
洗浴确实是很重要的事，但把它当成科研成果未免有些小题大做，也许爱泡澡的评委会喜欢？
Bathing is undoubtedly important, but presenting it as a groundbreaking scientific discovery? That might be a stretch. Then again, maybe there's a bath-loving reviewer on the panel?
一周后，你收到了神悟树庭发来的悲报「您的学术成果非常具有科研价值！但遗憾的是与主流研究方向不符，您的简历已进入人才库，期待未来与您合作。」

However, it does not align with our current primary research direction. Your credentials have been added to our talent database, and we look forward to potential collaboration in the future."
