"So be it. If the Grove of Epiphany can't recognize true genius, then I want no part of them." You say the words with defiance... but as you do, a lone tear betrays you, sliding down your cheek.
山羊学派的评委应该会对这个成果感兴趣，但是标题似乎不太行，奇美拉有「产后」这个说法么？

一周后，你收到了神悟树庭发来的喜报「恭喜您通过学术成果筛选，我们诚邀您前往神悟树庭进行面试。」

A week later, the unthinkable happens — an acceptance letter from the Grove of Epiphany. "Congratulations! Your research has successfully passed the selection process. We are pleased to invite you for an interview at the Grove of Epiphany."
洗浴确实是很重要的事，但把它当成科研成果未免有些小题大做，也许爱泡澡的评委会喜欢？
Bathing is undoubtedly important, but presenting it as a groundbreaking scientific discovery? That might be a stretch. Then again, maybe there's a bath-loving reviewer on the panel?
一周后，你收到了神悟树庭发来的喜报「恭喜您通过学术成果筛选，我们诚邀您前往神悟树庭进行面试。」

A week later, the unthinkable happens — an acceptance letter from the Grove of Epiphany. "Congratulations! Your research has successfully passed the selection process. We are pleased to invite you for an interview at the Grove of Epiphany."
大胆！金血也是可以拿出来研究的吗？不过…这个课题好像还挺有意思的。
Outrageous! Researching golden blood — is that even ethical? But... you have to admit, the subject is rather fascinating.
一周后，你收到了神悟树庭发来的喜报「恭喜您通过学术成果筛选，我们诚邀您前往神悟树庭进行面试。」

A week later, the unthinkable happens — an acceptance letter from the Grove of Epiphany. "Congratulations! Your research has successfully passed the selection process. We are pleased to invite you for an interview at the Grove of Epiphany."
据说能进入神悟树庭面试的学者万里挑一，但是当你看到等候厅挤得像大甩卖期间的云石市集时，你不禁感叹如今的翁法罗斯真是人才济济。

They say only the rarest of scholars reach the interview stage, yet as you step into the waiting hall — crammed tighter than Marmoreal Market during a festival sale, you can't help but marvel at the sheer number of bright minds in Amphoreus.
目前还没有人通过面试，那些从隔音面试间出来的学者，要么嚎啕大哭得像个孩子，要么愤怒地撕碎自己的论文，看来评委极其擅长给人上压力。

So far, not a single person has passed. Those emerging from the soundproofed interview rooms are either weeping uncontrollably like lost children or ripping their papers to shreds in pure rage. No doubt about it — the judges are experts in breaking wills.
「第<unbreak>108</unbreak>号面试者请进。」叫号声打断了你的思绪，你擦了擦额头的冷汗，小心翼翼地推开了面试间沉重的木门。

"Applicant <unbreak>108</unbreak>, please enter." The voice shatters your train of thought. You wipe the sweat from your brow, steady your breath, and carefully push open the heavy wooden door to the interview room.
你决定给面试官一个令人印象深刻的开场，于是你把演示模型砸向了面试官光秃秃的脑门。
Determined to leave a lasting first impression, you toss your demonstration model straight at the interviewer's shiny bald head.
面试官的问题像连珠炮一样砸向你，你刚答第一个字，他的眉头就皱得能夹死一只苍蝇。

The interviewer fires a relentless barrage of questions, each one hitting like a precision strike. You barely manage to utter the first syllable of your answer before his brow furrows so deeply it could crush a fly.
你越答越没自信，面对面试官的冷嘲热讽，你恨不得找个地缝钻进去。
Your confidence crumbles with each passing second. Under the interviewer's withering sarcasm and razor-sharp critiques, you can feel yourself shrinking, desperately wishing for an escape.
果不其然，你连落选的通知都没收到，看来面试官甚至都忘记有你这样一名面试者存在。
As expected, you don't even receive a rejection letter. It's as if the reviewer even forgot that you never existed.
面试官的每一个问题都精准地命中了你的舒适区，只见你口若悬河滔滔不绝，讲到激动处甚至直接站起来在黑板上挥毫书写公式。

Every question lands squarely in your wheelhouse. With unshakable confidence, you answer fluently, words flowing like a torrential river. At the height of your enthusiasm, you even leap to your feet, passionately scrawling equations across the blackboard.
你的才华彻底征服了面试官，他直言不讳地说你是这么多年来最优秀的面试者。
Your sheer intellect and mastery leave the interviewer speechless. With rare, unfiltered admiration, he declares you the most outstanding candidate he's seen in years.
你当场就获得了实习学者的名额，并且他表示，以你的才华很快就会在树庭里升到正式学者。
Right there on the spot, you're granted the title of Intern Scholar. And beyond that, he assures you that your rise to full scholar status is only a matter of time in the Grove.
「你就拿这个考验树庭的面试官？哪个面试官经不起这样的考验？」面试官把你人赶了出去，礼物留了下来。

"This is what you call a challenge for the Grove's reviewers? Ha! Any half-decent reviewer could handle this!" With a dismissive scoff, the reviewer throws you out the door, but keeps the gift you brought.
三天后，你收到了树庭的信件「恭喜您通过面试！您现在已经是神悟树庭的实习学者了，请携邀请函前往树庭报道。」

Three days later, a letter arrives from the Grove of Epiphany: "Congratulations! You have successfully passed the interview. You are now an Intern Scholar of the Grove of Epiphany. Please bring your invitation and report to the Grove."
看来人情世故在哪个世界都是必备技能。
It seems that, no matter the world, understanding the art of human interaction is crucial.
经过羊肠般蜿蜒的经纬小径，穿越正在进行哲学辩论的仙女木林，出现在你面前的是所有翁法罗斯学者的圣地——神悟树庭的友爱之馆。

You follow the Woven Trail, its winding paths twisting in intricate, almost deliberate patterns. Passing through the White Dryad Woods, where philosophical debates took place, you finally arrive at the most sacred place in all of Amphorean academia — the Grove of Epiphany's Library of Philia.
这里汇聚了翁法罗斯绝大部分图书，你就像酒鬼找到了法吉娜的酒酿秘藏，恨不得在这座图书馆里用知识醉生梦死。

Here lies the vast majority of Amphoreus's books. To you, it's as if a hopeless drinker has stumbled upon Phagousa's legendary wine cellar — the sheer temptation to lose yourself in this sea of wisdom is overwhelming.
身为树庭学者的你，即使还没有去掉「实习」两个字，也拥有资格阅读这里的任何一本书，那先从哪本开始呢？
As a scholar of the Grove, even if the title still bears the "intern" prefix, you have the privilege to read any book in this library. So, where do you start?
你如饥似渴地摄取书本里的每一个字，就像干旱过后的小麦在痛饮从天而降的甘霖。

You consume the text with a hunger so ravenous, it's as if your mind itself has been starved. Each word, is like rainfall upon a drought-stricken field, quenching your insatiable thirst for knowledge.
有了这座强大知识库的助力，再凭借你那聪明过人的头脑，假以时日成为「七贤人」之一也未尝不可能。
With this formidable library at your disposal, and your own brilliant intellect to guide you, who's to say that one day you won't rise to the ranks of the Seven Sages?
大地兽吞完最后一块红土，满足地打了一个饱嗝，这也意味着今天的工作要开始了。身为哈图西里远近闻名的大地兽饲养员，你要为这些大家伙分配任务。

The dromas lets out a deep, contented burp after swallowing the last chunk of redsoil — a clear signal that the day's work is about to begin. As the most renowned dromas handler in Hattusili, it falls upon you to assign tasks to these towering beasts.
今年又是个丰年，收割完的农作物堆满田垛，急需运往市场。但远洋贸易的船队也到了港口，一捆捆木材和石料在召唤你。

This year has been a season of abundance. The fields overflow with freshly harvested crops, stacked high and ready for transport. But just as the last sheaf is gathered, the distant call of the harbor reaches your ears — the overseas trade fleet has arrived, bringing timber and stone in need of urgent delivery.
是去农田运输粮食还是去港口运输材料，你陷入了沉思。
To transport crops from the farmland or to transport materials from the port? You find yourself lost in thought.
农作物容易腐烂，先去农田比较好。
Crops spoil quickly. It makes sense to head for the farmland first.
大地兽发出一声低鸣，载着你一摇一晃地出发了。
With a low rumble, the dromas sways as it carries you off.
材料堆积在港口需要支付额外费用，先去港口比较好。
Storage fees at the port can be costly. Perhaps the materials should come first.
大地兽发出一声低鸣，载着你一摇一晃地出发了。
With a low rumble, the dromas sways as it carries you off.
唉，好难抉择啊，只能再苦一苦大地兽了，白天去农田，晚上去港口。
Decisions, decisions... In the end, you settle on the hardest path of all — both. The dromas will haul crops during the day and transport materials at night.
你的心肠简直比石头还要硬，那就别怪吉奥里亚惩罚你喽。
Your heart is harder than stone. Then don't be surprised when Georios delivers your punishment.
在你面前有两扇门，一扇由青铜铸造，庄严古朴，另一扇由黄金雕琢，熠熠生辉。自从你误入迷宫深处，已经与这两扇门对峙了整整一天。

Before you stand two doors — one ancient and solemn, cast in bronze. The other radiant and opulent, sculpted from gold. You've been staring at them, unmoving, for an entire day — ever since you wandered too deep into this twisting labyrinth.
身为云游四海的冒险家，你当然见过那些雅努斯祭祀利用「门径」穿越天堑，但是门一旦打开，则再也无回头的可能。

As an adventurer who has roamed the farthest reaches of the world, you've seen the Janus priests use their mystical "passages" to bypass insurmountable barriers. But once a gate is opened, there's no going back.
所幸你不用面对「选A还是选B」的经典困境，你还有第三个选择，靠自己的双腿走出去。
Luckily, you aren't limited to a simple A or B choice. There's always a third option — walking out on your own.
「这一定是雅努斯的阴谋！」你嘟囔着消失在迷宫的黑暗中。
"This has to be one of Janus' tricks!" you mutter before disappearing into the labyrinth's shadows.
你握紧门把手，卯足了劲推开门，这门的重量却如羽毛一样轻，你摔了进去。
You grip the handle tightly, plant your feet, and shove with all your strength. But the moment the door moves, it swings open effortlessly, sending you tumbling inside.
眼前的两扇门融化了，青铜和黄金像岩浆般淹没了你，等你好不容易挣扎出来，背包已经干瘪，里面的财宝化为乌有。

Before your eyes, the two doors liquefy, and the bronze and gold engulfs you like molten lava. You fight to escape, but by the time you claw your way free, your backpack is completely deflated — all your treasures, gone, swallowed by the tide.
你握紧门把手，卯足了劲推开门，这门的重量却如羽毛一样轻，你摔了进去。
You grip the handle tightly, plant your feet, and shove with all your strength. But the moment the door moves, it swings open effortlessly, sending you tumbling inside.
短暂的失明后，你重新看见了刚才的两扇门，唯一不同的是，你的背包里多了几样财宝，看来打开的方式对了，但又没完全对。

After a brief moment of blindness, your vision clears, and before you stand the same two doors as before. Only this time, your backpack feels heavier. A few new treasures rest inside. It seems you were on the right track, just not quite right enough.
你握紧门把手，卯足了劲推开门，这门的重量却如羽毛一样轻，你摔了进去。
You grip the handle tightly, plant your feet, and shove with all your strength. But the moment the door moves, it swings open effortlessly, sending you tumbling inside.
眼前的两扇门融化了，青铜和黄金像岩浆般淹没了你，等你好不容易挣扎出来，背包已经干瘪，里面的财宝化为乌有。

Before your eyes, the two doors liquefy, and the bronze and gold engulfs you like molten lava. You fight to escape, but by the time you claw your way free, your backpack is completely deflated — all your treasures, gone, swallowed by the tide.
欧洛尼斯高歌一段关于黄金的预言，这预言为初生的世界镀上繁荣。
Oronyx sang a prophecy about gold, a prophecy that gilded the newborn world with prosperity.
在那田园牧歌般的黄金世，欧洛尼斯是最被人类忽视的神明，它的真言几乎得不到倾听，它的祭坛前没有贡品和信徒，有的只是累累灰尘。
In that pastoral Era Chrysea, Oronyx was the most overlooked of the gods. Their words were barely heard, and their altar was devoid of offerings and followers, only accumulating dust.
因为人类所有的需求都能得到满足，没有饥饿寒冷和痛苦，未来的每一天都会如今天一般幸福，又有谁会需要预言？
Because all of humanity's needs were met, there was no hunger, cold, or pain, and every day in the future would be as happy as today. Who would need prophecies then?
在那个时代，人类唯一需要依赖欧洛尼斯的，或许就是在黑夜下欣赏繁星。而欧洛尼斯对此并不生气，依旧孤独地预言着。
In that era, the only thing humanity needed from Oronyx might have been to gaze at the stars under the night sky. And Oronyx was not angry about this, still lonely, continuing to prophesize.
当时的世界没有任何变数。
At that time, the world had no changes.
人类的好日子不会持续太久。
Humanity's good days wouldn't last long.
欧洛尼斯的价值终会体现。
Oronyx's value would finally reveal itself.
纷争踏着战鼓声滚滚袭来，紧随其后的是种种诡计。所有的确定性都被打破，今天还活着的人，明天就可能死去，不管你是贱民还是国王。

Conflicts, accompanied by the sound of war drums, surged forward, followed closely by various schemes. All certainties were shattered. Those alive today could be dead tomorrow, regardless of whether you are a commoner or a king.
欧洛尼斯的神殿前跪满了寻求启示的信徒，一座座诏示祭坛拔地而起，数量甚至一度超过了公共浴池。

Before the temple of Oronyx, the ground was filled with worshipers seeking guidance, and one after another, prophetic altars rose up, their number even surpassing that of the public baths at one point.
在至暗时刻，人类的脆弱性暴露无遗，而欧洛尼斯并没有抛弃这些见风使舵的子民，反而耐心的点拨他们。
In the darkest moments, the vulnerability of humanity was laid bare, but Oronyx did not abandon these opportunistic people. Instead, Oronyx patiently guided them.
它能让将领掌握叛军的进攻路线，也能让医生知晓瘟疫的爆发时间。人们在预言的庇护下重新燃起斗志，推动生活艰难地运转下去。

They allowed generals to grasp the routes of rebel attacks, and they enabled doctors to know the outbreak time of plagues. Under the protection of prophecy, people reignited their fighting spirit and struggled to keep life moving forward.
这预言的颗粒度还挺细。
The granularity of this prophecy is quite detailed.
人类不能有点主观能动性吗？
有人利用「永夜天帷」的旗号招摇撞骗，其中最知名者便是扎格列斯。它曾假扮成欧洛尼斯，宣告伊卡利亚即将爆发大洪水，害得满城百姓连夜迁移。

Some deceive others under the banner of the Evernight Veil, the most famous member being Zagreus. They once disguised themselves as Oronyx, declaring that a great flood was about to engulf Icaria, causing the entire city to flee overnight.
类似的把戏愚弄了一次又一次，但大多数人还是心甘情愿地受骗，足见当时的人们对欧洛尼斯多么虔诚。
Similar tricks were played time and again, but most people still willingly fell for them, showing just how devout they were toward Oronyx back then.
直到欧洛尼斯实在无法忍受，它便只将预言告诉那些资深祭司，因为只有他们才能分辨自己的真身。
That is, until Oronyx could no longer bear it and only shared the prophecies with the senior priests, for only they could discern Oronyx's true form.
扎格列斯凡事喜欢插一脚。
Zagreus likes to interfere in everything.
从那以后，大多数人再也无法与欧洛尼斯直接沟通，祭祀们垄断了与神交流的权利，但好在他们足够恪尽职守，年复一年的履行欧洛尼斯施加的义务。

From that point on, most people could no longer communicate directly with Oronyx. The priests monopolized the right to converse with the divine, but fortunately, they remained dutiful enough to carry out Oronyx's imposed obligations year after year.
可是，若所有的道路都需要神明才能照亮，人类又能走多远呢？若有那么一天，它抛弃了我们，我们又该何去何从？
But if every path requires a god's light to illuminate it, how far can humanity truly advance? Should the day come when they abandon us... where would we turn then?
欧洛尼斯宣告一段关于白金的预言，这预言为动荡的世界带来转机。
Oronyx proclaimed a prophecy concerning platinum, this revelation brought a turning point to the turbulent world.
其一为庞恩培拉的毁灭，那是屹立于大陆西侧的强盛城邦，建筑皆由坚固的花岗岩堆砌而成，却在顷刻间坠入深渊。

The first was the destruction of Pompeia, a prosperous city that stood on the western side of the continent. The buildings were built from sturdy granite stones, but they fell into the abyss in an instant.
据说是城里的子民玷污土地，此举惹怒了吉奥里亚，这位和蔼的泰坦罕见地爆发盛怒，它撕开自己的胸膛，吞噬了整座城与人。
It's said the people of the city defiled the land, provoking the wrath of Georios. The gentle Titan, in a rare fury, tore open their own chest and swallowed the entire city, every soul within.
由于地震的破坏性过于巨大，以至于庞恩培拉没有留下任何文物，全靠几位欧洛尼斯的信徒相信了预言并提前逃难，后世的人们才能知晓灾难的细节。

The earthquake's devastation was so absolute that Pompeia left no relics behind. Only a few devout followers of Oronyx, who heeded the prophecy and fled beforehand, allowed later generations to learn the details of the catastrophe.
吉奥里亚也是有脾气的。
不过也有人对这次预言抱有怀疑，毕竟获得神启的只是区区几名信徒，也不排除造假的可能，但接下来的这段预言确凿无疑，整个翁法罗斯无人不知。

Yet some doubt this prophecy. After all, only a handful of believers received the divine revelation, leaving room for deception. But the next prophecy was undeniable, known to all in Amphoreus without exception.
当时悬锋城准备吞并邻国小城，欧洛尼斯降下神谕，宣布了出征的失败。悬锋将军也会死于清泉关的隘口，死因是落马坠崖。

At that time, Castrum Kremnos prepared to annex its neighboring smaller city. Oronyx delivered a divine decree, foretelling the campaign's failure, and declaring the general of Kremnos would meet his end at Clearspring Gorge's narrow pass, doomed to fall from his steed to the cliffs below.
小城上下士气大振，最终利用狭隘地形以少胜多，挫败了悬锋城的进攻。而悬锋城的将军，也如预言中的那般牺牲。

The small city's morale soared, and they ultimately used the narrow terrain to achieve a victory against the overwhelming number of invaders, thwarting Kremnos's invasion. And just as the prophecy foretold, Kremnos's general met his destined end.
这场战争让那些曾经质疑欧洛尼斯的人也不得不信服，因为没有人能阻挡尼卡多利征服的脚步，而即使是这位战神，也没能违背欧洛尼斯的预言。
This war forced even those who once doubted Oronyx to believe, for no one could halt Nikador's conquests, yet not even this God of War could defy Oronyx's prophecy.
好想让欧洛尼斯帮我算一卦。
How I wish Oronyx would divine a fortune for me.
这预言的数值也太高了。
欧洛尼斯的所有预言中，仅仅只有一个还未验证，那便是关于翁法罗斯未来的走向。
Of all Oronyx's prophecies, only one remains unfulfilled, and that is the foretelling of Amphoreus' ultimate fate.
可惜预言的具体内容被祭祀们死死封锁，关于这条预言诞生了很多阴谋论。有人说黄金裔会带来永远的安宁，也有人说黑潮最终会吞没一切。

Yet the prophecy's exact contents remain fiercely guarded by the priests, spawning countless conspiracy theories. Some claim the Chrysos Heir will bring eternal peace, while others insist the black tide will ultimately devour all.
虽然祭祀们对这些猜测一一否定，并一再表示欧洛尼斯并未给出具体的定论，但还是有越来越多的流言蜚语随风而散。
Though the priests deny every rumor, insisting Oronyx never delivered a definitive verdict, whispers continue to multiply, carried by the wind like scattering leaves.
Conspiracy theories are always the most popular.
我也很好奇预言的内容。
I'm curious about the prophecy, too.
不管祭祀们有没有刻意隐瞒，他们的做法或许是正确的。对于凡人来说，过早地知道世界的真相并非好事。
Whether the priests deliberately conceal it or not, their approach may be wise. For mortals, knowing the world's truth too soon is never a blessing.
毕竟就算明天便是世界末日，我们也没办法改变任何事情，只不过是徒增烦恼。所以，活在今天才是最好的答案。
After all, even if tomorrow brought the world's end, we could change nothing, it would only multiply our despair. So living for today remains the wisest answer.
欧洛尼斯低吟一段关于铅铁的预言，这预言为堕落的世界平添迷惘。
Oronyx murmured a prophecy of lead and steel, this revelation deepened the confusion of a fallen world.
现在的欧洛尼斯变成了一位聋哑人，既听不到人们的祈求，也说不出任何神启。无论祭祀们如何祷告，它都三缄其口，空旷的神殿里只有人类自己的回音。

Now that Oronyx has turned wordless and deafened, hearing no prayers, speaking no revelations. No matter how fervently the priests plead, they keep silent. Only the echoes of human voices remain in the hollow temple halls.
欧洛尼斯的神隐早有端倪。最开始，预言的内容由具体变得越来越模糊，面对这些不知所云的文字，有时候连老祭祀都束手无策。
The signs of Oronyx's withdrawal were there from the beginning. At first, their prophecies grew increasingly vague, reduced to cryptic fragments that even the seasoned priests struggled to decipher.
再后来，预言的颁布间隔越来越长，甚至苦苦等待数年，也只能换来它的只言片语。
Later, the intervals between prophecies stretched longer. Years of desperate waiting might yield only a handful of broken phrases.
关于欧洛尼斯的沉默，祭祀和学者争论不休，一种说法是欧洛尼斯在过去颁布了太多预言，透支了自己的神力，等它休养生息完毕，便能再次开口。

Priests and scholars argue endlessly regarding Oronyx's silence. One hypothesis claims Oronyx exhausted their divine power by delivering too many prophecies in the past, and that once restored, their voice will return.
另一种说法是祭祀们故意阻断了欧洛尼斯和凡人的交流，目的是让大权垄断在自己手里。
Another conjecture is that the priests deliberately severed the communication between Oronyx and mortals, with the intention of consolidating power in their own hands.
无论假设再多再荒诞，欧洛尼斯都以沉默应对。如此说来，凡人们还真是可悲，没有了泰坦的指引，便无法统一意见。
No matter how many absurd hypotheses arose, Oronyx responded with silence. In this way, mortals were truly pitiful. Without the guidance of the Titans, they could not unify their opinions.
人只是习惯了依赖泰坦。
Humans are simply accustomed to relying on the Titans.
欧洛尼斯倒是吱个声啊。
Make a sound, any sound, Oronyx.
当最后一位和欧洛尼斯直接沟通过的老祭祀去世，那些伟大的预言变成了遥远的传说，在一代代翁法罗斯人中口口相传。
When the last priest who communicated directly with Oronyx passed away, those great prophecies became distant legends, passed down from generation to generation in Amphoreus.
后世的人们只能从残存的神迹中，一窥这位泰坦的力量，并从如同梦呓的声音中，获得一丁点可怜的启示。
Future generations could only glimpse the power of this Titan from the remaining divine miracles and obtain a small, pitiful revelation from dreamlike whispers.
不要去管启示的真假，只要能让无助的人在黑夜中安然入眠，便足够有帮助了。
Don't worry about the truth of the revelation. As long as they allow the helpless to sleep peacefully in the night, it is enough to be of help.
主要起到心理安慰作用吧。
It mainly serves as psychological comfort.
It's always good to have help.
抬头看看那深邃的夜吧，黯淡的群星无法给人启示，回应我们的只有沉默的黑，仿佛世界上已经没有了这位泰坦的存在。
Look up at the deep night, the dim stars offer no guidance, and all that responds to us is the silent darkness, as if this Titan no longer exists in the world.
只有预言选中的人，才能从这万古吹拂的西风中，听到一声声呜咽。
Only those chosen by the prophecy can hear whimpers of sound from the ancient west wind that has blown for millennia.
是谁于鸿蒙初开之时，颁布约束万物的法律，令秩序通行寰宇。
Who was the one that enacted the laws that bound all things at the inception of primordial chaos, and set order upon existence?
在那连泰坦都无法呼吸的压抑当中，时空交织如麻，襁褓中的翁法罗斯危在旦夕。
In that repression that could even choke Titans, space and time intertwined like fibers, with the newborn Amphoreus on the cusp of peril.
冷静沉着的塔兰顿，化身横贯混沌的天平，它呼唤姐妹分别怀抱时空立于天平两端，并宣告了世间第一道法律——空间与时间永远分离。

Talanton, the calm and steady one, manifested as a scale that traversed the chaos. They called their sisters to embrace both sides of the scale holding up time and space, and thus was the first law of the world proclaimed: that space and time be forever separate.
从此，空间如高山，岿然不动，让万物有依附之地；时间如长河，滚滚向前，令众生有变化之机。
From then on, space became a mountain — unmovable, allowing all things to reside within. Time became a river — tumbling forward, granting all things capable of change.
What principles are at work here?
创世的工作远远没有结束，塔兰顿一刻不停地开始制定新的法律。
The work of world-forging was far from complete, and Talanton works endlessly to formulate new laws.
重的下沉，轻的上浮；硬的稳定，软的脆弱；热的发光，冷的黯淡…就这样，无数规则被摆上天平两端，接受公正之秤苛刻的评判。

That which is heavy presses down, and what is light ascends. That which is hard offers firmness, while that which is soft remains brittle. The hot shines while the cold dims... This way, countless laws were placed on both sides of the scale, to face harsh judgment before the Scale of Justice.
也正是有了这些规则的支撑，才有了大地、海洋、天空的诞生。
It is with the support of these laws that the land, the sea, and the sky came to be.
即使是一捧微不足道的泥土，一朵转瞬即逝的浪花，一股若有若无的空气，都蕴含了塔兰顿精妙绝伦的平衡之道。
Even an insignificant grain of soil, a fleeting, tumbling wave, or an ephemeral gust of air bears Talanton's delicate balance.
The very model of what a Titan should be.
很难想象没有规则的世界。
It's hard to imagine a world without rules.
可是规则…就是用来打破的。
But rules... are made to be broken.
在所有的法律和规则中，有一条至关重要——有始即有终。
In the order and laws above all things, there is one essential maxim: All matters have a beginning as well as an end.
塔兰顿深知万物皆从命运中来，当回归命运中去。所以树木会凋零，河流会干枯，狂风会消散。就算是众神宠爱的万物灵长，也要在百年之后渡往冥国。

Talanton knows that all things are born of destiny, and are destined to return thusly as well. Hence, trees wither, rivers dry, and wailing gales will scatter. Even the primate of all intelligent beings, we humans loved by the gods, will depart for the netherworld after our death.
可不要去责怪塔兰顿的无情，去看看大地断裂的脊梁，那就是生命无休止繁衍的代价。
But do not blame Talanton for their coldness. Go see the broken spine of the land, for that is the price to pay for the endless propagation of life.
Then do Titans have an end?
Balance sets the cycle of life in motion.
愿塔兰顿的天平永远公正，否则一切光暗，轻重，快慢，乃至时空本身，都将回归混沌。
May Talanton's scale be forever righteous, lest light and dark, lightness and heaviness, quickness and slowness, and even time and space themselves return to chaos.
愿万物遵从自身的规则，勿要违背公正之秤的度量，否则迎接它的将是最严厉的审判。
May all things follow their ordained laws, and never defy the Scale of Justice, lest they face the severest judgment.
是谁于智慧觉醒之时，制定约束行为的道德，令伦理照耀人心。
Who, at the dawn of wisdom's awakening, decreed the morals that bind our deeds, letting ethics illuminate human hearts?
人子对塔兰顿的感情十分复杂，它既不像美丽的墨涅塔和智慧的瑟希斯那样人人欢喜，也不像狡诈的扎格列斯那样人人唾弃。
Humanity's opinion of Talanton is complicated. They aren't universally beloved like the beautiful Mnestia or wise Cerces, but they also aren't disdained like treacherous Zagreus.
因为公平只是一个中性词，它在大多数时候违背人的本能。
Because fairness is but a neutral term, and it goes against the instincts of a person far too often.
在人类狭隘的道德观念中，公平意味着付出有所得，行善有所赏，施恶有所惩。
In the narrow morality that rules humanity, fairness means to give and receive in turn, getting rewarded for doing good, being punished for doing wrong.
但在塔兰顿来说，衡量公平的唯一准则，只是将比较的两者放于托盘两侧，看看孰重孰轻而已。
But to Talanton, the only means of judging fairness is by weighing both sides of the scales and see which rises and which falls.
泰坦的思维非人能理解。
The mind of a Titan is beyond human comprehension.
I can do this job too.
自人子间的第一起冲突开始，无数年来不乏文明的城邦被蛮军摧毁，也有正直勇敢的英雄惨遭小人谋害，但这些都不足以成为撼动天平的砝码。

Since the first conflict among humankind, countless civilized city-states have fallen to barbarian hordes, and noble heroes met their end by schemers' hands, yet none of this could tip the scales of cosmic justice.
因为在塔兰顿看来，这只是人间的游戏。可一旦人子的行为跨越界线，它的审判便如雷霆般降临。
For in Talanton's eyes, these were but mortal games. Yet should humanity cross the line, their judgment would strike like thunder.
例如那弑君者的故事，近卫将军于无月之夜刺杀残暴的君王，却也在事成之后突发暴毙身亡。
Take the tale of the kingslayer, the guard captain who assassinated a tyrant king on a moonless night, only to drop dead himself when the deed was done.
因为他违背了殿前忠心护主的誓言，即便结出大义的花，也要尝下赎罪的果。
For he betrayed his oath of loyalty to protect his lord. Even should a flower of righteousness bloom for him, he must still taste the fruit of atonement.
I don't understand this kind of impartiality.
I understand this kind of impartiality.
如此不通人情的裁决，招来了反对的声浪。民众涌进塔兰顿的神殿，为弑君者奔走呐喊。
This merciless verdict sparked outrage. Crowds flooded Talanton's temples, shouting demands for the kingslayer's vindication.
塔兰顿会理会他们么？在这位铁面无私的泰坦看来，弑君者只是一颗扰动了天平的砝码，而反对者的声音，也不会对平衡造成丝毫波澜。
Would Talanton heed them? To this implacable Titan, the kingslayer was merely a weight that disturbed the scales — and the protesters' cries could never ripple the balance.
或许这些抗议声，本身也是维持天平配重的一枚砝码呢？
Perhaps these very protests were themselves but another counterweight on the scales of balance?
一切都在塔兰顿的计算中。
它就像一台精密的机器。
They are like an intricate machine.
人子们卑微的智慧，岂能理解塔兰顿的所思所想，要相信公正之秤的判断，绝不会向任何一方偏袒。
How could the lowly intellect of a human ever grasp what Talanton is thinking? One must believe in the judgment of the Scale of Justice, for it never leans toward any side.
愿人子铭记神罚，克制内心的欲望和反叛，自觉行于道德和律法的坦途上。
May humanity remember divine retribution, restrain their inner desires and defiance, and willingly walk the straight path of morality and law.
是谁于天平将倾时，催动制约泰坦的咒语，令平衡回归大地。
Who, as the scales began to tilt, invoked the spells that bound the Titans, restoring balance to the earth?
扎格列斯曾取悦过所有泰坦，唯独没有逗笑塔兰顿，因为它怕自己的笑声会让天平颤抖。
Zagreus once pleased every Titan — except Talanton, who feared even their laughter might tremble the scales.
顽劣的扎格列斯心生一计，它趁着黎明前最黑暗的时刻，悄悄偷走了天平，边把玩边向西飞去。而这么做的原因，只是因为它看不惯塔兰顿总板着脸。
The mischievous Zagreus hatched a plan. In the darkest hour before dawn, they stole the scales, toying with them as they flew west — all because they loathed Talanton's perpetual sternness.
那天，所有人抬头就能看到一颗硕大的飞星。飞星所经之处，巨树漂浮空中，河水倒灌农田，城内偷盗横行。
That day, all who looked up saw a colossal shooting star. Where it passed, giant trees floated midair, rivers flooded fields, and theft ran rampant through cities.
扎格列斯这次闯大祸了。
Zagreus's is in big trouble this time.
塔兰顿的愤怒令众神担忧，连法吉娜都收敛了笑声，大海平静得像是一面镜子。
Talanton's wrath alarmed even the gods — Phagousa themself stifled their laughter, and the sea lay still as polished glass.
众神决定联手找回天平。吉奥里亚伸出手臂竖起高山，阻挡了扎格列斯的去路，艾格勒睁开百目照亮大地，让它无所遁形。

The gods united to reclaim the scales. Georios raised mountains with outstretched arms to block Zagreus' path, while Aquila unveiled a hundred eyes to illuminate the earth, leaving Zagreus nowhere to hide.
最后连尼卡多利都拔出长矛，抵着扎格列斯的喉咙，逼它交出了天平。
Even Nikador finally drew their spear, pressing it to Zagreus' throat until the scales were surrendered.
但塔兰顿哪能轻饶这个卑劣小人，从此一道新的铁律诞生了——每个偷盗之徒，最终都会失去所有赃物。
Yet Talanton would never spare such a rogue — and so a new law was forged: Every thief would, in the end, lose all they had stolen.
从赢下所有到一无所有。
From winning everything to having nothing.
Even Nikador is on the move.
可怜又可恨的扎格列斯，只能无穷无尽地盗取，再无穷无尽地失去，如此循环不止。
O Zagreus, pitiable, yet detestable. They can only ceaselessly steal and constantly lose. Such is an endless cycle.
而它那些人间的信徒，也被这如同毒蛇一样的诅咒缠绕着，再也没有金盆洗手的可能。
And the ones that believe in them in mortal realms are also afflicted by this curse, coursing through them like a snake's poison. There is no more hope of absolution for their like.
每一个小偷都在咒骂着塔兰顿，咒骂那座天平。
Every thief is cursing Talanton, cursing that scale.
It's pretty rough being a thief.
感谢塔兰顿吧，它为诡计的泰坦锻造永远不可解除的枷锁，震慑了每个鸡鸣狗盗之徒。
Thank Talanton. They forged an unbreakable shackle for the Trickery Titan, and terrified every deceitful thief.
可它是否知道，这样做会为自己树敌无数，它的天平，也成为了每一个窃贼梦寐以求的战利品。
But did they know, from this, countless enemies would arise? That their scales would become every thief's coveted prize?
它是翁法罗斯最沉重之物，用庞大的身躯承载万物生长。
It is the most weighty thing in Amphoreus, carrying the growth of all things on its massive body.
在遥远过去的太古岁月中，无形无相的命运孤独地运转了无数年，但在那个空灵抽象的世界中，无法诞生任何具象的事物。
In the distant past of the primordial ages, for countless years, the formless and abstract fate moved alone, but in that ethereal and abstract world, no tangible things could be born.
于是，雅努斯于虚空中创造了一团本不该存在的物质，塔兰顿将其揉捏成具体的形状，欧洛尼斯为它赋予自我演变的能力。
Thus, Janus created a mass of material that should not have existed in the void, Talanton molded it into a concrete shape, and Oronyx gave it the ability to self-evolve.
吉奥里亚就这样诞生了。命运三姐妹将它作为最初的砝码，置于天平一侧，翁法罗斯终于有了稳定的物质。
And so, Georios was born. The Three Fates placed it as the initial counterweight on one side of the balance, and finally, Amphoreus had stable matter.
So that's how the land came to be.
This is the right use for a heavy weight.
最初的吉奥里亚不像今天这般稳重，它和所有新生的孩子一样，对这个世界充满好奇，而每一次向外的探索都会导致剧烈的地震。

Originally, Georios wasn't as steady as they are now. Like all newborns, they were filled with curiosity about this world, and every time they explored beyond, tremendous earthquakes would plague the land.
命运三姐妹需要的是一片稳定的大地，于是塔兰顿耐心地教导吉奥里亚，使它把自己的冲动深埋于厚重的地壳之下。
What the Three Fates sisters needed was a stable land, and so Talanton patiently instructed Georios to bury their impulses deep beneath the thickness of the crust.
吉奥里亚谨遵塔兰顿的教诲，它慢慢变得沉稳内敛，在年复一年的沉默中生长出厚厚的泥土与高耸的群山。
Georios followed Talanton's teachings diligently, slowly becoming steady and reserved. In the silence of the passing years, they grew thick layers of soil and towering mountains.
而今从高空俯瞰，只有那些深不可测的裂谷，还记录着吉奥里亚年幼时的顽皮。
Now, from a high vantage point, only the deep, unfathomable ravines remain, recording the mischievousness of Georios in their youth.
Heh, they were pretty naughty as a kid.
Talanton's teachings are true and righteous.
百无聊赖的吉奥里亚选择沉睡，它呼出气体中冷的那部分，在上升的过程中凝聚成露水。露水降落回大地，最终在凹陷的地区汇聚成大海，这便是法吉娜。

Bored and restless, Georios chose to fall into a deep sleep. The cold part of the air they exhaled condensed into dew as it rose. The dew fell back to the earth, eventually gathering in the sunken areas to form the great sea, which became Phagousa.
热的部分一直上升到空间的顶端，由于遭到永夜之帷的阻挡，气体凝聚成一只翅膀足以遮蔽住大地的巨鸟——艾格勒也诞生了。
The hot part of it rose to the top of the sky, and due to the obstruction of Veil of Evernight, the gas condensed into a giant bird with wings large enough to cover the earth — Aquila was also born.
但是吉奥里亚并不喜欢它的血亲，它总抱怨海浪侵蚀土地，飓风摧毁森林。但宽厚的吉奥里亚并不会诉诸武力，而是默默生长出新的土地和森林。

But Georios did not like their blood relatives. They constantly complained about the waves eroding the land and the hurricanes destroying the forests. However, the kind-hearted Georios did not resort to violence, but instead quietly grew new lands and forests.
虽然是神话，但还挺浪漫。
It might be a myth, but that's pretty romantic.
为什么人类的活动范围基本都在陆地，偶有横渡大洋者，也难免葬身海底，更别提那不可逾越的高天。

Why is it that the range of human activity is mostly confined to the land? Even those who occasionally cross the oceans often meet their end at the bottom of the sea, not to mention the unreachable heights of the sky.
因为天空傲慢，大海欢愉，唯有大地那宽厚的性格，才能包容人类的弱小和愚蠢。
It is because the sky is arrogant, the ocean is frolicsome, but only the earth is generous in nature, willing to accommodate the weakness and stupidity of man.
它是翁法罗斯最顽固之物，用永恒的耐心塑造大地万象。
They are Amphoreus's most obstinate creation, wielding endless patience to intricately sculpt the world in all its facets.
吉奥里亚是最初的工匠，各种崎岖的怪石，山之民，大地兽，都是它的作品。但它一定不是最优秀的工匠，因为所有作品都与精巧毫无关系。

Georios is the original craftsman. Every manner of strange rock, Mountain Dweller, and dromas are works of their creation. Yet, they cannot be considered the best crafter, for their works lack any semblance of finesse.
众神都拿它的造物打趣，连扎格列斯都懒地偷它的作品。
All the gods mocked Georios's creations for amusement, and even Zagreus was too slothful to steal from them.
墨涅塔曾好心指点他，愿意教它精致的线条和缤纷的颜色，吉奥里亚却摆摆大手，声称自己没有兴趣。
Mnestia kindly offered to teach them the delicate lines and vibrant colors, but Georios waved it off with their big hand, claiming they had no interest.
法吉娜最喜欢捉弄老实的吉奥里亚，有时捏好的泥塑还没硬化，一场暴雨便毫无预兆的砸下，让吉奥里亚的心血变成一摊烂泥。

Phagousa loved to tease the earnest Georios. Sometimes, before the clay sculptures were fully hardened, a sudden downpour would smash down without warning, turning Georios's hard work into a puddle of mud.
吉奥里亚这次要生气了。
Georios is going to blow a gasket this time.
法吉娜这么做有点过分。
Phagousa did go a little too far here.
吉奥里亚罕见地发了脾气，它把这坨烂泥堆积到河口以示不满，却意外地给人们带来肥沃的河口土地。
It was rare for Georios to lose their temper. They piled the lump of mud at the river mouth in protest, but unexpectedly, it brought fertile land to the people.
这场小小争端还带来一个后果，那便是陆上的动物们一旦遇到暴雨，便纷纷放弃觅食和休憩，四处寻找避雨处。
This small dispute had another consequence: Whenever heavy rain came, the land animals would give up hunting and resting, scattering in all directions in search of shelter from the rain.
可能直到现在，它们还在害怕被法吉娜变成烂泥。
Perhaps, even till the very present, they remain fearful of being turned to mud and mire by Phagousa.
May we sleep soundly on every rain-sodden night.
艾格勒见大地上生机勃勃，自己的天上王国却冷冷落落，于是便想学着吉奥里亚的样子打造自己的作品。
Aquila saw the land filled with vitality, and noticed their own kingdom of sky was empty and cold, and thus decided to learn from Georios and forge creations of their own.
可是天上没有泥土，艾格勒也没有耐心，它只能随便揉捏一团团气，造出了一朵朵不规则的云。
But the sky was devoid of mud, and Aquila was hollow of patience, so they could only knead balls of air to create irregular clouds.
艾格勒很快对这些作品失去了兴趣，任由它们在天上飘来飘去。
Aquila quickly lost interest in these creations and allowed them to float as they will in the sky.
倒是吉奥里亚对这些云朵很好奇，时不时与它们握手，于是我们看到高山总是与云彩相依。
But Georios found themself interested in these clouds, and sometimes shakes hands with them. Thus, we see a mingling of mountains and clouds.
Aquila didn't understand the essence of things.
吉奥里亚其实懂得欣赏美。
Georios actually does know how to appreciate beauty.
我曾游历八方，见过大地万般景象，那广阔的平原是吉奥里亚的胸膛，巍峨的群山则是它的臂膀。
I wandered the world and saw countless sights. The vast plains were Georios's chest, and the majestic mountains were their arms.
祝我们的磐岩之脊永远安详沉睡，祝我们脚下的土地永远稳固坚定。
May our Pillar of Stone know eternal slumber, and may the soil beneath our feet remain ever firm.
它是翁法罗斯最温柔之物，用无价的神启挽救堕落人子。
They were the most gentle being of Amphoreus, using priceless revelations to save fallen humanity.
自黄金世结束后，众多城邦陷入黑暗，人们再也享受不到无穷无尽的食物，只能倒退回渔猎生活。
After the Era Chrysea ended, many city-states fell into darkness. People could no longer enjoy endless supplies of food and were forced to revert to a hunter-gatherer lifestyle.
然而过量的人口已经占满了大地的各个角落，竭泽而渔的索取方式也令生态不堪重负，无数人因饥饿而相互争斗，无数骇人听闻的惨剧每天都在发生。

However, the excessive population had already filled every corner of the land. The exploitative way of taking from nature had overwhelmed the ecosystem, and countless people fought each other out of hunger. Terrifying tragedies occurred every day.
瑟希斯被人们的贪婪激怒，它命令所有的植物枯萎，就当人们认为泰坦已经抛弃他们的时候，吉奥里亚又一次展现了自己的悲悯。
Enraged by humanity's greed, Cerces commanded all plants to wilt. Just as mortals believed the Titans had forsaken them, Georios once again revealed their boundless compassion.
人们要为自己的行为付出代价。
People have to pay for their actions.
Georios, seriously... I'm literally in tears.
吉奥里亚向瑟希斯求情，它与瑟希斯达成约定，只要人类能克服自己的贪婪，春天就会重新回到大地。
Georios pleaded with Cerces and struck a covenant: Spring would return to the land if humans overcame their avarice.
吉奥里亚找来自己的眷属，一只鼹鼠，它从众人口袋里偷走最后的一颗苹果，饥饿的人们追逐这只鼹鼠，一直到一片谷地。
So Georios sought their own Kin, a mole. It stole the last apple from the people's sack, and the hungry masses chased this mole to a valley.
他们看见一片金色的麦田，鼹鼠钻进土壤，令地面变得松软且适宜耕种。
They saw a golden wheat field where the moles would burrow into the soil, making the ground soft and suitable for farming.
这是两位泰坦给人类的考验。如果他们收下种子，以耕种获取粮食，瑟希斯就不再追究。
This was a trial set by the two Titans for humanity. If they collected the seeds and grew their own food through farming, Cerces would forgive their past transgressions.
反之，如果他们将麦田里的食物全部吃掉，即使身为刻法勒的造物，人类也将就此灭绝。
On the contrary, if they devoured all the food in the wheat field, humanity would face extinction, even though they were Kephale's creation.
What a harsh and brutal test.
人类还在，说明结果是好的。
Well, humanity's still here, so the outcome can't be that bad.
吉奥里亚的祭司模仿鼹鼠的动作，把土壤挖开，将一抔麦种埋入土中。
Georios's priests mimicked the moles, tilling the soil to bury the first handful of wheat seeds.
在瑟希斯的赐福下，密密麻麻的麦穗从土里长出，春天也回到翁法罗斯。
Under Cerces's blessing, dense ears of wheat sprouted forth from the soil, and spring returned to Amphoreus.
人们学会了从劳动中获得粮食，他们在第一抔小麦种下的地方建起吉奥里亚的神殿，将收获月的第一捆麦子送到这里，献祭给这位伟大的泰坦。

Humans learned to reap sustenance through toil. Where the first seeds were sown, they raised Georios's sanctuary, offering the inaugural wheat harvested during the Month of Reaping to the merciful Titan.
如今的吉奥里亚已经陷入永恒的沉睡中，它无法再看到曾经拯救过的土地和人类。
Now, Georios has sunken into eternal slumber. They can no longer see the land and people they once saved.
但是那一片片金色的麦田，一捆捆饱满的麦穗，一张张满足的笑脸，就是对这位泰坦最好的告慰。
But those patches of golden wheat fields, and those tight bundles of harvest, and smiling, satisfied faces are the best means of comfort for this Titan.
无人不颂扬瑟希斯的睿智。它开枝散叶，将理性的藤蔓扎根于吉奥里亚的身躯。
Countless people praised Cerces's wisdom. They spread their branches and scattered their leaves, letting the roots of reason settle within Georios's body.
飞鸟暂撇开艾格勒精心镌刻的云彩，俯身衔走蕴含智慧露珠的枝条；走兽亦贪恋难得的闲适，扇动鼻翼畅快地吸取林间醒神的清气。

Birds briefly veer away from the clouds meticulously engraved by Aquila, swooping down to seize branches glistening with the dew of wisdom. Beasts, too, indulge in rare respite, flaring their nostrils to drink deeply of the forest's awakening breath.
生机勃勃的色彩和灵动遍布于天地之间，受它眷顾的人子们也繁衍生息，追本溯源地采集那些金色的硕果，奉于他们中最秀逸的智士。

Vibrant colors and vivid movements fill the space between heaven and earth. Those blessed by them prosper and multiply, gathering those golden fruits at their source and presenting them to the wisest scholars in their midst.
This is the fruit of wisdom.
So eating it will make you smarter?
然而，并不是所有泰坦都乐见于瑟希斯的春天降临于世。从冥河探来的灰黯之手，已经攀上它的根须。塞纳托斯振振有词——
However, not all Titans welcomed Cerces's spring descending upon the world. The Hand of Shadow from the River of Souls had already crawled up its roots. Thanatos declared with conviction —
「鲜花无法永远盛开，终将凋谢而化作尘泥；巨树不可矗立不倒，必会腐朽并沦为枯木。」
"Fresh flowers cannot bloom eternal, and eventually they will wilt and become dust and ash. Giant trees cannot stand eternal, and eventually they will decay and become withered woods."
「唯有如此，世界方得存续，而不被无限滋长的植物所掩埋。」
"This is the only way. This is how the world survives without being consumed by the endless growth of vegetation."
瑟希斯当然没有愤怒，理智的花束于它永不凋落——
Cerces naturally wasn't rageful. The flowers of reason would never wither...
「那么，所有的鲜花都将化为泥淖，所有的大树都变成枯枝。这地上所有的植物，都将敬畏于塞纳托斯赠予的名为腐朽的死亡。」
"Then all flowers shall rot into mire, all mighty trees wither to barren husks. Every plant upon this earth shall tremble before the death bestowed by Thanatos through decay."
There's life, and then there must be death.
There's a beginning, and there must be an ending.
「但是，但是……若将淤泥与枯枝尽皆倾覆于那冥河，终有一天河流将堵塞不堪，不再流动。如此一来，其余的生灵便无法迎来命定的终结。」

"However... if all the silt and withered branches are cast into the River of Souls, the rivers would eventually become clogged and cease to flow. Should this happen, other living beings would be unable to meet their fated end."
灰黯之手静静垂听，桀骜如它，唯有倾听理性之声时才会稍显温驯。于是它们立下约定，将所有花草树木的尸骸，仍旧归还于瑟希斯。
The Hand of Shadow paused, their arrogance tempered only by reason's whisper. Thus, a covenant was forged: all fallen flora would return to Cerces.
它将敞开怀抱，迎回逝去的孩子。为了表达它的哀悼，它将陷入苍茫的沉睡。
They opened their arms to reclaim their lost children and sank into a mournful slumber.
但死神并不知道，瑟希斯早已和大地秘语轻谈，让腐朽的生命化为滋养的元素，去盈润崭新的生命。
But the Reaper did not know Cerces had already murmured to the earth, transmuting decay into nourishment for new life.
Death is but the seed of new life.
An impressive demonstration of a feigned retreat.
于是在造物世，地上成为了瑟希斯的花园。
Thus, in Era Creatura, the land became Cerces's garden.
后来，许久以后，直到黄金世的到来。人子们在地上建起了新的城阙，这才略微滞缓了那繁茂的扩张。
Later, after a long time, with the arrival of Era Chrysea, mankind established a new citadel on the earth, and this stalled the expansion of that lushness slightly.
无人不唏嘘瑟希斯的冷漠。当墨涅塔为它献上热切的爱，它将其封藏进拒绝的琥珀。
All lamented Cerces's indifference. When Mnestia offered fervent love, they sealed that passion within the amber of rejection.
当爱意和浪漫再无法抑制，它们如泉水般喷涌而出，化为漫野的金色羽翼。墨涅塔的蝴蝶飞向翁法罗斯的各方，直至那众神亦未洞晓的边陲。
Yet love and romance, unrestrained, burst forth like springs, flooding the fields as golden wings. Mnestia's butterflies soared to Amphoreus's farthest reaches — even to realms beyond divine sight.
就像蜂群采集花蜜，爱的羽翼是为那圣树中蛰居的泰坦找寻珍稀的赠礼。
Like bees collecting nectar, the wings of love sought rare gifts for the Titan dwelling within the Sacred Tree.
有斯廷法洛最为挺拔的橄榄树上，那清晨第一滴朝露滋润而成的碧绿果实；也有米拉瓦塔最勇敢无畏、胸脯宽阔的潜水者也采集不到的彩色巨蚌，如夜色般的珍珠在其中闪动。

There were emerald fruits nourished by the first dewdrops of dawn from the tallest olive tree in Stymphalia. Also colorful giant clams that even the bravest, most broad-chested divers of Mileutus could not harvest, with pearls dark as night shimmering within.
甚至从那似乎莽荒未化的沃辛尼什，也撷来了几则令人捧腹的笑谈。如此，不胜枚举。
Even from the seemingly primeval Worthinnis, they gathered several amusing tales. And countless others beyond these.
碧绿的橄榄，似常青的爱意。
Dark green olives, symbolizing evergreen love.
黝黑的珍珠，如深沉的陪伴。
A black pearl, as if a profound companion.
A good joke never gets old.
但瑟希斯并未回应如此的赤忱。毕竟，理性也无法完全将爱拆解成道理和哲思。
But Cerces did not respond to such devotion. After all, even reason cannot fully deconstruct love into logic and philosophical thought.
它如同不为风蚀日晒所动的顽石，闭塞了耳目。它藏身于圣树中，隐没了一切生机勃勃的迹象。连花草亦为之含羞，只被轻微抚动，就会卷缩了枝叶。

Like a stubborn stone unmoved by wind erosion or scorching sun, they sealed off their senses. They hid within the sacred tree, concealing all signs of vibrant life. Even flowers grew shy in their presence, curling their leaves at the slightest touch.
接着世界迎来了寒冬，大地上不见了盎然的绿意，一切都变得苍白而倦怠。艾格勒为了唤醒沉睡的瑟希斯，降下风雪和冰雹，但却未曾得到哪怕些微的回应。

Then winter came to the world, and the earth lost its vibrant greenery as everything turned pale and weary. Aquila, attempting to awaken the slumbering Cerces, sent down snow and hail, but received not even the faintest response.
Unfruitful love is as cold as winter.
最后，还是墨涅塔一片痴心，找上前来。羽翼们停留在圣树之上，用扇动出的鸣叫，对树中的挚爱轻声呢喃。
In the end, it was still the devoted Mnestia who approached. Wings alighted upon the Sacred Tree, whispering to their beloved within through the sounds of their fluttering.
然而，圣树却渗出金黄的汁液，将羽翼们包裹其中。墨涅塔的蝴蝶飘落在地，被落叶和泥沙遮蔽，层层叠叠，至坠地心。

However, the Sacred Tree exuded golden sap, encasing the wings within it. Mnestia's butterflies fell to the ground, covered by fallen leaves and sand, layer upon layer, until they sank to the core of the earth.
这便是谢绝爱意的琥珀，将一切都埋入遗忘的地底。
This was the amber of rejected love, burying everything in the forgotten depths of the earth.
The wings of love are ever so easy to break.
换个角度，它们也算是凝结在了一起。
From another perspective, they're technically all condensed together.
听闻如此悲伤的过往，后来的人子们在揣度爱意之时，便会拾起一朵落红。
Hearing of such a sorrowful past, the later children of humanity would pick up a fallen red blossom when contemplating love.
他们忐忑不安地祈求瑟希斯的垂怜，一边拈去一片花瓣，深恐那拒绝的琥珀会将他们封入无望的深渊。
Anxiously, they would pray for Cerces's mercy, plucking petals one by one, deeply fearing that the amber of rejection would seal them in an abyss of hopelessness.
无人不感喟瑟希斯的赐福。它为人子留下了两件至伟的礼物，其一是智慧与理性，其二便是耕种之术。
All marveled at Cerces's blessing. It left two magnificent gifts for humanity: one was wisdom and reason, the other the art of cultivation.
当死亡出现在世界上后，饥饿便也开始侵扰地上的生灵。为数众多的人子，不得不舍弃身为万物灵长的尊严，像野兽一样互相争斗，抢夺食物。

After death appeared in the world, hunger also began to afflict the beings of the earth. The numerous children of humanity, forced to abandon the dignity of being the most exalted of creatures, fought each other like beasts for food.
人子为了充饥，将瑟希斯赐予的理性抛诸脑后；人子为了果腹，掠尽了林中的野菜和果实。
To satisfy their hunger, humanity cast aside the reason bestowed by Cerces. To fill their stomachs, they depleted all the wild vegetables and fruits of the forest.
这便触怒了瑟希斯，它命令所有的植物枯萎，忍饥挨饿的人子或许很快就会从大地上消逝。
This angered Cerces, who commanded all plants to wither. The starving children of humanity would soon vanish from the earth.
人忘却了敬畏，罪有应得。
Humanity forgot reverence and deserved punishment.
未曾忍饥之人，无权指责。
Those who have never known hunger have no right to blame.
悲悯的吉奥里亚为人子求情，瑟希斯允许麦子从土地中长出。
The compassionate Georios pleaded for humanity, and Cerces allowed wheat to grow from the soil.
然而人子为了存活于世，光是麦子也并不足够。他们仍需要橄榄、土豆和洋葱，还有那曾漫山遍野为他们生长的甜美果实。

Yet for humanity to survive in the world, wheat alone was insufficient. They still needed olives, potatoes, and onions, as well as the sweet fruits that once grew abundantly across the hills and valleys.
于是瑟希斯降下了它的试炼。它从自己的祭祀中，选了一位最为虔诚的年轻人，并给了他一把形状奇异、颗粒饱满的种子。
So Cerces imposed their trial. From among their devotees, they selected the most devout young man and gave him a handful of strangely shaped, plump seeds.
它宣布将对年轻人施以诅咒——无论这位可怜的祭祀吃下什么，吃了多少，他永远也无法饱足，无尽的饥饿将如烈火将他烧灼！

They proclaimed that they would place a curse upon the young man — no matter what this poor devotee ate, or how much he consumed, he would never be satisfied, and endless hunger would burn within him like raging fire!
折磨人这块，神是专业的。
The divine are experts at torturing people.
于是，漫长的酷刑开始了。年轻的祭祀是渔民之子，他吃光了父亲打捞而来的鱼虾。
And so the long torture began. The young devotee, a fisherman's son, devoured all the fish and shrimp his father had caught.
然后，他吃光了家中的麦子，还有邻居们的存粮。他为了寻找更多食物，不得不背井离乡。目所能及的飞禽走兽都被他吃了。
Then, he consumed all the wheat in his home, and the provisions of his neighbors. To find more food, he was forced to leave his homeland. He devoured all the birds and beasts within his sight.
可是正如瑟希斯所言，无论他吃下什么，吃了多少，永恒的饥饿仍折磨着他。
But as Cerces had foretold, no matter what he ate or how much he consumed, eternal hunger continued to torment him.
这样的苦旅不知持续了多久，最终，祭祀终于耗尽了力气，精神和肉体都已破败不堪。他坐在了一棵枯木前，感知到自己即将殒灭。

This painful journey continued for an unknown amount of time. Finally, the devotee exhausted his strength, his spirit and body utterly broken. He sat before a withered tree, sensing his imminent demise.
他用尽最后的力气，举起信仰的神明赐予的那把种子，并未放入自己的口中，而是将其扬洒在空气之中。
Using his last ounce of strength, he raised the seeds given by his god, and instead of putting them in his mouth, he scattered them into the air.
He wasted the last of the food.
种子在空中绽放出瑟希斯的祝福，年轻人身后的枯木，也变成了参天的圣树。
The seeds blossomed with Cerces's blessing in the air, and the withered tree behind the young man transformed into a towering Sacred Tree.
年轻的祭祀通过了瑟希斯的考验。于是各种作物开始在大地上生长，人们也聚集在圣树之下，垂听智慧之声的教诲。
The young devotee had passed Cerces's test. Thus, various crops began to grow upon the earth, and people gathered beneath the Sacred Tree to listen to the teachings of the voice of wisdom.
当欧洛尼斯的幻夜尚且独占着天幕，无垠的永夜中，一只翅膀足以遮蔽大地的巨鸟睁开了眼眸。
When the phantom night of Oronyx still dominated the sky, in the boundless Evernight, a giant bird whose wings could cover the land opened its eyes.
艾格勒感受着身边的一切，它睁开第一双眼睛，可昏暗的世界让它无法看清；它睁开第二双眼睛，但黑暗仍然笼罩着万物。

Aquila sensed everything around them. They opened their first pair of eyes, but the dim world prevented them from seeing clearly. Then they opened their second pair of eyes, but darkness still enveloped everything.
于是它睁开更多的眼睛，在头颅上，在翅膀上。艾格勒每睁开一双眼睛，世界都会变得清晰一些。最终，当睁开第一百双眼睛的时候，艾格勒终于看清了它所在的世界。

So they opened more eyes, on their head and wings. With each pair of eyes Aquila opened, the world became a little clearer. Finally, when they opened the hundredth pair of eyes, Aquila could at last see the world they inhabited.
当艾格勒看清这个世界，世界便也在它的眼中。它的眼中发出炽热而绝对的光亮，盖过了「永夜之帷」和嵌于其上的群星。
When Aquila saw the world clearly, the world was also within their eyes. From their eyes came a blazing and absolute light, outshining the Veil of Evernight and the stars embedded upon it.
黑夜消散了，大地迎接着世界诞生以来的第一缕阳光。
The night was over, and the earth welcomed the first ray of sunlight since the birth of the world.
白昼给了它光亮的眼睛。
艾格勒在翁法罗斯的边界建立起它的神国。它翅膀上短的羽毛，化作天上的霞光和火焰；那些长的羽毛，则被它制作成雷霆和闪电。
Aquila established their divine realm at the border of Amphoreus. The short feathers on their wings became the glow and flames in the sky. The long feathers were fashioned into thunder and lightning.
云朵是艾格勒最初的眷属。艾格勒从自己的眸子里取下白色的部分，制作出云彩。这些眷属永远飘浮在主人的身边，但它们崇尚自由而生性散漫，因而很容易沉湎于享乐。

Clouds were Aquila's first Titankins. They took the white from their own eyes to create clouds. These kins forever float around their master, but they cherish freedom and are naturally carefree, thus easily indulging in pleasure.
有时云朵们被法吉娜连哄带骗喝下了陈酿，因而打湿了艾格勒的羽毛，愤怒的艾格勒便抖擞身子，将淋湿的羽毛撒进云层，唤起了雷暴。

Sometimes, the clouds would be tricked by Phagousa into drinking vintage wine, thus wetting Aquila's feathers. The angry Aquila would then shake their body, casting the wet feathers into the clouds and summoning thunderstorms.
但也有诗人说，这是法吉娜偷偷怂恿云朵们挠动艾格勒的鼻子，才让它不停在天空中打起喷嚏。
But some poets claim this happened because Phagousa secretly encouraged the clouds to tickle Aquila's nose, causing them to constantly sneeze in the sky.
它的一举一动关乎气象。
Their every movement affects the weather.
法吉娜多少有点调皮了。
Phagousa is a wee bit naughty.
当人子初降于世，他们仰头看去，却险些被艾格勒锐利的眼眸夺去了视力。
When the children of humanity first descended to the world, they looked up, yet lost their sight to Aquila's piercing gaze.
而它的眼睛绽放出的神光，也太过于灼热，乃至大地被消融成岩浆，花草被炙烤而焦黑。
And the divine light emanating from their eyes was so scorching that the earth melted into magma, and plants were charred black from the heat.
艾格勒盘旋于天幕，听到身下传来芸芸众生的哀求，不免心生恻隐。于是它便缓缓闭上一对眼眸。
Aquila circled across the sky, hearing the countless pleas rising from below. Moved by a tinge of compassion, they slowly closed one pair of eyes.
那哀嚎之声顷刻便弱上几分，于是艾格勒再闭上一双眼睛。
At that moment, the wailing grew a little fainter. So Aquila closed another pair of eyes.
如是往复，直到艾格勒只睁开一双眼睛之时，众生的声音才转为了赞美和欢呼。
And so this continued until Aquila left only a pair of eyes open, the voices of the people turned to praises and cheers.
As they say, heed the needs of the people.
于是艾格勒收获了启示——要打造只有一双眼睛的新眷属。
And so, Aquila received their revelation — to create a new Titankin with only a single pair of eyes.
当加蒂尔的首领在梦中遇到那阳光般的伟岸身影，他并不知晓自己即将受赐跨越海洋的智慧。
When the leader of Kadir encountered that towering, sun-like figure in his dream, he did not yet realize that he was about to be granted the wisdom to cross the ocean.
很久以前，在法吉娜最肆无忌惮的时代，即使是最伟大的临海城邦，也得祈祷不会得到她随性的垂青。
Long ago, in Phagousa's most unrestrained era, even the greatest coastal city-states could only pray not to attract their fickle favor.
正如加蒂尔的首领带领他的民众，不舍昼夜地搬运石料，巩固堤坝，就为了不让家园在海洋之神的某次狂欢中毁于一旦。
Just as the leader of Kadir led his people to toil day and night, hauling stone and fortifying the dikes, all in the hope that their home would not be destroyed in one of the sea god's wild revels.
但人世无常，事难如意，他们的努力反而激得法吉娜玩心大起，随即掀起巨浪，将堤坝尽数推倒。
But the world is fickle, and fate seldom favors human effort. Instead, their toil only amused Phagousa, who, in their playful whim, raised towering waves and swept the dikes away.
第二次，这位首领在岸边挖出沟壑，希望能以此阻拦海浪的前进，但法吉娜的力量将深沟填满，再度冲上海滩。
The second time, the leader carved deep trenches along the shore, hoping to halt the advance of the waves. But Phagousa's power filled the ravines and surged onto the beach once more.
Humans are powerless against the gods.
But he is still a fighter.
两次的失败令这位首领分外悲愤，他向大海怒吼，控诉这场不公平的对决。
Twice defeated, the leader was consumed by sorrow and fury. He roared at the sea, condemning the injustice of this one-sided struggle.
听到这话的法吉娜也不恼怒，她化身一条巨鲸来到海边，告诉众人，七天之内，如果他们能横渡大海，到达海洋那头的小岛，自己就不再侵扰他们的休憩，还会把自己的宝物赠给他们。

Phagousa was not angered by his outburst. Taking the form of a great whale, they appeared by the shore and spoke to the people. If the people could cross the sea and reach a distant island within seven days, they would cease their disturbances and even bestow upon them Phagousa's treasures.
但如果他们失败了，这片海岸就要永远被大海淹没。说罢，巨鲸便消失在海里。
But if they failed, the coastline would be swallowed by the ocean forever. With that, the great whale vanished into the depths.
首领苦思五天五夜，也没能想到渡海之法。是夜，他在梦中看到一个身影，就如同天上的太阳，正是艾格勒。
The leader pondered for five days and nights but could find no way to cross the sea. That night, in his dreams, he saw a figure as radiant as the sun — Aquila.
艾格勒告诉他，用藤蔓将粗壮的树枝绑起来，就能渡过大海。
Aquila told him that by binding sturdy branches together with vines, they could cross the sea.
天刚亮，首领便叫上同伴，找来许多树枝捆成木筏。第七天一早，首领在人们的注视下开启了渡海之旅。

At dawn, the leader gathered his people and set to work, lashing branches into rafts. On the morning of the seventh day, under the watchful eyes of his people, the leader embarked on his journey across the sea.
最终，首领到达了小岛。但法吉娜知道他们借助了神的智慧，扬言将用海水灌满加蒂尔的土地。
At last, the leader reached the island. But Phagousa, realizing they had borrowed divine wisdom, declared that they would flood Kadir's land with seawater.
危急之时，仍是艾格勒现身解围——
In this moment of crisis, Aquila appeared once more to intervene —
「如要灌满他们的土地，不要用海水，用你引以为傲的蜜酿吧！」
"If you must flood their land, do not use seawater. Use the honeyed brew that is your pride and honor instead!"
没想到艾格勒还是公关大师。
Sounds like Aquila is also a PR expert.
法吉娜受了恭维，又听这话颇为巧妙，顿时心满意足。
Phagousa, pleased by the flattery and amused by the clever words, was instantly satisfied.
于是，海边的战士们学会了法吉娜的酿造知识，也会在扬帆起航之日，向天空献上敬意。
From that day on, the warriors of the coast mastered the art of fermenting drinks and, before setting sail, would offer their respects to the sky.
当艾格勒向欧洛尼斯夸耀完美的眷属，引来的却是不屑一顾的轻蔑。
But when Aquila boasted to Oronyx of their perfect Kin, they were met with nothing but a dismissive scoff.
「世上哪有完美的事物？你引以为傲的眷属也不过尔尔！」
"Is there such a thing as perfection in this world? Your so-called flawless Kin is nothing special."
骄傲的天空之主，何尝能忍受这般质疑？
How could the proud ruler of the heavens tolerate such doubt?
被好胜心蒙蔽的艾格勒急于证明自己，它用鸟喙啄下自己的一双眼睛，和它的雷枪一起投进吉奥里亚的脊髓。
Blinded by pride and the desire to prove themself, Aquila tore out one of their own pairs of eyes with their beak and cast those — along with their lightning spear — into Georios's spine.
Not even divinity is safe from pride.
土壤里很快长出一个婴孩，那婴孩拥有思考的头脑，与支撑世界的四肢。每呼吸一次，它的身体就变得更大一些，它的智慧也变得更多一些。
From the soil, a child swiftly emerged, with a mind that could think and limbs strong enough to bear the weight of the world. With each breath, their body grew larger, and their wisdom deepened.
直到最后，婴孩成为一个比艾格勒还要庞大的巨人。它生出双手以触摸天空，生出双腿以脚踏大地。它的头发被云朵染上雪白，它的眼睛被天空染上苍蓝。

Until, at last, the infant became a giant, greater than even Aquila. They sprouted hands to touch the sky and legs to stride across the earth. Their hair turned snow-white, dyed by the clouds, and their eyes became deep blue, painted by the skies.
全知全能的刻法勒就此降生。
Thus, the omniscient and omnipotent Kephale was born.
A god begets a new god.
然而，不待艾格勒去向欧洛尼斯夸耀自己的造物，愤怒的塔兰顿却拦住了它的去路。
However, before Aquila could boast to Oronyx of their creation, their path was blocked by an enraged Talanton.
艾格勒被勒令打造出一座「天平」，交付新生的刻法勒保管，由它划定「黎明」和「黄昏」，平衡白昼与黑夜。
Aquila was ordered to forge a great scale and entrust it to the newborn Kephale. It would be the scale's duty to divide dawn and dusk, ensuring the balance between day and night.
当黄昏降临时，天空泰坦就必须阖上自己的眼睛，进入短暂的睡眠，直到黎明将他唤醒。
When dusk fell, the Sky Titan would be compelled to close their eyes and enter a momentary slumber — until dawn called them back to wakefulness once more.
但塔兰顿并不知晓，只因刻法勒是从艾格勒的双眸中诞生，它的双眼也更适应白昼。
Yet Talanton did not realize that, having been born from Aquila's eyes, Kephale's gaze was naturally attuned to the light of day.
直至后世，人子诞生，他们亦将延续刻法勒的喜恶，于白昼劳作，于夜晚歇息。
And so, in the ages to come, when humankind was born, they too would inherit Kephale's nature — working beneath the sun and resting beneath the stars.
Nighttime allows people to recuperate and rest.
至此，尽管关于昼夜的争夺永不止息，但当一年的时间结束，昼夜的时间仍会在最终保持完美的平衡。
Thus, though the struggle between day and night would never truly cease, by the year's end, their hours would always return to perfect balance.
全知全能的造物违背了世界的平衡，作为惩罚，艾格勒和怂恿他违背律法的欧洛尼斯，永远只能占有一半的天幕，不可逾越。

The omniscient, omnipotent creation had disrupted the balance of the world. As punishment, Aquila and Oronyx — who had encouraged this defiance of cosmic law — were each condemned to rule only half of the sky, never to cross into the other's domain.
古老的歌谣口口相传，讲述那遥远的过去，巨人刻法勒的行进。
Ancient songs passed down through generations tell of that distant past — the march of the giant Kephale.
且说巨人因为昼夜之争，降生于世。虽坦然接过重担，但举目望去，天地之间，无比寂寥，缺少了勃勃生气。
It is said that the giant was born from the conflict of day and night, and though they accepted their burden without complaint, when they gazed upon the world, they found it desolate.
巨人知晓一切，所以顷刻间便理解，世界缺少的元素乃「生命」。
The vast expanse of heaven and earth lacked the vibrant pulse of existence. Kephale, omniscient, understood in an instant — the world was missing life.
于是巨人迈开大步，誓要找寻生命的足迹。
And so, with great strides, the giant set forth, vowing to seek the footprints of life.
巨人为了寻遍整个世界，便沿着海岸线出发。它的大脚踏进被海水浸湿的泥沙，飞溅起阵阵泥浆。
To search every corner of the world, the giant followed the coastline. Their massive feet sank into the wet sand, sending mud flying in all directions.
泥浆落到海中，化为游鱼；溅到地上，变成走兽；甩到空中，被阳光晒干，就变成了飞鸟。
Mud that splashed into the sea became swimming fish. Mud that struck the land turned into roaming beasts. Mud flung into the sky, dried by the sun, transformed into soaring birds.
泥浆化成的生命，或拍打水花，或发出鸣叫，呼唤着自己的造物主。但巨人走得太快，没听到它们的响动，便将这些生命远远抛在身后。
The creatures born of the mud splashed in the waves, cried out from the earth, and called to their creator. But the giant walked too fast to hear them, leaving them far behind.
巨人不停地走，背上的天平也在不断交替着日夜，它一次也未曾停息。仿佛它一旦停下脚步，就追不上那理想中的生命。

Kephale never stopped. With each step, the scale on their back shifted, exchanging day for night and night for day. They never paused, as if the moment they did, they would lose sight of the perfect life they sought.
巨人走了非常久的时间。为了让它稍作休息，命运三姐妹悄悄用星夜的轻纱蒙住它的双眼，让他以为夜晚已经到来。

The giant walked for an unfathomable length of time. Wishing to grant them a moment's rest, the Three Fates sisters gently draped the veil of night over their eyes, tricking them into believing that night had truly fallen.
于是他放下天平，躺倒在吉奥里亚的背脊上，短暂地进入睡梦。他的一缕头发落到石头上，因他在睡梦中仍然在思考生命，这缕头发便长成一棵巨树。

Kephale then set down the scales, lay upon Georios's spine, and drifted into a brief slumber. As they slept, a single strand of their hair fell onto a stone. But because the giant continued to ponder the essence of life even in dreams, that strand of hair took root and grew into a mighty tree.
巨树飞快生长，从中诞生出智慧和理性。枝叶延伸之处，大地百花盛开，连那些泥浆中诞生的生命也沾染上些许灵气。

The tree sprouted with astonishing speed, and from it, wisdom and reason were born. Wherever its branches reached, the land blossomed with countless flowers, and even the creatures formed from mud were touched by its essence, gaining a spark of spirit.
Will one get smarter if they touch it?
当巨人刻法勒自睡梦中醒来，世界已然大不相同。生灵们围绕在圣树和巨人身边，感谢它赋予的生命。
When the giant Kephale awoke from their slumber, the world had changed beyond recognition. Living beings gathered around the Sacred Tree and their creator, offering thanks for the gift of life.
而巨人却摸不着头脑，浑然不知是自己的无心之举所致。这或许也是它唯一无从了解的真实。
But the giant was bewildered, unaware that this flourishing world had sprung from their own unintentional act. Perhaps this was the one truth they could never fully comprehend.
古老的歌谣口口相传，讲述那遥远的过去，巨人刻法勒的使命。
Ancient songs passed down through generations tell of that distant past — the mission of the giant Kephale.
诸神的造物令巨人分外欣喜，世界也因此充满生气。但它仍旧不曾满意——
The creations of the gods filled Kephale with joy, and the world thrived with newfound vitality. Yet, despite this, the giant remained unsatisfied —
「这些造物终究不具备我等的智慧，时间终将让他们化为砂砾。」
"These beings, for all their beauty, still lack our wisdom. In time, they will crumble into dust."
于是，巨人冥思苦想。终于在某一刻，顿悟了自身的使命——
And so, Kephale pondered deeply. Until, in a single moment of revelation, they understood their true purpose —
"I shall make them in my image, that they may bear the spirit of all things."
Humanity shall bear the spirit of all things.
They only realized it now? Does that count as a blind spot?
巨人找来天空之主，言语恭谦——
The giant sought out the ruler of the sky, speaking with humble words —
「您目光如炬，普照万方，希望造物受您的祝福，能在白昼行动自如。」
"Your gaze is piercing and shines upon all the world. I hope that my creations may receive your blessing, so they may walk freely beneath the light of day."
艾格勒微笑答应。而那星夜之神却面露不愉，刚要转身离去——
Aquila smiled and readily agreed. Yet the god of the starry night frowned, turning away in displeasure —
「请您不要生气。当星夜降临，造物需要您的庇护，才能安然休憩。」
"Please, do not be angered. When the stars rise, my creations will need your protection, so they may rest in peace."
于是欧洛尼斯回转心意，也应允赐福。
Hearing this, Oronyx reconsidered — and, at last, granted their blessing as well.
巨人又俯下身躯，唤醒大地。造物将以吉奥里亚的神体为躯壳。
The giant bent down once more and awakened the earth. Their creations would take form from Georios's divine body.
巨人又找来瑟希斯，请它摘下智慧的果实，赐予造物理性与思考的能力。诞生自圣树的神明自无不允，还带来了好奇心起的墨涅塔。

Then, the giant sought out Cerces, asking them to pluck the fruit of wisdom and grant their creations the gifts of reason and thought. The god born of the Sacred Tree had no reason to refuse, and even brought forth the curious Mnestia.
爱之女神飞到造物的泥塑前，用翅膀拂过他们的眼睛，轻点他们的胸膛，使他们的双眼得以观赏美的存在，使他们的心灵得以迸发爱的话语。

The goddess of love then flew to the mud figures, brushing their wings over the creations' eyes and gently touching their chests — so that their eyes might perceive beauty and their hearts might speak the words of love.
一切都已准备妥当，唯有巨人尚未给自己的造物献上祝福。于是巨人割开自己的手臂，黄金般的血液流淌而出。这血液赋予了造物真正的生命。
Everything was prepared. Yet the giant had not yet given their own blessing. So Kephale cut open their own arm, and golden blood flowed forth. This divine ichor bestowed true life upon the creations.
于是，最初的人子得以诞生。
Thus, the first children of humanity came into being.
被创造出的人子们聚集在巨人刻法勒的脚下，他们建立起一座城邦。
Humanity, newly created, gathered at the feet of the giant Kephale and built a great city.
而这座立在巨人脚下的城市，后来被人们如此称呼——圣城奥赫玛。
And this city, founded beneath the giant, would one day be known as the Holy City of Okhema.
古老的歌谣口口相传，讲述那遥远的过去，巨人刻法勒的死敌。
Ancient songs passed down through generations speak of that distant past — Kephale's nemesis.
生命在大地上繁衍，最后没入死亡。死亡滋生争斗，争斗滋生战争。当手无寸铁的人子被杀死，坠入冥河，越来越多的哀怨和憎恨在塞纳托斯的神国里增长。

Life spread across the land, but in the end, all living things met their death. From death arose conflict, and from conflict, war. As unarmed mortals fell, their souls plunged into the River of Souls, and within the realm of Thanatos, sorrow and hatred festered and grew.
死者的骸骨和冥河的水相结合，被怨声重塑为巨像，它在大地之下找到了艾格勒创造巨人时投下的那把雷枪，用冥河的水淬洗之后，制作成手中的天谴之矛。

The bones of the dead, fused with the waters of the River of Souls, were shaped by grief into a colossal figure. Beneath the earth, they unearthed the lightning spear that Aquila had once cast down when creating the giant. Tempered in the waters of the River of Souls, the weapon was reforged into the Lance of Fury.
此即为巨人死敌——天谴之矛尼卡多利的诞生。
Thus was born Nikador, the Lance of Fury — the eternal enemy of the giant.
Life will never yield to wars.
众多造物罹遭杀害，令巨人心生悲戚。
The slaughter of countless creations filled the giant with sorrow.
它找到天空之主，祈求它收回雷枪，令那神兵不再助纣为虐。但艾格勒不以为然——
Seeking aid, they turned to the Sky Titan, pleading for the return of the lightning spear, so that the divine weapon would no longer aid in destruction. But Aquila was unmoved —
「那枪早已沾染冥河的污秽，我不会再触碰那等浊物，更遑论将它收回。」
"That spear has been tainted by the filth of the River of Souls. I will not lay a hand upon such corruption, much less reclaim it."
巨人无可奈何，又呼唤智慧之神瑟希斯，与它商议。瑟希斯果然心生一计——
With no other recourse, the giant then called upon the god of wisdom, Cerces, to seek counsel. As expected, Cerces devised a plan —
「尼卡多利有一面塞纳托斯赠予的神盾，由冥土抟造，足以抵挡万钧雷霆。」
"Nikador wields a divine shield, a gift from Thanatos themself. Forged from the very essence of the underworld, it is strong enough to withstand even the fiercest lightning."
「只要你巧言说服它，让它用神矛去刺神盾，二者必有一伤。」
"If you can convince them to strike their own shield with the spear — one of them is bound to break."
于是巨人前往冥河，面见宿敌。它用商议好的言辞鼓动尼卡多利。
Thus, the giant journeyed to the River of Souls to confront its sworn enemy. They spoke with carefully chosen words, goading Nikador into action.
尼卡多利自负于武力和神兵，听了巨人的话早已满面不屑。它一手执矛，一手提盾，用尽全力，将二者撞在一起。
Proud of their unmatched strength and divine weapons, Nikador listened with scorn. Gripping their spear in one hand and shield in the other, they summoned all their might and clashed them together.
只听一声响彻天地的轰鸣，似有雷霆炸响，随即地面震颤，半晌才得平息。而当一切尘埃落定，尼卡多利手中的矛完好无损，而盾牌却已经化作了万千碎片。

A thunderous roar split the heavens. The earth trembled as if struck by divine wrath, quaking for what felt like an eternity before settling into silence. As the dust cleared, the spear remained unscathed, but the shield had shattered into countless fragments.
那些碎片，后来被扎格列斯铸成了金币。也有人说，它们成为了后世悬锋城盾的秘密。
Some say these fragments were later minted into coins by Zagreus. Others whisper that they became the secret behind the indomitable shields of Castrum Kremnos.
The Lance of Fury is really something.
如此，巨人总算是除去了死敌的一件利器，但也仅此而已。
Thus, the giant had finally stripped its foe of one deadly weapon — But that was all.
他们的争斗不会就此休止，仍将延绵于后日。
Their struggle would not end here. It would carry on, stretching into the days yet to come.
你睁开眼，发现自己正置身于金黄色的麦浪中。微风滑过你的脸庞，远方的村落炊烟袅袅，天空中不见太阳，却总是散发着明亮的柔光。

You open your eyes to find yourself amidst waves of golden wheat. A gentle breeze caresses your face as wisps of smoke rise from the chimneys of a distant village. Though the sun is nowhere to be seen in the sky, it radiates a soft, bright glow.
「嗨，没想到你也在这里呀。」熟悉的声音从背后传来。你转过身，发现了那位少女——她的衣角与袖口上沾着麦粒，看来之前正躺在地里睡觉。
"Hey, fancy seeing you here." A familiar voice comes from behind. You turn around to find that girl, wheat grains stuck to the hem and cuffs of her clothes, as if she's been napping in the fields.
「啊，事先说好，我可没偷懒哦，只是来这里采采风。你看，这是我用麦穗编的头环，送给你啦。」
"Ah, just to be clear, I wasn't slacking off. I was just getting some creative inspiration here. Look, I made you this wreath from wheat stalks. It's yours!"
Thank you, I'll take good care of it.
「不记得了吗？」她笑了起来，「没关系，我会一五一十地讲给你听，那段我们一起写下的故事。」
"Don't you remember?" she laughed, "That's okay, I'll tell you everything, every last detail of the story we wrote together."
「嘻嘻，不用谢啦，喜欢就好。我们一起经历了那么多故事，就当是小小的纪念礼物吧。」
"Hehe, no need to thank me! I'm just glad you like it. After all the adventures we've been through together, consider it a little memento."
「是梦，还是回忆呢？」她笑了起来，「也许是一回事哦？总之，好好享受这个故事吧。」
"Is it a dream, or a memory?" she laughed, "Maybe they're one and the same? Either way, just sit back and enjoy the story."
「怎么样，是不是很棒？我最喜欢它的尾声，带有一抹小小的奇幻色彩。」少女合上书本，英雄、史诗，都变作其中永恒的一页。
"How about that? Isn't it wonderful? I love the ending most. It comes with a little touch of fantasy." The girl closes the book, and the heroes and epics all become a page of eternity.
「那么，期待下次再会啦。相信我们的故事，也会迎来一个浪漫的结局♪」
"Well then, I look forward to seeing you again. I'm sure our story will have a romantic ending too ♪"
「说到故事…」少女变出一本似曾相识的厚书，书页被风翻动，发出沙沙的声音。
"Speaking of stories..." The girl conjures up a seemingly familiar, thick book, and its pages rustle in the wind.
「今天，你想听什么样的故事呢？」
"What kind of story would you like to hear today?"
「…就这样，故事结束了。王国边境的威胁得以平定，勇士的功绩将被传颂；而无人知晓，那传说中的『恶龙』，悄然成为了勇士身侧的少女……」

"...And so the story ends. The threat at the kingdom's borders was quelled, and the hero's deeds would be sung for ages to come. Yet none would know that the legendary 'evil dragon' had quietly become the maiden who stood by the hero's side..."
「…就这样，故事结束了。追求永生的国王再也不会死去，但他的青春却在时光中一点点凋零…这个故事告诉我们，许愿时一定要很小心，很小心呀。」
"...And so the story ends. The king who sought immortality would never die, but his youth slowly withered away with time... The moral of the story is: Be very, very careful what you wish for."
「…就这样，故事结束了。死去的英雄们从冥界归返，受损的村庄也得以修复。大家手拉着手，围在篝火前唱歌跳舞，直至暮色尽头……」

"...And so the story ends. The fallen heroes returned from the nether realm, and the ravaged villages were restored. Everyone joined hands, singing and dancing around the bonfire until twilight's end..."
行至某座城邦废墟附近时，艾格勒降下了一场大雨。你淋着雨兜兜转转，终于躲进附近的一片岩洞。
As you approach the ruins of a city-state, Aquila brings down heavy rain. You wander around in the downpour until finally finding shelter in a nearby cave.
看着水珠沿凸起的石刺滴落，你内心泛起一股伤感，直到有脚步声从洞穴深处传来——你并非独自一人。
As you watch droplets trickle down the protruding stone spikes, a wave of melancholy washes over you, until footsteps echo from deep within the cave. You're not alone.
「你想问我火漆的事吗？」那老者显露身形，眼神如炬。
"Do you want to ask me about the Sealing Wax?" The old man reveals himself, his eyes burning like torches.
「如果你想，可以随时回这片洞穴找我，我就住在这。」老者收紧肩上的布包，「或许很快，你就会意识到离群索居的好处。」

"If you want, you can come back to find me in these caves anytime. This is where I live." The old man tightens the cloth bag on his shoulder. "Perhaps soon, you'll understand the benefits of living in solitude."
你又一次抵达了那座城邦废墟。循着熟悉的小径，你回到了躲雨的岩洞。老者站在洞口迎接你，他看起来精神依旧，你的脸上却满是风霜。

Once again, you arrive at the ruins of the city-state. Following the familiar path, you return to the cave where you once took shelter from the rain. The old man stands at the entrance to welcome you. He still looks spirited, while your face bears the marks of wind and frost.
「看来你经历了许多。」他笑笑，皱纹仿佛斑驳的老树，「现在你应该明白，外面没有我们这些无邦者的容身之处。无论黑潮还是权贵，我都不想为伍。」

"Looks like you've been through a lot." He smiles, his wrinkles like the weathered bark of an old tree. "Now you must understand that there's no place for stateless folks like us out there. And I want nothing to do with either the black tide or the privileged elite."
「来，拿着这些火漆，和我一起在山洞里永远住下去吧。这里还有许多人……」老者解下背上的布袋。
"Here, take this Sealing Wax and come live with me in the cave forever. There are many others here..." The old man unties the sack from his back.
苦难与失败磨平了你的棱角，灾厄与纷争挫败了你的锐气。你接过老者手中的火漆，一缕微光从洞穴深处亮起，仿佛燃烧的火焰。

Hardship and failure have worn down your edges, while calamity and strife have crushed your spirit. As you take the Sealing Wax from the elder's hand, a faint glow emerges from deep within the cave, like a burning flame.
自那以后，你再不曾见过洞外的蓝天。
Since then, you've never seen the blue sky outside the cave again.
你虽经历过失败，却仍未放弃勇气与希望。老者露出一个豁牙的微笑，你背上饱满的行囊，重新向黑潮丛生的大地发起挑战。

Though you faced failure before, you haven't lost your courage and hope. The old man flashes a gap-toothed smile as you shoulder your well-packed bag, ready to challenge the land where black tides surge once more.
自那以后，你再不曾到访那片岩洞。
Since then, you've never returned to those caves.
你带着满满一包火漆，行走在无人的乡间小路上。突然，一颗巨大的紫色球状物体跳了出来，像颗葡萄……
You're walking down a deserted country road with a bag full of Sealing Wax. Suddenly, a huge purple spherical object, which looks just like a grape, jumps out...
「此山是我开，此路是我栽。不对……」紫色葡萄开口说道——看来它是个活物。
"This mountain's mine to claim, and this path's mine to... wait, that's not right..." says the purple grape. Apparently it is a living thing.
「我是翁法罗斯掌管火漆的神。」它双手叉腰，挺起胸膛，「把火漆给我，我来帮你合成一枚超~强大的火漆！」
"I am the Amphorean God of Sealing Wax." It puts its hands on its hips and puffs out its chest. "Give me your Sealing Wax, and I'll synthesize a super powerful one for you!"
「多好的宝贝啊，现在它是你的了。」紫色葡萄吐出一枚独特的火漆，随后便像风一样消失了。
"What a fine treasure! It's yours now." The purple grape spits out a unique Sealing Wax, then vanishes like the wind.
「多好的宝贝们啊，现在它是你的了。」紫色葡萄吐出成吨的奇物，随后便像风一样消失了。
"What fine treasures! They're yours now." The purple grape spits out tons of Curios, then vanishes like the wind.
「哎哟，老巴特鲁斯的腰可经不起折腾！」紫色葡萄吐出成吨的利衡币，随后便像风一样消失了。
"Ouch, old Bartholos' back can't take this anymore!" The purple grape spits out tons of Balance Coins, then vanishes like the wind.
生命花园里，身为训练家的你正在检查小奇美拉的工作。
In the Garden of Life, as a trainer, you are checking on the little chimera's work.
「今日的绩效又不达标，你能不能学学其他同事，追赶一下它们的工作进度？」你对小奇美拉质问道。
"Your performance is below standard again today. Can't you learn from your coworkers and try to keep up with their task progress?" You interrogate the little chimera.
「可是…领导，追赶进度是不可能的事情！」小奇美拉开始了它的论证，「因为若要追赶其他同事的进度，就先要追上现在的进度。可是追上了现在的进度后，其他同事的进度就又往前推进了！」

"But... boss, keeping up with the schedule is impossible!" The little chimera begins to argue for itself, "Because to catch up with other coworkers' progress, I first need to catch up with the current progress. But once I catch up with the current progress, the others will have moved even further ahead!"
「如此反复下去，我永远也不可能追得上其他同事的进度！」小奇美拉义正言辞地说道。
"At this rate, I'll never catch up with my coworkers!" The little chimera declared blatantly.
你被这有理有据的论证打动了，决定不再狠抓员工绩效。小奇美拉露出了幸福的笑容。
Moved by this well-reasoned argument, you decide to ease up on employee performance metrics. The little chimera beams gleefully.
小奇美拉说的不无道理，你很快就找到了解决问题的方法——
The little chimera has a point, and you quickly find a way to solve the problem:
只要给其他奇美拉放假，让进度落后的小奇美拉继续工作不就好了？在其他人放假时工作，也是一种加班。你管这个方法叫「加班相对论」。

Why not just give other chimeras time off and let the little chimera who's behind schedule continue working? Working while others are on holiday is also a form of overtime. You've dubbed this method the "Theory of Relative Overtime."
「叽里咕噜说什么呢？」你完全没听懂小奇美拉的论证，只知道今天的工作要是没做完，就不给饭吃！
"What's all this mumbo-jumbo?" You couldn't understand the little chimera's reasoning at all, but you know one thing for sure: No food to eat if today's work isn't finished!
「嗷呜~」小奇美拉垂头丧气地回到了工位上，开始加班。
"Awoo~" The little chimera trudges back to its workstation, ready for another round of overtime.
身为神悟树庭的优秀辩手，你很快便指出了小奇美拉的问题所在——
As a skilled debater from the Grove of Epiphany, you quickly point out the issue with the little chimera:
工作的进度的确可以被无限地分割，但这并不代表需要无限的时间。随着越来越短的进度差距，所需的时间也会收敛为一个有限的级数。
While work progress can indeed be infinitely divided, it doesn't mean it requires infinite time. As the progress gaps become shorter and shorter, the time needed converges into a finite level.
「嗷呜~」小奇美拉垂头丧气地回到了工位上，开始加班。
"Awoo~" The little chimera trudges back to its workstation, ready for another round of overtime.
听了小奇美拉的话，你陷入了沉思。对啊，工作的意义到底是什么呢？生命是有限的，工作却是无限的。奇美拉所做的，不过是重复将巨石推上山顶罢了……

After hearing the little chimera's words, you sink into deep contemplation. Yeah, what's the meaning of work, anyway? Life is finite, but work is endless. What the chimera does is nothing more than repeatedly pushing a boulder up the mountain...
你似乎领悟了某种道理，而小奇美拉早就趁你沉思的功夫摸鱼去了。
While you seem to have grasped some profound truth, the little chimera has long since slacked off during your philosophical moment.
身为一名废墟行者，你奔走在远离文明的断壁残垣之间，尤其偏爱那些深埋地下的古墓。有人说你是觊觎死者的财宝，你不在乎。

As a Ruin Strider, you roam among crumbling walls far from civilization, with a particular fondness for ancient tombs buried deep underground. Some say you're just after the dead's treasures, but you couldn't care less.
「喀嚓…咚！」机关触发，墓室的板门轰然倒塌。你举起火把，照亮面前黑暗的房间，墙壁上的一行铭文映入眼帘：
"Click... THUD!" A mechanism triggers, and the tomb's stone door comes crashing down. You raise your torch, illuminating the dark room ahead, where an inscription on the wall catches your eye:
「抵达此处之人，必有非凡的胆色。执掌命运的三相神会赐你奖励，它将是你的传奇之始。」
"Those who have reached this place possess extraordinary courage. The Gods of Three Fates who govern destiny shall bestow upon you a reward, which marks the beginning of your legend."
在房间的尽头，一座宝箱正在静静等候。里面装着你最为渴望之物……
At the far end of the room, a chest silently awaits. Inside lies what you desire most...
你掀开箱盖，拿上属于你的神赐珍宝。冥冥中似有目光从身后传来，又或是一阵温暖的风。
You lift the lid of the chest and take out the divine treasure that belongs to you. A gaze seems to linger from behind, or perhaps it's just a warm breeze.
自古墓中得到神赐珍宝后，你又经历了许许多多的冒险：从黑潮造物手里救下一对逃难的母子、帮助树庭运送濒危植物的种子，甚至短暂介入过悬锋城和奥赫玛的纷争。珍宝总能起到作用。

After obtaining the divine treasure from the ancient tomb, you've been through countless adventures: Rescuing a refugee mother and child from Black Tide Creatures, helping the Grove transport endangered plant seeds, and even briefly getting involved in the strife between Castrum Kremnos and Okhema. The treasure always proves useful.
某个夜晚，你坐在雪山脚下的湖岸边，把玩着那份神赐珍宝。月亮被浓云遮蔽，只漏出几丝不太明亮的光。
One night, you sit by the lake at the foot of the snowy mountain, toying with the divine treasure. The moon is hidden behind thick clouds, with only a few dim rays of light seeping through.
「汝已证明自己的资格。」一串温柔的低语从耳边传来，不知是命运三相神中的哪一位，「汝之美德会为珍宝增添力量。」
"Thou hast proven thyself worthy." A gentle whisper drifts by your ear, from one of the Gods of Three Fates, unknown which. "Thy virtue shall add strength to the treasure."
你向那声音点头应允。浓云褪去，柔和的月光倾泻而下，你的珍宝似乎发生了变化……
You nod in agreement to the voice. As the thick clouds disperse, soft moonlight pours down, and your treasure seems to have undergone a change...
你向那声音提出要求。浓云褪去，柔和的月光倾泻而下，一些东西出现在你的脚边……
You make a request to the voice. The thick clouds part, and gentle moonlight pours down, revealing something at your feet...
你谦逊地拒绝了这份力量。浓云褪去，柔和的月光倾泻而下。当你睁开眼时，神赐珍宝已然消失，有些东西留在了你的脚边。
You humbly decline this power. The thick clouds disperse as gentle moonlight pours down. When you open your eyes, the divine treasure has vanished, but something is left at your feet.
得到更强大的神赐珍宝后，你又建立了许多功业，与它的绑定也愈发紧密。诗人们编写歌谣，将你的事迹传颂。一股神圣的使命感从你心底升起：你要带它缔造真正的传奇。

After obtaining a more powerful divine treasure, you achieve many great deeds, and your bond with it grows even stronger. Poets compose ballads, spreading tales of your feats. A sacred sense of purpose rises from deep within: You are destined to forge true legends with it.
神话与传说编织成网，将你引向命定的终点。你攀上夜晚的山巅，最靠近星空与诸神的地方，跪下祈祷。
Myths and legends weave a web, guiding you to your fated destination. You climb to the mountain's peak at night, to the place closest to the stars and gods, and kneel in prayer.
「少有人能走到这个地步。」声音自群星传来，「你仍可升得更高，只是要付出些许代价……」
"Few have made it this far." The voice echoes from the stars, "You can still rise higher, but it will come at a certain cost..."
你用祭刀割开手掌，金色的血液沿指尖滴入湖面。相传，某位古贤曾以自己的单眼为代价，从欧洛尼斯处换取了预言未来的能力。英雄本身就是最好的祭品。

You cut your palm with the ritual blade, and golden blood drips from your fingertips into the lake. Legend has it that an ancient sage once traded their own eye to Oronyx in exchange for the power to foresee the future. A hero themselves is the greatest sacrifice.
你的伤口顷刻愈合——诸神已然接受这份代价。浓云褪去，柔和的月光倾泻而下，温热与力量一同涌入身躯。你感到前所未有地充沛，仿佛满盈的容器。

Your wounds heal in an instant. The pantheon has accepted this offering. The thick clouds part, letting gentle moonlight pour down as warmth and power surge through your body. You feel more invigorated than ever before, like a vessel filled to the brim.
阿卡索斯城曾流传着这样一个故事：一位木匠打造出了巧夺天工的雕塑，却始终活在它的阴影下，再无法拿出更好的作品。临终之际，他亲手毁掉了自己的得意之作，笑着咽了气。

There was a tale told in the city of Icatus: A carpenter crafted a sculpture so masterful it seemed divine, yet he lived forever in its shadow, never able to create anything better. On his deathbed, he destroyed his masterpiece with his own hands and died with a smile on his face.
神赐珍宝是不可多得的利器，但也可能是一份束缚、一个执念。你请求神明收回恩赐。浓云褪去，柔和的月光倾泻而下。当你睁开眼时，神赐珍宝已然消失，有些东西留在了你的脚边。

The divine treasure is a rare and powerful tool, but it can also become a shackle, an obsession. You beg the gods to take back their blessing. The thick clouds disperse as gentle moonlight pours down. When you open your eyes, the divine treasure has vanished, but something is left at your feet.
月光洒在你离去的路上。等到时机成熟时，你仍可升得更高。
Moonlight shines upon your path of departure. When the time is right, you can still rise higher.
你的声音愈发沙哑——那是诸神在委婉地表达拒绝。你笑了笑，识相地起身离去。你的时机未到。
Your voice grows increasingly hoarse. It's the gods' subtle refusal. You smile wryly and take the hint, getting up to leave. Your time hasn't come yet.
你的声音愈发洪亮——诸神已然接受这份代价。浓云褪去，柔和的月光倾泻而下，温热与力量一同涌入身躯。你感到前所未有地充沛，仿佛满盈的容器。

Your voice grows ever more resonant. The divine has accepted this offering. The thick clouds part, letting gentle moonlight pour down as warmth and power surge through your body. You feel more invigorated than ever before, like a vessel filled to the brim.
门关月是一年的起点。在这一月，人们会抛弃不再需要的身外之物，烧毁与带来羁绊之人相关的东西，宣告斩断过去、直面未来。

The Month of Gate marks the beginning of a year. During this month, people discard possessions they no longer need, burn items connected to those they were bonded with, declaring a break from the past and facing the future head-on.
去年的哀悼月，战争吞噬了你的挚友。士兵们用盾牌带回了他的身体，塞纳托斯已将他阖入手掌。人们推荐你参加今年的焚烧仪式——你应当开始新的生活。

In the previous Month of Mourning, war devoured your dearest friend. The soldiers brought back his body atop their shields, and Thanatos has taken him into their palm. People recommend you attend this year's Enkindle Ceremony. It's time for you to begin a new life.
「希望塞纳托斯善待我的母亲。」面容坚毅的男人将一枚陶碗投入火坑，走过来拍拍你的肩膀。轮到你了。
"May Thanatos be kind to my mother." The stern-faced man tosses a clay bowl into the fire pit, then walks over and pats your shoulder. It's your turn.
你掏出挚友赠与你的护身符，投入火坑，那是你们友谊的开始。思念随火光和烟尘徐徐上升，直到在空中消失不见。

You take out the amulet given to you by your dear friend and cast it into the fire pit. It was the memento of how your friendship began. Your longing rises slowly with the flames and smoke until it disappears into thin air.
你拿起涂抹油彩的陶板，投入火坑，那是你们许多个日夜的心血。思念随火光和烟尘徐徐上升，直到在空中消失不见。
